### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-09T03:44:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-155100>

**<Weight Loss Week 4 Update>**

Heh, this week was a FAIL

Too much work-related stress meant the return of my life-long manic-depressive symptoms this week. I would feel like shit, not feel like doing any physical activity at all, literally lying around sometimes for hours, while my mind gets super sharp and focused on logical next steps. You will also see some self-destructive dietary behaviour in the meal logs, which is basically indicative of disruptions to gut-brain dopamine signalling. After this phase however, I'd get hyper excited and actually do all the things that I need to do (hence the "manic" in manic-depressive).

I do lose electrons from my brain when I get stressed, but apparently it's mostly from the areas controlling mood, while the logic centres like the prefrontal cortex remain fully functional. To be honest, I'm glad that my brain fails that way, since I'm forced to think my way out of any bad situation when I'm stressed. Still feels bad, and I'm not fun to be around when that happens, but damn is it a useful reaction

There was an increase in perceived stress levels over the week, culminating on Friday, whereby I had gained 2kg and seen some dry skin start to pop out. So I basically cut down work time, read a couple of good stories, went out in nature (despite the heavy rain), increased my fat intake, ate a bunch of beef tendons for gut support, and recovered within 2 days. ("recovered" means that I'm putting out good mental work output, am not feeling depressed, and the 2kg I gained is now gone on Monday)

Ended up back where I started at 79.2kg today, which I guess is a good sign that I can cope with energetic losses much better now, and rebound pretty quick. Still no progress made this last week of course.

This week will just be controlling work stress, and continuing with the stated plan.

\----

Nicotine always makes me drop a lot of water (peeing a lot) and increases my thirst. Probably a good effect similar to that of CT. Moderate doses of nicotine are something that I'll continue to use on a periodic basis. "Moderate" means 2-4mg a day, and "periodic" means whenever I feel like I need a boost to fatty acid uncoupling (eg: a plane flight)

\----

In other news, it's still raining now, after 389mm of rain in the 48-hour period of 9am on Saturday to 9am on Monday. There was 247mm on Saturday alone, which resulted in a car knocking down one of the power lines outside my suburb and leading to an almost-12-hour power outage from 6:30pm to 5am the next morning . Any circadian disruptions were prevented that night

\----

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-16T00:51:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-155722>

**<Weight Loss Week 5 Update>**

Still a very stressful week work-wise, but much better managed health-wise.

-   Dropped a lot of carbs (no more than 100g on any day, usually <50g)
-   ate more
-   ate earlier in the day (main meal at 12pm, still summer time here with 12hr 40min of daylight and a 85.6deg noon sun altitude)
-   kept the 5pm insulin spike (using a grass-fed whey protein shake with about 40-50g of protein)
-   basically back to full Epi-Paleo with the exception of some rice on Wednesday

I should say that the calorie counts in the meal logs below are probably waaaaay off  . I count the calories retrospectively, meaning I eat what I feel like is appropriate first, and then count the totals at the end of the day.

The methodology behind "what I feel like eating" is basically first eating until I'm not bloated. So I'd just eat a bunch, and then realise that my stomach "didn't get bigger", and then eat until I start seeing a little bit of distention. Usually this is like 2000+kcal in a meal

There is some planning, but that's just involving protein and meat amounts (eg: I'd plan to eat 350g of beef because I need to take it out of the freezer the previous day to defrost)

You'll see some slip-shod markings like "misc fat", which TBH, I think I'm accounting for completely wrong. I'd like take a "2 tablespoons" of cream from my 300mL bottle of Gippsland double cream (51% milk fat, about 700kcal per 100mL), and then realise that 2 days later it's gone  . 250g stick of butter I bought 3 days ago is gone this morning ....

Calorie counts for the last 4 days in reality would probably exceed 3000kcal, and fat intake was probably >250g

\----

Bodyweight and waist measurements remained the same (79.2kg to 80kg), but the mirror says that I got leaner. The most noticeable difference I saw is that my jawline became a little bit more pronounced.

That's a reminder to people that weight isn't always a good measure of body composition, and that you can and should track as many useful measures of weight loss progress (if your goal is weight loss).

The amount of face fat is a great indicator of body fat percentage for me, and I see big variations with every kilogram difference.

Here are examples:

-   At 84kg -- [http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/20140524-face.png](http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/20140524-face.png)
-   At 80kg -- [http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/hair-straight-front-ponytail.jpeg](http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/hair-straight-front-ponytail.jpeg)
-   At 77kg -- [http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/P1010378.png](http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/P1010378.png)
-   At 75kg -- [http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/lean\_face.jpg](http://tanyewwei.com/static/pics/lean_face.jpg)

\-----

Mentally speaking, you could say that I'm a little more robust in the face of stress, so staying high fat, with the biggest meal at midday, is probably my go-to strategy for dealing with stressful times.

\----

5pm insulin spike using only protein definitely has significantly effects in some way. I generally feel a little hungrier after taking that Whey Shake, and definitely start dropping water after that.

Again, this re-affirms my theory that you don't need carbs to get the effects of a refeed, and using an isolated protein bolus is probably a good workable idea that is easy enough to do (drinking a whey shake is possible even for busy people).

My recommended eating pattern was discussed in the 'Reverse Electron Flow' thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/page-2#post-155437](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/page-2#post-155437)

\----
**Moving Forward**

I'll probably stay high-fat low-carb for the rest of the week.

Saturday morning I'm flying down to Brisbane to meet up with @[Jude](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/904/) and @[Nick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/3014/) for 2 days, then back in Melbourne for a week, so diet will be just kept high fat whenever possible.

\----
**Random**

2 foods this week made me bloat a little bit:

-   200g of Smoked Salmon eaten in a single meal
-   600g of pork belly (with skin, eaten yesterday)

The first is definitely due to the sodium in processed food. Similar experience to @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/ingers-journal.6472/page-63#post-155548](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/ingers-journal.6472/page-63#post-155548) , and I saw I little bit of water retention in the belly region that cleared after 1 day.

The second was probably due to the large amount of skin and collagen. In this case, I look the same in the mirror, but waist measurement went up a little, probably just indicative of extra food in the gut due to slower GI transit time, which is nothing to worry about.

\-----

I agree with Pusheen's stance on eating Broccoli . Haven't had much vegetables in the diet at all, and frankly, every time I try to add some in I don't see good effects.

Bah, I'm under 25, brain still growing, need fat, not veggies

\----

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-27T09:08:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-156789>

Catch-up with @[Nick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/3014/) over the weekend was awesome! Nick fed me with too much alcohol though  But at least he will testify that I'm a gentle drunk

Then I flew into Melbourne this Monday <23-Feb-2015>, and I can re-affirm that postcode 3058 sucks for my health

This place in Melbourne is definitely NOT treating me well . Had some duck and pork, some of which was covered in definitely non-Epi-Paleo sauce, and hell, {weight, waist} went from {79.1kg, 78.5cm} one day, to {81.3kg, 81cm} the next, and today (2 days later) it's still {81.2kg, 80cm}.

Felt shitty on the day after as well, not so bad today, but definitely not optimal. Skin dryness immediately started appearing on my feet, and I somehow feel like I have less tolerance for being out in the sun.

So I can't deal with gut stressors at this particular place  I've had waaaaaayyy worse in Cairns, and still didn't get screwed up like I did here. Environment matters folks! But I'll have to spend some extra beach time when I'm back in Cairns on Monday <02-Ma-2015> , and get rid of all this bloat

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-04T13:16:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-157408>

**Summary of period <21-Feb-2015> to <04-Mar-2015>
**
Just a quick high-level play-by-play of the events in the last week, the result, and the recovery protocol (travelling is always stressful )

Biggest changes over the period were skin quality, weight/waist, and overall gut health.

I left Cairns at around 79.0kg to 79.5kg, with a 79cm waist, with great gut health, and with great skin quality

\----

Flew out to Brisbane to to meet up with Nick and Sandra over the weekend of the <21-Feb-2015>. Had too much alcohol over the weekend. "Too much" meaning I woke up Sunday morning and literally fell out of bed.

Sidenote: When I get I drunk, it doesn't seem to affect my brain that badly. I lose all coordination to the rest of my body, so I'll be stumbling about and maybe butchering the pronunciation of a specific set of sounds. But my logical thinking abilities are all 100% intact, self awareness is 100% intact, and I get really happy

This leg of the trip didn't do me any damage it seems.

\----

Flew out to Melbourne on <23-Feb-2015>, and then went to stay at my parent's place in Coburg. Did fine for the first 2 days, and weight/waist was around 79.1kg/79cm

Then I had the nonsense above, and the bloating ensued . Weight has been around 80kg to 81kg, and waist has been 80cm to 81cm ever since (6 days later)

By "bloat", it just manifests itself as a more-than-usual distention of the abdomen (hence larger waist), and horrible bowel movement patterns (5 instances of diarrhea so far). But there's no pain, no gas, and no discomfort at all. There is also no puffiness under my skin.

I had mood issues the day after the bloat started, but that disappeared one day later. I also had sleep issues for one day, but that resolved after that day. This is all to be expected, since the gut mediates melatonin and dopamine production.

So this is a localised swelling of the tissues in the GI tract. No other peripheral tissues seem to be affected.

\----

To get myself back on track, I had a breakfast a la the Leptin Reset yesterday and today to re-yoke the GI and peripheral circadian clocks. All signs of Leptin Sensitivity seem to be coming back now (see the questions used to assess Leptin Status in the Leptin part 3 blog -- [http://jackkruse.com/why-is-oprah-still-obese-leptin-part-3/](http://jackkruse.com/why-is-oprah-still-obese-leptin-part-3/)).

Bowel movement regularity is also back to normal after fluctuating wildly over the last few days. Now it's back to a bristol 4 about one hour after waking (which is usually about 1.5hrs after sunrise).

The breakfasts were pretty big (I tend to overdo things ). Today was 400g of beef chuck, bunch of nuts, lots of coconut oil and butter, and 60g of Lindt 90% chocolate. I don't bother with calories, but if you must, that's around 130g of protein and maybe 2000kcal today upon waking.

I followed it up with a protein rich late-day meal as well. Example, today's dinner was a can of mackerel (400g), a can of sardines, mixed up with herbs and spices as usual. 80+g of protein in this meal (and 200+g for the day)

For exercise, just some stretching and walking around the beach these 2 days. Of course, I got into the ocean both days first thing in the morning

\----

Starting tomorrow, I'll incorporate Intermittent Fasting (IF) to help with the GI tract recovery, as well as the goal of weight loss. This time I won't force myself to eat , so I'd expect some natural calorie restriction in response to the IF protocol.

Should basically be following the same sort of eating plan as Danco3636 -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/page-2#post-155572](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/reverse-electron-flow.12744/page-2#post-155572)

The current light cycle here in Cairns is from 6:15am to 6:40pm (approx 12 hrs 20 mins daylight), noon at around 12:30pm with 79.7deg sun altitude.

So the important points are basically:

\- wake up around 6:30am, get onto the beach and into the ocean
\- some coffee and/or tea and mental work until around 10:30am
\- get out into the sun, more general activity
\- first and largest meal at around 12pm to 1pm (6+ hours post sunrise)
\- get onto the beach again at 3pm
\- small meal at 5pm (80 mins before sunset), usually this will be just high protein and nothing else
\- wind down for the day and do all my recreational work (posting on the forum is "recreational" )
\- bed by 9:30pm (3hrs post sunset, 4.5hrs after the last meal)

We'll see how that plays with my goal of experimenting with weight loss and how it affects my peripheral collagen quality.

....

Actually, screw it.

(a) I'm not really beholden to any particular daily time schedule since I work whenever I want to work.
(b) I'm not trying to perform any experiments right now

So eating schedule will be sporadic and completely random . Some days I'll skip breakfast, some days I'll skip dinner. As long as I always mind circadian cycles though, and only eat and exercise during daylight hours, I should be able to get on quite an intuitive eating plan.

\----

My personal stress-relief agent has been very good at telling me to "stop working and head to the beach!!!!" whenever I get too flustered with work.

Having that little cute out-of-place object on my work desk is a good reminder to always be able to have a good objective perspective of my current state of health on a minute-by-minute basis.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-11T11:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-158189>

Not much new yet. Main stressor right now for me now is stress from work, which I recover pretty quickly from once I take time off work .

This quote kinda describes me pretty well though:

It’s much harder, in my experience, to get someone to slow down than it is to get someone who is unmotivated and lazy to do more.

[https://twitter.com/DHKiefer/status/571403395396988928](https://twitter.com/DHKiefer/status/571403395396988928)
​

I will admit to working more than I'd like to be working (currently 4-5 hours a day), on a project that isn't what I want to do, but is profitable as hell anyway. As usual, "working too hard" is relative, and can't be measured by number of hours worked. I don't seem to have good leading indicators for this stuff either, and my "mental rating of perceived exertion" for something like writing code is not good at all

I always tend to get excited about coding to begin with, which slowly devolves into bebrudgingly dealing with menial tasks. I'd think that I'm just fine after a coding session, and then after that feel mentally exhausted and unmotivated to do "mentally challenging work" ..... so I read research papers and post on the forum instead

Random lesson from Sebastian Marshall: when faced with the urge to procrastinate, just aim to do "anything useful" (as opposed to doing something self destructive like consuming too much trashy media).

So happens that dealing with Quantum Health is something that is relatively lower in terms of mental strain for me, and I can do it even when not functioning optimally.​

It was definitely a case that when I discovered this stuff about EMF and blue light 2 years back, I just thought to myself, "Heh, so I've been building stuff to burn people's eyes out all along ". There is definitely lots of personal disillusionment with regard to my profession to this day, but ultimately, it's the best solution to my money problem, and does pave the way forward to allow me to do better things.

Money is going to be a major enabler, both to help with the pursuit of my own health, and to set up the social conditions to further the optimal health effort. I'll have to get a house (with land) anyway to be able to control my envirnoment, so why not just build 2 dome-homes (which are cheap and robust to weather events), and then use one of those as a way to show someone what Optimal Health feels like?

ie: Hey come over to Cairns for a week! You'll eat fresh seafood every day, be in the ocean a lot, have some fun in the sun, and sleep like a rockstar on a magnetico when the day is done . Free of charge, you just need to bring interesting conversations, and be able to put up with my craziness

Oh well, I've never cared for money, and if I could have good health without needing lots of money, I'd optimise my life different. But too bad, I was born into a toxic environment (in every sense), and it's is forcing me to solve the money problem quickly in a time-scalable fashion (so I'm not bound to the computer for long hours). Working as an employee or contractor in software is profitable as hell, but that puts you in a toxic environment (in every sense), and doesn't give you the ability to deliver constant value to people. A small SaaS business is the way to do for me, and that unfortunately brings with it the associated stressors (just ask @[Nick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/3014/) )

\-----

I'm still not 100% recovered from my 10 days of travelling . Bodyweight is not dropping yet, and I can tell exercise recovery is not 100%, but all other indicators of health seem to be back to where they should be. Peripheral collagen issues always bug me first.

Will post detailed diet and weight logs for the last 2 weeks after the weekend is over.

\-----

Second cyclone in 3 weeks forming over Queensland. This one (Nathan) won't likely breach the coast, and will likely go strong (category 3) for 1 more day (<Thu-12-Mar-2015>) and then die down. Hopefully it's just lots of good water for the land though , and no real damage done.

\-----

Heh, I just did some quick math, and apparently the top 10 largest cities in Australia represent about 72-74% of the population (17 million out of 23.7 million) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List\_of\_cities\_in\_Australia\_by\_population](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_cities_in_Australia_by_population)

Every one of those is below the tropic of capricorn and basically seeing significant seasonal variation light cycles, and all have altered water supply.

I'm stuck in quite a large region with about 166,000 people -- [https://www.google.com.au/maps/plac...m2!3m1!1s0x697862555ba22413:0x400eef17f207860](https://www.google.com.au/maps/place/Cairns+QLD/@-17.045755,145.6227,10z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x697862555ba22413:0x400eef17f207860)

Then I'm in Trinity Beach (about 5,000 people), 20km north of the CBD, and at a latitude of about 16.5deg South. I definitely like the more consistent light cycles and the heavy rain.

Darwin is the northern-most major city at about 12deg South, but it's faaaaaar away in the NT , which is why I didn't want to re-locate that if I could help it. Seems like the 16.5deg latitude is doing me fine though

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-16T23:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-158694>

**Period <21-Feb-2015> to <16-Mar-2015>
**
That dietary insult basically is an almost 3-week setback at this point in time . I basically gained 1.5kg after that incident, and haven't lost it since. If you count the time it will take me to lose the weight again, it's probably on the order of 5 weeks before I get back to where I was in mid February.

The lesson learnt is that I need to be very wary of certain dietary stressors while travelling. Wine didn't hurt me at all, neither did beef burgers, cheese, nuts, etc .... bloody Chinese food really screwed up my system (I can speak Chinese, but fuck eating Chinese food )

\----

My eating pattern since then has mostly included breakfast upon waking to restore leptin sensitivity. The quantity of food consumed was also not restricted at all.

One thing is that despite gaining the 1.5kg immediately, and eating ad libitum (which always ends up as a lot of food for me), I have not gained any additional weight since coming back to Cairns. At least a good sign that I'm not becoming more energy inefficient.

By now, any morning cravings are pretty much gone, so I'll probably revert to just eating some fat in the morning, and then having a main meal at lunch.

\----
Reminder about current Goals

As a quick reminder, the original goal here was to try and get back down to a lower bodyweight, and see how that affects the health of my peripheral collagen. When I last got to a 75kg bodyweight (-5.5kg from where I'm currently at), I experienced far less collagen-related symptoms.

Less back pain (6-year-old L3-L4 herniation still bugging me), joint mobility increased (hips especially), not as much edema in fingers and feet, endurance capacity very high (eg: easy fasted 100+km bike ride within 4hours).

At the end of the day, it's because of better ubiquination regulation, better autophagy at all scales, leading to better collagen quality all around.

I have not been able to get the same benefits simply by intermittent fasting or exercising at the appropriate times, and so I think there's a high likelihood of me being much healthier at a lower bodyweight.

To get to that lower bodyweight (and get good autophagy) I'll likely have to do forced caloric restriction of some form (aka: standard dieting). I've done this multiple times in the past, and know not to ever try dieting during periods of high stress, which has been the case since I started working on my current business.

Sidenote: as of last Friday, I've pretty much finished up the bulk of the software for my business, and have put the thing live and ready to accept customers ([https://plus23.com/](https://plus23.com/)). Looking at the 2 main code bases, 33,000 lines and 28,000 lines of code ..... and in reality I've averaged about 1 in 4 lines actually ending up in production (=> got good stastistics about this, now having more than 200,000 lines of code "written in anger") ..... ie: the 33k + 28k lines of production code for Plus23 probably took about 240k lines of actual written code (3/4ths are deleted) .... I've done too much typing in the last 10 months  .....​

I'll still have to work constantly to make sure that it pays all my bills, but that's more like a 2 or 3 hour daily commitment.

As a result, my schedule is pretty relaxed right now, and I'm re-arranging things to be more beneficial to optimal health. So at a high level, my schedule will look like:

\- Wake up at sunrise (6am to 6:30am)
\- Head to the beach and the ocean if possible for an hour
\- much around with fun stuff (like the Kruse forums ) on the computer for awhile
\- get out of the house from 9am onwards to at least 11am or 12pm (still have just eaten a little bit of fat and nothing else at this point)
\- maybe do some "workout" at midday
\- have lunch anywhere from 11am to 1pm
\- actual 3-hour work block from 2pm to 5pm
\- more activity from 5pm until sunset (earliest sunset in Cairns is 6pm at the end of June)
\- maybe have some protein after said activity .... or just skip dinner and do more fasting
\- do whatever enjoyable nonsense until bedtime

As for caloric goals, I don't really have a strict schedule. But I think it will be counter-productive for me to ever go below 2000kcal, or 100g of protein.

In all likelihood, I will:

\- eat around 2,000kcal a day (some counting needed) with anywhere from 100-150g for a couple of days
\- Then, I will get a little hungrier. On this day (usually after 2-3 days of going 2,000kcal) I will eat more protein -- probably 200g or more. Calories will shoot up to around 2,500-3,000kcal
\- Then I won't feel hungry and continue with the 2,000kcal plan.

(Random: when I came to Cairns last June, I ate roughly 2,200kcal on average a day, cycled at least 20km a day, ate only breakfast and lunch, and lost 3kg of pure fat in 2 weeks )

Exercise wise, it's just going to be boring low-stress stuff. Cycling whenever I need transport. Pullups, handstands, shadow boxing, core stability work, foam rolling, random crawling, etc .... just a bunch of random stuff whenever I think I need to move around (all the while ruminating on Pavel Kolar's clinical rehab principles). I may introduce some HIIT, but I won't unless I really need to use it to lose weight.

\---

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-25T10:11:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-159396>

**Higher Carbs with Seaonal Changes**

First up, philosophically speaking, it is only wise to switch things up when things are not working.

At this point, I'm still not faring very well. Ever since taking that 9 day trip to Brisbane and Melbourne, I've suffered from quite a fair bit of inefficiency.

Fortunately, all the damage that has resulted were only:

\+ **Massive weight gain** \-- Left Cairns around 79.5kg with a <79cm waist. 3 weeks after I get back to Cairns, I'm around 81kg with a 80cm waist.
\+ **Slight Sleep Disruptions** -- waking up 1 hour earlier than sunrise.
\+ **Dip in physical energy anywhere from 10am to 2pm**. However, if I get activity in the sunshine during this time, the fatigue goes away after 10 mins and doesn't return.

Mentally speaking, I am still very sharp. Logical reasoning capability has not reduced at all, and the rate at which I can process information has not dropped at all.

I am also surprised that there are no other symptoms that have commonly plagued me in the past:

\+ No gut issues (resolved within days of coming back to Cairns), and no foods that trigger any problems. Bristol score is very consistent
\+ No feet or hand swelling
\+ No exercise intolerance (don't feel bad after exercise)
\+ Still pretty yoked to the sun cycle -- eg: bowel movements still consistently 0 to 1.5 hours after sunrise, even without food ingestion, suggesting that the gut is yoked to the light cycle
\+ Measures of leptin sensitivity still good -- eg: tolerate fasting with no hunger, exercise performance is good, sweating is well controlled

\----

**So What's going on?**

Based on the symptoms, it looks to me like a standard cortisol over-production issue leading to looser collagen. The affected regions are likely the adipose tissue, and the central nervous system, which lead to inefficiencies cascading down to weight gain.

I should note that I'm going to bed at around 9pm, and sleep onset probably occurs 30-45 mins later. The slightly early waking is probably a sign that I am more sensitive to the effects of cortisol in the morning, especially since the homeostatic sleep requirements have likely been fulfilled at that point. I will not attempt to stay up later to try and squeeze out

All of these regressions in health occurred after I came back from flying 3 flights and going back to a high EMF zone.

The travel alone cannot account for the downhill health metrics. After all, in December, I travelled to Melbourne for 2 days, then Japan for 15 days, then Melbourne for 2 days, then back in Cairns for 8 days, then in NOLA for 7 days, then in Florida for 4 days, before getting back to Cairns. I ate like absolutely garbage in Japan, felt great (albeit a bit cold), and didn't put on any weight whatsoever. The same goes for the NOLA trip.

On top of that, I was feeling decent leaving for my trip about a month ago. There was no change in my habits after coming back from the trip as well. I was pretty much high fat, low carb, moderate protein for the 3 weeks since returning from the trip, and doing my usual routine.

\----

Therefore, **the likely underlying factor is the change in the seasons, and me not managing the transition well.**

Like everyone else, I will tend to do better at the extremes of the seasons. When I left for my December vacation, it was full summer in Cairns, and full winter in the Northern Hemisphere. The cold also helped with having good energetics during my vacation.

Sidenote: In the future, I'll probably always plan stressful events (like travel) during either early December to early January, or in June and July.​

Light levels have been decreasing in the past few weeks. Pressure levels will change, magnetic effects will change, and gravitational effects will change. I've got direct proof of the gravitational effects right at my doorst, with the sea level rising to a yearly high of 3.8m, when the normal highs in summer time are just over 2m. Clearly that is a large gravitional effect observed right there, and our cells are paying attention to that.

During said transition period, I got conceited, and assumed that the same behaviourial strategy would work. Of course, such periods are also when the 4 cups of morning coffee suddenly becomes a cause for cortisol over-production instead of a potent fat-burning agent. I'd love a magnetico and a cold tub right about now

High cortisol becomes an issue because it unwinds collagen and pushes things further apart. Large distance between components make for degradation signalling, and energetic inefficiency results (the observation being weight gain)

\----

**The key for me will be to:**

(a) moderate cortisol production
(b) provide extra signalling molecules
(c) improve collagen tensegrity as much as possible

**Specific Interventions**

\- As much red light as possible through all forms. I'll need to use my Vielight 810 more diligently. CT to produce endogenous red light is always good

\- As much morning sunlight as possible. I will always get outside from 9am to 12pm, rain or shine. This is easy and fun, and I'm already doing it pretty consistently. Dry season means less rainy days, and more sunshine.

\- As much swimming as possible. This should become easier as the dry season comes along, and the water gets cooler (no jellyfish). I spent >1hour in the water today

\- Increase carbs to 50-75g and 200g a day in a cyclic approach. I will basically time the 200g bolus of carbs during the days I do more exercise, which is typically 3 times a week. The rest of the days I won't explicitly restrict carbs to keto levels, which will likely net me up to 75g of carbs if I go nuts on the nuts and chocolate.

\- No cortisol stimulating foods. As of today, no coffee, no black tea, and probably just 1-2 cups of green tea in the morning

\- Stay hypo-caloric (deficit), and get lots of fasting done. This is mainly to facilitate extra autophagy together with the added free radical stimulus I'm getting with the carbs. My daily pattern will be to:

\+ wake up at 6am to 6:30am, get to the beach
\+ get some work done until 9am
\+ head out, do some stuff on the beach, do some exercise (this is where my workouts are placed)
\+ eat the main meal of the day anywhere from 12pm to 1pm
\+ little bit more activity before sunset
\+ if still hungry, eat some protein in the 2hours before sunset
\+ no more computer screens by 8pm
\+ audio work and some reading (next to red light) until 9pm
\+ stretching and then go to bed

**Sidenote:** I personally like to put the largest and most protein and/or carb heavy meal at midday (12pm).

This eating pattern will fit all seasons, since noon is always around 12-1pm regardless of the season.

During the short light cycles, you may not need another meal in the 2 hours before sunset.
In long light cycles, you would have one more small meal containing at least 25g of protein in the 2 hours before sunset (or 4 hours before bed, whichever is earlier).

I personally would fast until the main midday meal.​

\----

**Glucose Tolerance**

Did a little bit of blood glucose testing to see how my carb tolerance is. In part I want to see whether or not adding carbs right now is "safe" from the perspective of glucose clearance.

I did this test after 2 days of higher carbs (1 day at 200g carbs, 1 day at 150g carbs), and with just some moderate amount of activity during those 2 days.

The following was the progression today.

\+ fasting: **5.6 mmol/L => 100 mg/dL** (blood ketones at **0.7mM**)
\+ went for some exercise:
\- 9:30am head to the neighbouring beach and took a nap and read 'The Body Electric' by Robert Becker
\- sometime at 11am I get back to my local beach. Cycling about 15 mins both ways
\- 30 mins of walking around, 20 total pull-ups, some handstands, and some sensory awareness exercises
\+ post workout: **5.8 mmol/L => 105 mg/dL** (blood ketones at **0.8 mM**)
\+ 12pm meal:
\- glutinous rice 150g, some berries, some chocolate, whey protein 100g: **200g carbs and 100g protein**
\+ proceeded to sit around and do nothing but computer work until 4pm
\+ 1pm: **6.0 mmol/L => 108 mg/dL**
\+ 2pm: **6.4 mmol/L => 115 mg/dL**
\+ 3pm: **6.0 mmol/L => 108mg/dL**
\+ 4pm (before another meal): **7.9 mmol/L => 143mg/dL**
\+ 4pm meal: mackerel bowl with some seaweed (maybe around \[carbs: 30g, protein: 40g, fat: 40g, kcal: 600\])
\+ went out for a 20min walk on the beach with a 10min swim
\+ 5pm: **4.4 mmol/L => 80mg/dL**
\+ 6pm: **6.2 mmol/L => 111mg/dL**

Note that it took 3hours for the glutinous rice and whey protein combo to hit the body as glucose.

Basically I've got physiologic insulin resistance thanks to my prolonged low carb diet, that makes me want to maintain my blood sugar at roughly 110mg/dL.

I have really good ability to clear that glucose through muscular contractions. 200g of carbs and 100g of protein only caused my glucose to rise to a not-very-high 143mg/dL, and once I got active again, my blood sugar actually dropped pretty low before re-stabilising at the 110mg/dL level.

I'm not too concerned with the high blood glucose, since it's within the usual range seen in low carb dieters, and especially because I also see high levels of ketones, meaning that free FFAs are likely pretty high during this time.

This ability to clear glucose makes me believe that I will not be harmed by adding up to this level of carbs (200g a day), on an alternating daily basis in conjunction with exercise.

\-----

As with everything, I will test again in the comings weeks and re-assess . We'll see how things turn out in a month

.....

**Random Pre-Easter Update**

Weight loss experiment going very well. Dropped from 81.9kg with a 80cm waist on <27-Mar-2015>, to 79.7kg (-2.2) with a 78cm waist today (6 days later)

Looks like I was right that my problem was a signalling issue due to a lack of superoxide. Adding carbs fixed that, and I have no problem mobilising stored protons for fuel. More detailed updates in about a week (2 weeks since start of intervention).

\----
Went for my usual morning swim today. There were 2 other men swimming there -- 1 skinny guy whom I see almost every day doing his laps, 1 obese guy also doing laps.

Got out, went for a quick 300m walk in the direction away from my house, and then circled back less than 5 mins later, to see the obese man being dragged out. Got emergency CPR, and EMTs arrived pretty soon after, but 30 mins of emergency intervention didn't save him. EMTs couldn't jump his heart since it was already flat-lined. Likely cause of death is a seizure during his swim.

Living by the ocean with consistent bright light cycles doesn't fix circadian mismatches . You can guarantee that the drowned man lived with chronic nn-EMF exposure.

News Article -- [http://www.cairnspost.com.au/news/c...at-trinity-beach/story-fnjpusyw-1227288806431](http://www.cairnspost.com.au/news/cairns/medical-condition-could-be-cause-of-death-for-man-at-trinity-beach/story-fnjpusyw-1227288806431)

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-03T12:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-160211>

This post is totally an aside, and represents my personal opinions; a reflection of my idiosyncratic, dopamine-deficient brain

Recently I've been really listening to stuff whereby there are 2-4 simple melodies playing at the same time, which each would be boring on their own, but combine to form a unique melody.

Examples:

Really really really nice acoustic cover of 'Teardrop' by 'Massive Attack'

Tobias Rauscher's 'Still Awake' is an amazing display of percussive guitar, while maintaining a dual melody

'Four Prophets' by General Fuzz again overlays a couple of simple melodies. (Everything by 'General Fuzz' is awesome  -- [http://generalfuzz.net/](http://generalfuzz.net/))

\----

Then just some random dumps because I'm a total tool when it comes to certain kinds of music  (this post just represents my opinions of course)

'Glassy Sky' (I'll be strong till I see the end .....)

'The Gargoyle'. Paul Gilbert will always be the ultimate shredder!

'Let Me Hear'. SCREAMMMM!!!!  (Japanese Band) 'Fear, and Loathing in Las Vegas' does some pretty wicked shit with 4 different rifts playing alongside complex drum patterns, and with the song getting more and more complicated as it goes along. I like how it all came together.
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NF8GRMO5V\_4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NF8GRMO5V_4)

'季節は次々死んでいく' (seasons die one after another). Best poetry I've heard in awhile (It's in Japanese of course)
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wtJcLWeY114](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wtJcLWeY114)

As much as I complain that Chinese is fucking hard to learn, the ability to condense a lot meaning into pictorial symbols is remarkable. Japanese took it's writing system and much of it's vocabulary from Chinese, so it shares the same characteristics. This is the chorus from that last song:

Chorus, which starts at around 1m 30s is:

拝啓　忌まわしき過去に告ぐ　絶縁の詩
最低な日々の　最悪な夢の　残骸を捨てては行けず　ここで息絶えようと
後世　花は咲き君に伝う　変遷の詩
苦悩にまみれて　嘆き悲しみ　それでも途絶えぬ歌に　陽は射さずとも

\----
Literal translation in the same order as the Japanese text:

Dear Sir/Madam (拝啓), mourning (忌) the screwed-up past (まわしき過去) notification (告ぐ), breaking of relations (絶縁) poem (詩)
Worst days' (最低な日々), most dreadful dreams', (最悪な夢) ruins (残骸) are forsaken (捨てて) and I leave (行けず), this place (ここで) burden is laid down (息絶えよう)
Future life to come (後世), flowers bloom (花は咲き) in your path (君に伝う), poem of transition (変遷の詩)
In suffering (苦悩に) to be covered by (まみれて), to grieve and moan (嘆き悲しみ), and yet (それでも) this intermission (途絶え) song in (歌に), sun (陽) shine (射さ) always (ずとも)

\----
Grammatically correct English with implied meanings:

Dear Sir/Madam, I notify you with a poem of severance, mourning my screwed-up past
In this place I get rid of my burden -- my worst days, my worst dreams -- and leave the ruins (of my life)
Flowers will bloom in your path, but for my future life to come, I offer you this poem of transition (\*\*\*implied that "I'm still leaving")
I grieve and moan in my suffering, and yet during this intermission, the sun still shines

\----
Last Chorus is:

拝啓　今は亡き過去を想う　望郷の詩
最低な日々が　最悪な夢が　始まりだったと思えば　随分遠くだ
どうせ花は散り　輪廻の輪に還る命　
苦悩にまみれて　嘆き悲しみ　それでも途絶えぬ歌に　陽は射さずとも

Which in grammatically correct English, is:

Dir Sir/Madam, a poem of homesickness for the recently deceased (\*\*implied that the deceased are those that I left)
I am now very far from the first time I felt the pain (\*\* literal meaning is "worst dreams of my worst days is for the first time, very far away" => implied "this is a good future")
In any case, the flowers are now scattered (\*\*implied: the flowers that bloomed in your path -- mentioned in first chorus -- are no longer alive), the wheel of life-and-death returns to life
I grieve and moan in my suffering, and yet during this intermission, the sun still shines (\*\*implied: I still suffer today despite being much happier)
​

Everything is about the charge contained within your cells. You are small collection of coupled integrated electric magnetic and photonic couple systems designed to control energy flows in a specific fashion based upon your design.

Not extra water...........proper density water to allow quantum transfers of energy from outside inside and inside back out.

Humans can see but they are poor observers of nature. The eye can always see, but seeing is not believing. To believe you must understand. If the mind fails to understand, the eye remains blind to reality. Vision without action is a dream. Action without vision is simply a passage of time. Action with vision is harnessing a positive force for creating a difference, because that vision, if focused and coherent and can properly decode the ciphers embedded in nature.

Metastability is built in in to cells. We get sick when we are contrary to QED........but the beauty of this design is that all is reversible once we get the message that when you live within your design any disease is reversible. The laws of physics are bi-driectional. They are not a one way street or ticket to oblivion.

Your mind is like iron fillings on a sheet of paper........your environment is the magnet below the paper.........

at sleep the magnet's forces reorganizes your fillings back to your previous form based upon the lines of force in that field.......within day time light and electric currents move some of your atoms around in space and time. They alter your atomic geometry, so to speak.

That small daily movement is what you call life.

Your brain and tensegrity systems act in coherent fashion when they are pre loaded with energy that is acting as one in a syncytium and your atoms in cells codify those small atomic changes.

And you learn from those small daily movements as alterations of sizes and shapes. When your brain sense this is a good movement, a decision that is made based upon the redox or an increase of energy flows within that open system........you automatically magnetically imprint that change into your magnets field at night in REM sleep by using light and O2 to generate free radicals to entangle and coherently connect those new atoms to your tissues. You are recycling atoms from chaos into your system..........and it works in a slightly new way every day in your to organize the atoms in you.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-10T02:15:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-160762>

**Update <Fri-10-Apr-2015>
**
OK, so it's now 14 days since I've started another experiment with achieving weight loss.

Starting <2015-03-27 Fri>, I switched over from high fat, moderate protein, slight caloric deficit, to:

\- Significant caloric deficit (eating between 1,500-2,000kcal a day)
\- Anywhere from 100-200g carbs a day. This almost always took the form of white rice (starchy carbs)
\- usually moderate protein of around 80g (+/- 20g), and 4 days with protein 150g
\- some experiments with feeding window changes, but ultimately settling on a 8am to 12pm eating window
\- cut off all tech use at 8pm (I broke this rule on 3 days, and you can see in the "Metrics" section below that my weight loss stalled )

All other factors remained roughly the same, and dietary food sources remain pretty standard.

**Some other notes:**

\- High-dose Nicotine use continued, and seems to have a impact in mobilising proton stores (nicotine is a stimulator of Uncoupling Proteins). I'd always do 4mg first thing in the morning, and then usually more in the afternoon. Maybe 10mg a day

\- Activity levels remain roughly consistent. I'd get out of bed at around 6:30am, be on the beach until 7:30am, then grab some nuts and chocolate at 8am, then focus my main activity at 10am-12pm, then eat again, then possibly run out to the beach for more activity at 4-5pm.

\- Rule of thumb when eating has been to have around 500kcal worth of chocolate and nuts in the morning (1/2 bar of Lindt + handful of nuts), then at noon, I eat my 500kcal worth of rice (I weigh 300g raw, and just eat half of it), and then whatever balance I have is allocated to a protein source.

\-----

I started out at **81.9kg (180.5lbs)** with a **80cm (31.5'')** waist.
14 days later, I'm **79.5kg (175.2lbs) \[-2.4kg, -5.3lbs\]** with a **77.5cm (30.5'') \[-2.5cm, -1.0''\]** waist.

 That's pretty drastic for what seems like such a small change . You cannot account for this with calorie counts. It's some energetic efficiency gained with the simultaneous fasting + carb consumption (superoxide production) + night-time tech limitation.

Again, circadian biology trumps diet. Yes, putting myself into a food-deprived fasting state was needed as a stimulus, but my body's subsequent reaction to that stimulus isn't governed by how much mathematical food-derived-energy deficit I'm creating. Also see the '**Killing the Tech after sunset**' section below for some surprising results.

Took some pics today first thing in the morning for bodycomp reference (\*\*red speedo warning\*\*): [Front](http://tanyewwei.com/pics/bodycomp/20150410-front.jpg), [Back](http://tanyewwei.com/pics/bodycomp/20150410-back.jpg), [BackDB](http://tanyewwei.com/pics/bodycomp/20150410-backdb.jpg), [Abs](http://tanyewwei.com/pics/bodycomp/20150410-abs.jpg), [Hands-on-Hips](http://tanyewwei.com/pics/bodycomp/20150410-handships.jpg)

Sidenote: I've got very even subcutaneous fat distribution, meaning that I don't store more fat particularly in one region. I suspect this is a good thing, since I won't be prone to visceral fat stores, and any subcutaneous fat is easily mobilised and used (metabolised for energy, converted to BAT during CT, etc ...)

\----
**Positive Results**

Collagen issues seem to be much improved. Hip mobility has increased (I can tell mainly from how close I am to floor in a front split). Back tightness has decreased.

Strength on pullups have increased, which is partly to be expected with bodyweight decreases, but is also a good indicator that I'm not losing a lot of contractile tissue.

Eating rice literally kills my hunger completely . I usually only felt hungry when I did substantially more activity in the afternoon (only drinking water afterward)

\----
**Negative Results**

One negative so far has been the persistent effect of waking up around 5:45am to 6:00am. I know it's about 30-40 mins before sunrise because when I do wake up, I'll just stick my Vielight 810 up my nose and use it to give my brain some red light. The vielight automatically switches off after 20 mins (length of a "treatment session"), and I haven't needed more than 2 sessions before I see the sun shining (about 6:30am now).

Another negative is that drinking any amount of black tea or coffee during the day (even in the morning) leads to longer time for sleep onset. Green tea does not affect me at all.

I hypothesised that during the process of weight loss and improving collagen tightness, I am more sensitive to the effects of cortisol. I still don't know whether this theory can merit, but I'm avoiding those foods anyway. I tolerate chocolate very well, so it's obviously not a xanthine related issue.

\----
**Moving Forward
**
The "metrics" section shows that I gained a little bit of weight in the last 3 days, but waist measurement dropped significantly. I also looked visibly leaner in the morning.

This correlated with a slight bump in protein intake. I think I'll increase protein to 120-150g a day and see how things turn out 2 weeks from now.

Other than that, everything else is a success. **Don't change what is working**.

\----
**Killing the Tech after sunset**

The night-time tech cut-off was accidental. I got my red LED headlamp in the mail on <Fri-27-Mar-2015> (start of diet), which allowed me to read off books/kindle at night without disrupting circadian rhythms.

Nowadays I think I'll reserve night-times for reading, and listening to audio material (**neat circadian-appropriate Productivity hack** ).

The impact of not killing the tech by 8pm seemed to be substantial. I didn't lose weight from the 3-day period of <Fri-03-Apr-2015> to <Sun-05-Apr-2015>, all of which days I didn't do anything different, but stayed in front of the computer a little later than before (past 9pm).

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-15T02:01:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-161214>

**Random update**: currently sitting at 78.6kg with a 77cm waist measurement . Weight loss plan is working, and I'm looking leaner by the day.

\----
**Random Music Musings. Also, on Giving Up the Electric Guitar**

I picked up an Acoustic guitar when I was about 13.5 years old. Then a few months later, I listened to these 3 songs, and decided to play the Electric Guitar.

'Black Winter Night' by Dragonforce

'Valley of the Damned' by Dragonforce

'Telegraph Road' by Dire Straits

And so began weekends spent with 6-8hour session of sitting with that nn-EMF generator of an electric guitar in front of me. The fact that I got good at playing the damn thing really quickly made it even more tempting to spend more time with that magnetic device in my lap.

I managed to play 'Valley of the Damned' in quick time, and 'Telegraph Road' was a long slog, but I nailed that too. Never quite got to 'Black Winter Night' though.

Being 15 years old, I got into the whole high school rock band phenomenon, played a couple of gigs, and of course, spent a lot of time in recording studios and stages surrounded by blue light . If you search for "Yew-wei Canon in D" on youtube, I think you can still find one of my solo performances from that time of the 'Canon in D' rendition by Jerry C. One of those old videos that I would have probably wanted to keep private, but oh well, that's the youtube generation for you (everything gets recorded), and at least it's proof of my skill level at the time

Continued obsessively playing the Electric Guitar until I left for Australia at age 17, and still practiced at least the technical aspects pretty religiously, while composing a couple tunes. At some stage just before I turned 18, I wanted to make this a semi-professional thing, but decided that Programming Computers was more profitable, and swapped the magnetic nightmare of a guitar for the blue-light hypnotic spiral that was the programmer's text editor.

Fast forward another 5 years, and I don't plug in the electric guitar any more. Not worth the EMF risk, and all I really do is run through scales and riffs (while unplugged) to keep my technical skill level on the thing sharp. Nowadays I'm mostly playing acoustic guitars instead.

I still like to the pick up the Electric Guitar, but until there comes a day when I can get appropriate EMF shielding for the thing, it's going to remain a back-pocket skill that I consider given-up-on (but yet continue to practice unplugged on a daily basis in hopes of that day ).

What I'd really like is a carbon-fibre body acoustic that is built and feels EXACTLY like an electric guitar

(META: useless skills are fun, and are still useless)

.....

Practicing on acoustic is a great idea but I really have been focusing on minimizing the risks in my environment so I can start to feel better. THEN I can gradually add the guitar.
That is how I will be able to judge if I am improving - if I'm playing guitar regularly and ENJOYING it (not forcing myself) then I know I'm getting somewhere!

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-20T08:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-161771>

**Random Thoughts after Today's Epic April 2015 Q&A
**
I think I'll be making some notes on this once I get the recording

\----

Excerpts with my emphasis on the Resveratrol study I mentioned in the Q&A -- [http://qhwiki.com/study:sirt1-activation-by-resveratrol](http://qhwiki.com/study:sirt1-activation-by-resveratrol)

Key quote:

**SIRT1 may need the help of an activator to take advantage of this inherent property and discriminate among possible substrate targets.**​

Ha, there you go with light activation again

\-----

Nicotine and Cannabis have both been able to heal people's bad guts. I think both act through ROS and RNS, whereby those free radicals liberate biophotons when scavenged (and in the gut, that scavenger may well be the H- anion produced by gut bacteria).

I need to dig up studies about the effect these 2 compounds have on ROS and RNS synthesis in the GALT.

\-----

Regarding L-glutamine and cancer, I had this paper sitting in an open tab. I think it was something that @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) shared on facebook -- [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3641590/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3641590/)

Haven't read it fully yet, but I should tomorrow (AEST <21-Apr-2015>)

Quote:

These experimental observations serve as preliminary evidence to support our hypothesis that Gln (L-Glutamine) may play an important role in cancer cell survival and growth through enzymatic release of ammonia for acid resistance ([Figure 1E](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3641590/figure/fig1/)).

In other words, **the reliance of cancer cells on Gln is not just for nutrition but more importantly also for the purpose of fighting acid stress** as a result of Warburg Effect.

As the first step for metabolic utilization of Gln involves the glutaminase activity, **it is inherently difficult to separate the two potential functions of Gln — carbon/nitrogen source versus acid resistance**​

\-----

Random note. I seem to get harder stools (lower bristol score) when I eat a lot of red meat protein, but not when I eat non-red meat protein like chicken  or fish.

Red meat has higher iron concentration. Could the iron be the thing that's reducing the needed light delivery to the GALT

....

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-22T23:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-162127>

****Update <Fri-24-Apr-2015>
****
Another 13 days of this protocol, and I've lost another bunch of weight. Too fast in fact, and I'm finally starting to see some negative effects .

\----
**Positive results**

**(1)** Went from **79.5kg (175.2lbs)** with a **77.5cm (30.5'')** waist, to **77.5kg (170.8lbs)** with a waist slightly under **77cm (30.3'').** That's -2kg and a change of at least -0.5cm from just 13 days ago

**(2)** Joint flexibility in general improved again, especially knees and hips. Trigger points in my calves are drastically reduced. Tightness in trapezius largely reduced.

**(3)** Gut health stayed surprisingly good

**(4)** Cognitive performance and mood remained very good

\----
**Negative Results**

First off, today was the first day where I lost a significant amount of sleep. Went to bed at 9pm, and probably woke up around 4:30am . This alone is enough to tell me to switch up what I'm doing.

I saw some skin dryness, especially around the ankles. Again, this is related to brain function.

Had a couple of pimples, and that's likely due to massive liberation of proton stores in body fat.

\----
**Intervention**

Immediate intervention today is to chew some nicotine, put on some of my ice packs for at least 2 hours, and get on the ocean and the beach as much as possible. Good thing today is likely a sunny day too, so I'll be spending time in the sun with some topical coconut oil smeared all over my skin.

The main intervention is to get myself out of a chronically food-electron deprived state. Fat intake is going to go up a lot, primary through chocolate, nuts, coconut oil, butter, and more fatty fish.

Eating schedule should remain roughly the same -- first meal at around 8am, lunch anywhere from 11am to 1pm, skip dinner. I will raise food amounts to at least 2500kcal most of the time.

Not forgetting my ultimate goal: To improve skeletal tissue collagen quality. My lower back is still tight, and Josh Lamaro will look at the pics I posted below and still see the forward pelvic rotation on my right side.

I still think that a food-restricted autophagy stimulus is useful, but this must now be done transiently. I will likely spend anywhere from 2-4 days a week in a food restricted state. Experimentation with amounts and type of food input is going to be needed.

I will also need to start exercise interventions. Up to this point, I have lost the weight completely using diet alone, with no modifications to my relatively lackadaisy exercise schedule -- cycling for transport alone maybe 3 times a week for 40mins each, random bodyweight exercise and stretching when I feel like it (about 30-60mins a day), and some walking on the beach (maybe 60-90mins depending on the day).

Core stability (spatial awareness) and flexibility exercises need to be emphasised more. I will still avoid endurance exercise, but maybe some appropriately timed HIIT is warranted. This is again, something I will have to experiment with.

\----

I'm pretty satisfied with the results thus far . Let's see what happens in the coming weeks.

\----

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-24T07:43:47+0300

Slept well last night once I raised food intake. Don't recall any dreams, but woke up feeling very good.

LOL, paper was really long. Haven't finished reading it, and now a new blog which I must read too.

\----
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

As a programmer, I thrive in environments where I have 1,000 times more information than I can possibly process laid out in front of me, and then being told dismissively, "Read the Fucking Manual" (RTFM), or "Just look at the source code, everything is there". (and since it is source code, literally everything about the computational behaviour of the program really is there, regardless of how difficult to understand it is ).

Then I proceed to pick out the parts relevant to the job at hand at any one time, while having a broad understanding of where to find stuff. I don't need to necessarily understand the low-level details of items, I just need to know where to find the information, and dive in when the need calls for it.

\----
@[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/)

Heh, words of wisdom from a not-yet-24-year-old . I actually started writing some aphorisms on my site  -- [http://tanyewwei.com/geyan/](http://tanyewwei.com/geyan/)

Paraphrased quote from Nassim Nicholas Taleb: Frivolity is as unattractive in the old, as wisdom is in the young.

ie: Nobody ever got laid by "being wise" when they were young.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-28T12:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-162631>

**Completely Random

(1)** Lost my glasses 2 weeks ago while diving in the ocean. Rough ocean, surfaced from a dive at a depth of maybe about 3m, and then immediately got hit in the face by a wave . Forget about finding a pair of glasses in that depth of water. Had to get a new pair, and was reminded again that my eyesight sucks!!! (regular-use glasses are -8.00 in the right eye, and -9.00 in the left eye)

Need to fix this somehow in the next 2-3 years, and it's going to be a tough biohack . TBH, money is the limiting factor right now, only because if I had enough cash, I wouldn't be spending 5-6 hours extra a day in front of a computer trying to make more of it .

Anyway, I want to put this theory to the test that **(a)** the eye can restore itself from the current elongated (myopic) shape, and **(b)** there's a lot more factors at play, both in bending light to hit the retina, and in the way the brain interprets the signal to give a clear image. My research is still in the early days, but this is something that I've got huge personal incentive to fix.

For now, more diving in water, no wearing glasses while on the beach, and I'm probably going to experiment again with always using blue-blockers on the computer regardless of time of day. Of course, more research into relaxation and other types of eye exercises.

**(2)** Weight is holding steady. I'm still around 77.5kg with a waist below 77cm. I'm eating a lot , but ever since raising fat, I don't feel like eating either carbs nor protein . IMO that's a good sign that I don't really need that much of those 2 macronutrients, which in turn is a good sign of Leptin sensitivity. Still going to eat some rice though .

That said, eating too much carbs or problem gives me visible stomach distention, and I feel full, whereas eating fat does not, and I want to eat more ... and yet the day after eating lots of fat, I look better in the mirror (and feel leaner) despite the fat intake. LOL genetix (stab at someone from New York on the QH FB Group ). Asian Boy in the tropics is supposed to be eating lots of carbs right??  Yet my body likes fat better, and can obviously handle large amounts of carbs as well .... Good!

**(3)** Raising calories is killing my desire for nicotine and increasing by reaction to the compound as well. I'm getting a huge buzz from a 4mg stick, which wasn't the case when dieting, I'd barely notice the effect. This is probably a good thing that I naturally don't feel like having the compound.

**(4)** When eating lots of fat, the 5-o-clock shadow is real even on this hairless Asian

**(5)** Too much goddamn computer work. I'm spending too much time trying to make money. I have no clue how you guys with families reconcile this problem .

That expensive bottle of Grange I'm bringing to the Kruse Cruise better lead to some good conversation .

.....

Call it what you want, it is what it is, I wonder what crystals do when they are known, but I know what crystals do when they condense. There is a higher tier to this. You don't see it. I often wonder how much food do we really need. If I eat a lot of fat, i am never hungry, I can eventually go on foodless as my body keeps great energy levels using what is available in the system. My fat intake lowered now as the spring fully unwinded around, but I do not feel like compensating it with protein and carbs. I have no one to discuss this or compare it to, but the truth is as the due date is approaching I need much less food. I can go foodless if I let myslef. Just a lot of water.

My hubby has about the same refraction. He always goes surfing without his glasses, because splashing salty sea water blurs them up. I don't understand how he does that and never get in to accidents. Anyway he always complaints after summer that his sight with his glasses on is far too sharp and bright. His optician said his sight improves because when surfing he can stare and gaze at the horizon far away at sea a lot. Somehow this helps myopia. But it never is a sustainable improvement for him. During winter the effect wears off, not so much surfing and lots of screen involved activities. Maybe worth to look in to... glassless distant horizon gazing.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-04T00:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-163218>

**Exercise and Bodycomp
**
Spurred on by this thread in the QH Facebook Group: [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/997163460296548/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/997163460296548/)

I decided to put up some pictures showing the difference in myself from my heavy-exercising days, and today.

Pictures on left are from <May-2012> and pictures on right are from end <Apr-2015>, so a 3 year difference.

On the left (2012 pics) I'm about 78kg, waist about 78cm.
On the right (2015 pics) I'm about 77.5kg, waist about 77cm.

Back when the 2012 pics were taken, I was deadlifting about 170kg conventional pretty easily, about 160kg sumo, and squat was around 130kg x 3. Not very strong, but stronger than most people . In the right, I haven't touched a weight for 6 months (not even kettlebells), and bodyweight exercises are limited to ONLY stuff on a pull-up bar. (not even any pushups ).

LOL exercise . But objectively speaking, my legs are smaller now, while my lats and deltoids are bigger, and waist is smaller. I like my current state better, both from a health point-of-view, as well as the aesthetic of my look.

My current thoughts on muscular gain have been stated in the Ubiquitination #6 thread:
\- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...uitination-6-is-live.13196/page-8#post-158400](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uncoupling-light-uncouples-your-knowledge-ubiquitination-6-is-live.13196/page-8#post-158400)
\- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...uitination-6-is-live.13196/page-9#post-158485](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uncoupling-light-uncouples-your-knowledge-ubiquitination-6-is-live.13196/page-9#post-158485)

In short, I think that muscular hypertrophy of anything but Type 1 fibers fundamentally leads to energetic inefficiency. Sarcoplasmic hypertrophy, and hypertrophy of Type 2 fibers, does little to help the body be more efficient at utilising energy.

Type 1 fibers contain mitochondria, which drive energy production, whereas Type 2 fibers either have little or no motichondria. Sarcoplasmic hypertrophy increases distances between proteins, and reduces likelihood of quantum mechanical effects (like the enzyme tunnelling needed for )

And then of course, you're putting energy into muscles instead of your brain

IMO the best types of exercise will help you:
(a) preserve your Type 1 fibers (not necessarily grow them)
(b) improve the innervation ratio of individual motor neurons, thus improving "how well you can perceive your body", which fundamentally is about better signalling between the nervous system and your muscles

IMO, the best sort of way to introduce exercise to control Ubiquitination is:
(a) Exercise for short bursts, always after cortisol is low (enough red light) -- at least 2 hours after sunrise, and ideally in the last 2 hours of sunset
(b) If exercising in the last 2 hours before sunset, either only eat a small amount of protein (25g) or nothing at all

.....

Could be the lighting but your face looks more chiseled now too. Short bursts, never maxing out is how some Russian olympians have been training for decades. In the book the "Naked Warrior" by Pavel they talk about doing one legged squats w/ heavy (80-90% max. weight) kettle bells only doing one or two reps and never to anywhere near exhaustion. They would do this type of exercising, in short staggered bursts, throughout the day. You want the power and strength, but not necessarily the size.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-07T03:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-163478>

> [Danny said:](/goto/post?id=163276)
>
> Could be the lighting but your face looks more chiseled now too. Short bursts, never maxing out is how some Russian olympians have been training for decades. In the book the "Naked Warrior" by Pavel they talk about doing one legged squats w/ heavy (80-90% max. weight) kettle bells only doing one or two reps and never to anywhere near exhaustion. They would do this type of exercising, in short staggered bursts, throughout the day. You want the power and strength, but not necessarily the size.

> [Mike Collins said:](/goto/post?id=163304)
>
> Deadlifts and squats are awesome exercises

Squats and Deadlifts are horrible with my body proportions. Especially given the poor health status at the time that I was doing them

I've actually tried the old Soviet Block routines. Daily squatting to a daily 1RM. Must be relatively easy, at most RPE 9 while feeling pretty fresh afterward. Best way of assessing RPE in my opinion is bar speed through sticking points (points in the lift where acceleration goes negative; bar speed starts to slow). In my case, that's just above parallel, as you'll see in the vid below.

This is an old video from 2012 of a 160kg Sumo Deadlift. You can see that I've learnt how to round my back and initiate the pull from the lats, which gives a better leverages at the expense of greater risk of back injury. To get to a vertical tibia position, I am very bent over. It's even worse when I'm in a conventional deadlift position.

I consciously learnt to pull with a rounded back, and then keep it rounded all the way until having to lockout, which kept a somewhat rigid position under most of the force was compression instead of shear (the back is much more resilient under compressive loads). Lockout was also very hard for me as a result.

I wouldn't consider this optimal exercise at all

Second vid is of some squats. You can see the bar slow down right as I get out of the hole on the 130kg attempt, which was the daily maximum for that day. If I spend less than one second through my sticking point, I know that it's not causing much CNS stress (therefore allowing you to squat again the next day). The rest of the lift is easy once I get through that point.

I like the daily lifting philosophy though, and love Pavel's idea of: Exercise as frequent as possible, while being as fresh as possible. His ideas of treating Exercise as "Practicing a Skill" is something that I do today.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-07T04:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-163485>

**Minor Update
**
I'm just coasting and letting diet an lifestyle settle where it may. Bodyweight has been stable the past week. Today was about 77.7kg and a 77cm waist. Feeling satisfied with food intake, sleeping mostly fine, libido and energy pretty good, so my body doesn't mind being in its current state.

Eating schedule is still a heavy heavy breakfast at around 8am, which is about 1.5 hours after waking. I may experiment with adding a small protein dose at 5pm.

Sleep is only "mostly fine" because duration is slightly reduced every time I try to add more starch carbs into the diet. On most days, I'd say I would get 100g of carbs just from the sugars and other foods that I'm eating. eg: ketchup, chilli sauce, and the herbs with my mackerel probably gives me 30g of usable carbs. Chocolate has carbs, even Lindt 90% has about 15g carbs when you eat the whole bar (which I often do ). Nuts have some carbs, especially when I'm eating so much of them.

While I don't care for going full on keto, I still feel better at this 100g dose of carbs, rather than putting in some starch to get to around the 200g a day mark. Head is just clearer, and I sleep better.

I should probably be holding back on the carb intake as the seasons change anyway. I get 11hours of sunlight at about a 55deg angle most of the day on the June Solstice, but that's still diminished compared to summer time levels.

Waxing Gibbous Moon probably isn't that good for sleep either, since it peaks during the early daylight hours, So you end up having the moon exerting it's full force on you at about 1-2am right now and mucking things up, which is exactly when you should be in deep sleep mode creating entangled particles.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-12T05:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-163908>

Goddamit I'm getting like 2-3 mosquito bites a day

Happened once I switched back to lower protein, high fat, low carb. That's also when I started getting leaner despite eating much more . Not counting calories, but probably:

\- Some mackerel and/or sardines (600kcal)
\- Bar of chocolate (600kcal)
\- Lots of nuts (probably 1500-2000kcal)
\- some protein at 4-5pm (100kcal)
\- maybe some honey (200kcal)

In any case, that's like 80% fat, and I'm still in a state whereby I'm losing fat slowly -- Today I'm 77.3kg .

Also getting a transient cold stimulus in the morning, since most mornings are 15-17C, and I'm out in the ocean.

Ketosis => Mobilise fat to CO2 and water => CO2 expiration makes you a mosquito magnet.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-14T07:00:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-164192>

What I do is not really considered proper "free-diving" if you ask me . I just swim out from the beach until the water is deep enough and start going up and down. Sometimes for 10 mins, sometimes up to 40 mins. I'd estimate that the deepest I'd go is about 2x my body-length, which for a 1.89m guy is probably close to 4m, but nothing formal. 3m is definitely easy, and I don't really feel any increase in pressure from about 2m deep onward.

Water temp here is warm mate!  Average: 26.1°C / 78.9°F -- [http://www.seatemperature.org/australia-pacific/australia/trinity-beach-may.htm](http://www.seatemperature.org/australia-pacific/australia/trinity-beach-may.htm) . No such thing as ocean CT in the tropics.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-15T03:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-164320>

**Misc Update
**
This calorie thing is really not useful  -- [http://caloriesproper.com/artificial-light-regulates-fat-mass-no-bueno/](http://caloriesproper.com/artificial-light-regulates-fat-mass-no-bueno/)

My diet recently has been really boring. I'm a fan of not adding anything that I don't need. (green vegetables are one of the many things that I don't need ). Very literally, the list is down to:

-   Canned fish -- mackerel, sardines
-   whey protein
-   nuts -- (in order of quantity eaten) macadamia, hazelnut, pecan, almond, pistachio, walnut, brazil nut
-   dark chocolate
-   coconut oil and butter
-   seaweed -- laver
-   some raw honey (started buying local after @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) started giving me a hard time for not doing so . These guys are about 25km from me -- [http://www.honeyhousekuranda.com/shop/index.php](http://www.honeyhousekuranda.com/shop/index.php))
-   green tea
-   dried herbs and spices -- turmeric, black pepper, dill, oregano, parsley, basil, chives, marjoram, cumin, coriander, chillies, garam masala
-   condiments -- chilli sauce and ketchup

Supplements:

-   Trans-resveratrol -- 300mg first thing in the morning
-   Curcumin -- about 300mg first thing in the morning
-   Quercetin -- 400mg first thing in the morning
-   Vitamin K2 -- 5mg a day with food
-   Nicotine Gum -- anywhere from 0mg to 12mg a day, depending on how stressed I think I feel

Lifestyle pattern is consistent:

-   Wake at sunrise 6:30am, post some random stuff on the internet, aim to head out to the beach by 7+am
-   Breakfast at 8am. A lot of food . If I have to eat more later it's because I didn't eat enough here, but I'm getting better at calibrating the appropriate amount
-   Computer work and misc stuff for the rest of the day, usually interspersed with 2 hours of beach time
-   Usually beach again at 4pm, then 25g of whey protein
-   misc stuff and then all blue-light or nn-EMF emitting tech off by 8pm. Read books beside red light LED until maybe 9pm, then bed anywhere from 9-10pm

Despite eating a lot of food, I'm 77.0kg this morning, and looking better by conventional fitness-aesthestic standards . Obeying circadian rhythms works . Brain is working pretty well right now, and body is getting better.

Going to get some 90W red LEDs at around 630nm soon for experimentation.

> Also, wanted to confirm your K2 dosage of 5mg. 5mg= 5000cmg. I have tablets that are 100 mcg, so to get 5mg I would need to take 50 of these each day??
>
> Maybe you mean 0.5mg which is 500mcg

Should have been more specific. It's Vitamin K2 MK-4 (menatetranone). Post edited for clarity.

What you have is likely the MK-7 subfraction.

These are some notes I posted on MK-4 on the QH Facebook group:

I initially just took it on faith and experimentation with the various types of Vitamin K to see which had a better effect on my gut. I also kept hearing Jack talk about K2 supplementation specifically with MK-4 -- [http://www.jackkruse.com/top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://www.jackkruse.com/top-ten-paleo-supplements/)

My position currently is:

(1) MK-4 has shorter half life => changes to dosing can quickly affect outcomes. ie: the danger of dosing too high is less

(2) MK-4 is generally tolerated at very high doses (>50mg), and it seems that we can clear it well. Again, not likely any harm from overdose

(3) If your gut is good, you can synthesize enough of the longer isoprene Vitmain K2's MK-7 through MK-9. We cannot produce MK-4, so dietary supplementation is probably good.

(4) MK-4 is shorter, and thus smaller, and targets different tissues and can be carried by different proteins. It definitely functions differently than the longer chain K2's, and I suspect that deficiency is more common than not

So between some potential benefits, and not much harm, I tend to favour MK-4 supplementation.

I can't speak for my personal experience, because Vit K2 MK-4 was just one of a few supplements that I've been using during my disease reversal. I suspect the resveratrol could have an even bigger effect
​

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-15T11:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-it-slow.9428/post-164346>

Got a new smartphone today (for work ....). The video recording is scary good. What the hell does this tech need to get any better? (#neoluddite)

Pretty typical day during the Cairns dry season. A little bit cooler than usual today though, 17C low, and about 25C at the time the video was shot.

The Far North Queesland coast is a little unique, in that it's just a narrow band before the tableland range. You have maybe a 3-5km of beach, swanp, and minor rainforest, before you hit a slop that bring you up to the Tablelands that are up to 480m above sea level if I'm not wrong. But whatever, it's probably a 30 min drive from where I'm at, to Kuranda, which is 400m above sea level.

The tablelands also happen to be one of the major growing region in FNQ. Probably just because of the cooler climate and greater UV radiation. You get year round harvests of various plants.

You can smell the Nitrogen recyling going on while in the swamp lands . Ammonia smell is strong from all that decomposing material. O2 concentration from mangroves is supposedly higher too. Watch out for the stingers and crocs though
