# Thread: Sous Vide your bacon?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cinnamon — 2014-01-23T06:27:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106689>

In the recent reset community webinar, Jack told us how to cook our bacon to maximize the cysteine. I think he said to use a sous vide? I was a bit confused when I googled what that was and pictures of food in plastic bags showed up! I think I might have misunderstood?

In my google search, I also found recipes where people were cooking their bacon in water, so I tried that this morning. I put just enough water (good water, of course) to cover the bacon and then cooked over medium low until all the water evaporated and then kept cooking a couple more minutes - until the bacon crisped up a bit. It was delicious! And the fat in the pan was fabulous too! LOL please understand that I adore bacon!

So, what's the right way to cook it?

### caroline — 2014-01-23T08:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106696>

Cin ..... You use plastic bags for the sou vide. What you did sounds really interesting. I have been wondering about simmering a piece of pork belly in water - what do you think. Is the plastic bag just to protect the meat? Or does it have an added benefit?
We could just wrap the meat in saran wrap and simmer away? I used to make seafood sausages and do that....

### Andrea — 2014-01-23T09:10:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106703>

The point of sous vide cooking is to cook things at an exact temperature. A sous vide machine will keep the temperature of the water bath at your preset temperature Â±0.5Â°C or so. A regular stove or oven will never give you that precision.

The plastic bags are for vacuum packing the food (sous vide = under vacuum).
You vacuum pack the food to remove all the air from the bag, beacuse air leads heat worse than water, and it would also make the plastic bag - and parts of your food - float. And having the food in plastic bags makes the food cook in its own juice, which makes it very tender and juicy, and it doesn't lose any nutrients to the surrounding water.

And as for cooking in plastic bags... Well, most manufacturers of these vacuum bags claim that their bags are free from BPA and softeners. At least something. Another point to consider is that you normally don't boil your food or cook it at high temperatures when you cook sous vide. Even not-so-fine meats like prime rib can be cooked to perfection at around 57-58Â°C with enough time.

### cinnamon — 2014-01-23T23:44:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106827>

Caroline and Rerun, thanks so much for all the valuable info regarding the sous vide method! And thank you, Jack, for confirming that this is what you meant.

How long do you expect it would take to cook my bacon by the sous vide method?

Last night I saw Jack and Misty in the Optimal Kitchen video where we get to see Jack's kitchen. Great video! In that, Jack talked about cooking bacon in the oven at 200 degrees. I guess this is also a good way to cook your bacon!

Yesterday I purchased some sliced pork belly - a first for me. This morning I put it in the oven at 200 degrees. I left it in there for nearly 2 hours as the slices were really thick. I ate eggs and bulletproof tea while waiting because it did make for a late breakfast. It was wonderful! Will try the oven method with regular bacon next (and expect it take the 20 to 30 minutes or so that Jack and Misty discussed).

### Hope — 2014-01-23T23:50:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106830>

Cinnamon, how did you make bulletproof tea? With EVCO?

### cinnamon — 2014-01-24T01:10:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106839>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=106830)
>
> Cinnamon, how did you make bulletproof tea? With EVCO?

I use a large cup of green tea and add about 2 tbsp butter, 1 tsp coconut oil and a half tsp d-ribose. That's the winter version. If I make it during the summer, I use 1 tsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil together with the d-ribose.

### Hope — 2014-01-24T02:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106845>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=106839)
>
> I use a large cup of green tea and add about 2 tbsp butter, 1 tsp coconut oil and a half tsp d-ribose. That's the winter version. If I make it during the summer, I use 1 tsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil together with the d-ribose.

Oh thank you for sharing. Wow. I do not do coffee, though just got it all ready for DH. I never thought of doing it with tea. THanks so much...

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-24T11:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106893>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106696)
>
> I have been wondering about simmering a piece of pork belly in water - what do you think. ....

I put a couple of pieces of pork in the slow cooker on low setting overnight with 2 large chopped onions and water. Fished out the pork, added a ladle full of the onion broth, added seasoning (I like seaweed sprinkles) and breakfast was ready.  Pork was quite lean so will probably add more fat next time, or get a fattier cut of meat. Also have the rest of onion and meat juices in the water as the basis of a soup later.

### caroline — 2014-01-24T11:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-106894>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=106893)
>
> I put a couple of pieces of pork in the slow cooker on low setting overnight with 2 large chopped onions and water. Fished out the pork, added a ladle full of the onion broth, added seasoning (I like seaweed sprinkles) and breakfast was ready.  Pork was quite lean so will probably add more fat next time, or get a fattier cut of meat. Also have the rest of onion and meat juices in the water as the basis of a soup later.

so pork belly would have worked great in the slow cooker? trouble is I like pork belly with honey and soy sauce etc!

### acasey — 2016-06-30T01:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide-your-bacon.9437/post-197548>

I let pork belly simmer in water and when done saute it with veggies.

# Thread: MTHFR and PCOS and Hormones, Oh My!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 77
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T07:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106691>

Hi!
I've been around for a really long time- I was a long time MDA follower and because of that, I was there for the beginning of the original discussion over on MDA, back some time ago- was it 2011? 2010? I don' recall! It's been so long. I was a silent follower, letting people like Shiijin and MamaGrok do all of the talking, but I diligently followed every blog post, every message on the monster thread, read every single reply. You'll even see a post or two from me way back then, but I'm shy about posting- hence why I haven't ever posted a journal. For some time, early on, I volunteered with another member to edit Jack's blog posts! We edited quite a few but they never made it onto the site as revised versions, except maybe one. Oprah I think. Anyway, editing the blog posts made me understand them so much more! But it was time consuming and burnt me out on the whole thing. So I kind of went away for a while.
I learned a valuable lesson. Going away for any amount of time was a big mistake. I missed a lot and now I feel like I can never catch up. I don't know where to start, other than back at the beginning and so I'm very overwhelmed. I've also learned a lot on my own health journey over this time as well, so I guess I should get into that now.
Anyway, here's my story.

I've always thought of myself as fat- probably since about age 9. I always had a pot belly, although my arms and legs were very thin, and at the time I thought that meant I was fat. I had a mother who was always on some diet or another, and a grandmother who was obese but lived on slimfast shakes and tried everything under the sun to lose weight- acupuncture, herbs, fad diets. My other grandmother who went to great lengths to keep her trim figure that way- she walked 4 miles a day, and then spent hours working out. She ate toast for breakfast, half a sandwich for lunch and a few bites of dinner. And it worked- she was thin and blonde and beautiful, driving around in her red convertible. People thought she was my grandfather's daughter, not his wife, and he beamed with pride when he told people that. He was the man I admired (and still do) most in this world, and his actions made a deep impression on me. The men in my family are all quite fat- very large hard bellies- but that didn't matter. Only the women's figures were important.

It was the most important thing in my world to be thin if you were to be accepted and worthy of your husband's attentions. That's what these women taught me every day. I believe they still feel that way. I don't really know.

I went to a very small private school (there were about 50 kids in the school, between grades K-12!) and I had a lot of friends there. One of the high school girls told me I had great legs. I must've been 6 or 7 years old, but I still remember that, and I remember her name to this day! I began to realize that bodies were something to be admired when they were nice. I loved living in that area- we had a huge backyard that was open to the other neighbors, and all the kids would play outside together as long as there was light- running, climbing, riding bikes. I would tan so dark that my mom accused me of wearing nylons once. I had to let her feel my leg to prove I wasn't lying. My grandparents all lived nearby and they all had large properties with lots of animals. I would spend my weekends and summers with them, running free on the acreage, riding the horses bareback, chasing the chickens and sheep, playing in puddles and ice during the rain or winter. It was really fun! I miss that time.
When I was in 4th grade I moved to a new city and a new school- a public school that was full of new kids and new teachers. And something called gym class. At the old school we'd just all go play outside, whatever game sounded fun and the big kids wanted to play. But here that was all regulated. Sure there was recess- I loved jumping rope and climbing on their version of parallel bars, but gym class was scary- organized sports like soccer and volleyball and basketball. And even worse- the fitness assessment. I remember military type guys coming in and having us do pushups and sit-ups and run laps around the gym for time- and the worst part of all, a public weigh in. It was humiliating for me, because unbeknownst to me I was the heaviest girl in my class. I think I was around 100 lbs. I was suddenly informed that I was fat. And everyone knew it.
I continued to gain weight through 4th and 5th grade, and was pretty chubby once I got to middle school. Age 11- I was starting to hit puberty and I was rapidly developing. So not only was I fat- I also had a 36D chest to boot. I got lots of attention from the boys- and all of the girls suddenly hated me from jealousy. I remember that when we'd play basketball in gym, they'd scratch me, or knock my glasses off my face on purpose. They'd tell me "get a bra" when I'd run. It was humiliating. So I learned to hide them- hunch my shoulders, wear big baggy clothes. Change in the bathroom stall instead of out in the locker room. And so I got bigger. By age 13 I was 5'6" and 180 lbs.

In high school I got a little bit of a fresh start. Several schools all fed into the high school so I got a fresh infusion of kids, who had no history with me. I decided that I really liked some of the boys and that to get their attention, I would have to get skinny. So I embarked on a get skinny plan. Work out 2 hours per day, and eat only an apple for lunch. That's it. I did that for 1 year and got down to 145 lbs. I was ecstatic! And guess, what? I got my guy. I was very happy. I maintained this through my junior year, but then my boyfriend who was a year ahead of me went to college, my parents had a lot of financial difficulties and then marriage issues which led to their divorce, and generally my life was a mess. I gained about 20 lbs and ended up at 165 by the time I got to college. Still this was technically a healthy weight for my 5'7" height, but I had ruined my metabolism and so I was really flabby. I met my now husband at work, we both ironically worked at the campus gym. He is from a South American family- and that culture likes to EAT. He taught me to gorge myself on all sorts of bad foods- rice with melted velveeta and fried chicken was one of our staples. I kept gaining weight and losing it and gaining it- but I never really got up past 180 lbs. After college I got a desk job (I'm an accountant) and this made it all harder. We love movies and would spend our days off watching movies all day, and then going out to eat at night.
Fast forward some years and we decided to have a child. I was 24 and I had been on the pill since about age 18. I stopped the pill, we waited a month to let it get out of my system, and then tried the next month for a baby. I got pregnant right away! I knew it by the way I felt, but oddly enough I could never get a positive pregnancy test. I was now 8 weeks late on my cycle but still didn't get a positive test. I went in for a blood test and yes I was pregnant, but the hormone levels were very low. That same day, I started bleeding. Sadder yet, that is the very day our best friend's son was born. I was very sad, the miscarriage was something that I had never thought would happen. No one in my family had ever had one before. But, I was young, and while overweight, seemingly healthy as far as I could tell. But the miscarriage had messed something up, and I ate my pain and was now 200 lbs. My cycles were not regular after that, and by 6 months we were still not pregnant, and I stopped cycling altogether. I had a million blood tests, a 3 hour fasting glucose test, all sorts of things but they never found anything. By the seventh month of this journey, the next three months I didn't have a cycle. My doctor put me on the pill to jolt my body back into some sort of cycle again, so I did the pill for one month, followed immediately by a round of 50 mg Clomid. The first round didn't work. Then another round. This time, it worked! I was pregnant!! I was so scared to be happy. The blood pregnancy tests showed that the baby was developing at a good rate, the hormones were cooperating. But about 8 weeks I woke up bleeding a lot of bright red blood. I rushed to the emergency room. Not again! I couldn't believe it. Why was this happening to me? I waited hours for the ultrasound tech to get there, to see if my baby was still in there. And as she moved around silently, looking for the baby, I saw it. The tiny beating heart was there. She couldn't say anything about what she was seeing, but I knew what it was. They could never say why I was bleeding, but I have a feeling that maybe I lost a twin. I guess I'll never know.
My pregnancy was amazing after that! I never felt more alive, I had so much energy and felt so alive. I tried to eat really well, but starting at 200 lbs was a problem for me. I gained 40 more lbs during my pregnancy. I had my gestational diabetes test and was fine, no diabetes for me! But I was really overweight.

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T07:36:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106692>

My daughter was born healthy, 8 lbs, 8 oz. Bigger than my family babies normally are, but my husband was a 10 lb baby so I was grateful she wasn't any bigger- although she had a 14 inch head! The delivery was hard, I pushed for a long time and had a lot of pain, she gave me a 2nd degree tear and the nerve damage lasted nearly 2 years after her birth. My epidural stopped working for some reason, and no one believed me until they started to stitch me up and I started really complaining. They realized I could feel everything, even though I shouldn't have been able to.
I still wonder why the epidural stopped working right when I needed it the most, but I have also been able to sober up instantly when I need to if I'm drinking, so I maybe have some sort of super power, who knows.
Anyway, I wanted to be a good role model for my new daughter, healthy for her. I cleaned up my diet and started counting calories and working anytime I could. I got back to 200 lbs by my 6 week follow up. I tried lots more diets- vegetarian, vegan, the zone, LA weight loss. A couple years later I started realizing that gluten bothered me and I started to cut it out. I got down to 170 lbs, I felt so much better and looked better too. I hadn't had any blood tests as I hated going to the doctor, but a new job had me take a blood panel to apply for life insurance. They denied me due to strange results on my liver test, and said I had fatty liver disease. I went for a follow up at the doctor and they re-tested me, and said my liver was fine but my cholesterol was really really low. They told me to start drinking wine and sent me on my way. This was round about 2009.
In my continued search to chase my elusive goal weight of 135, I found Dr. Davis online somehow through someone else's blog, which was followed by an episode of Dr. Oz featuring Gary Taubes. I devoured his book, and then I found Mark Sisson's book Primal Blueprint at the library and it just made a lot of sense to me. And I thought it didn't hurt to try it. I also hired a personal trainer. I was determined to get to my goal weight hell or high water. I dabbled with the primal blueprint but someone introduced me to medifast so I got sidetracked diet wise and began doing that along with the very intense workouts with the trainer, although I was reading every discussion on the MDA boards I could get my hands on. The trainer convinced me to start training for a half marathon, so I also started running (he was an ultramarathoner). I trained and ran my half marathon, and I got down to 159 lbs. It was the lowest I'd weighed since age 14. I was very happy and got lots of compliments from people about how great I looked, and I felt like for the first time I was worthy of being around other people. Unfortunately, my sex drive disappeared, my hair started falling out and I was just kind of angry all the time. I began to hate my husband for no real reason, nothing he could do would make me happy with him. Our daughter also had caused a lot of stress as she was a very difficult baby (now I know that she had tongue tie but didn't know it at that time) and a difficult toddler, and she's proving to be a difficult child. Sleep and food have always been issues for her. She never wants to eat and she never wants to sleep. I think I say two things to her more than anything else- Eat! and go to sleep! So sleep deprivation was a huge part of our lives for nearly 5 years. At around age 5 she finally started having good bedtime habits.
Suddenly my stalking the message boards on MDA finally sparked my attention when Dr. Kruse appeared and began talking about leptin. Then someone stepped forward to be the first to try the leptin reset, and I though, what the hell. So I started too, about 2 weeks after her. I learned about the signs of leptin sensitivity and I thought maybe I was feeling like I had some of them after a couple of months. I was sweating for the first time while working out. I had a sex drive again and felt better. I had lost more fat and had lots of energy. I started working out, and did zumba 2X a week. My trainer had since moved away and I didn't replace him, realizing through Mark Sisson that he was promoting chronic cardio!
I was 155 lbs and feeling on top of the world. My diet was effortless. My weight was stable. I was a size 4! My husband lost his job so I picked up 2 more jobs on top of my 50+ hour per week job. I had boundless energy. This lasted for a long time. I was finally feeling free of my weight, and food. I wasn't binge eating, craving, starving, crashing. I felt like I fit in with the fit crowd, I felt like I belonged when I walked into the gym.
Then. I had a car accident in Feb 12. It wasn't a bad one- a woman T-boned me when she ran a red light. Neither of us was going fast but it did twist my spine pretty good and I had to stop doing any and all exercise until I could repair the support around my spine. I had to ice it every day. It was fine, forced CT! I was doing fine, I gained a little bit of weight but going from high activity to low can do that. I wasn't worried, it was only about 5 lbs and I was still maintaining through about June of that year, despite hardly any movement and many trips to Germany ( I travel there for work quite frequently, and it's a solid week of eating out and drinking LOTS of wine!).

Around about the end of June 2012 I was released for exercise, and I got a trainer that specializes in rehabilitation, natural movement and Paleo-ish. He started very slowly as my body was a mess, mostly working on very light cardio for warm-ups and mobility exercises, with a little strength thrown in. He has been a very good resource for helping me recover I slowly gained confidence and strength again.
I started seeing an Acupuncturist/Chinese Medicine (kinesiology) doctor, mostly to work on my wrist because I thought I had chronic tendonitis in there (turns out I actually had a Ganglion). He put me on several herbs to get rid of heavy metals and started working on other issues that popped up- my pituitary was very weak and after working on that to completion, my hormones were finally normal again. My parotid needed a lot of support, as well as my thyroid- despite the normal thyroid tests I’d been receiving from my blood work. My body seems to think I have Hashimoto’s when he tests me, even though my blood doesn’t show it. I had to stop seeing him because of cost and time commitment.
Then Mid October 2012 I got very bad Bronchitis- the doctor said it was likely to have become Pneumonia if I had waited much longer- a round of antibiotics and lots of rest, and I thought it would be done- but no. Apparently this bout of Bronchitis brought on another, much worse issue- Mono. I had no idea I had mono, and so I kept pushing- working long hours, working out at the gym very hard, although I felt like I was dying and couldn’t retain any information or hold a conversation most of the time. I had to start living on carbs to stay awake- sugar, coffee, gluten free pastries and bread- anything to help keep me alert at work. I also caught every single stinking illness that had come through- including a cold that I think I caught from my cat.
Back to my naturopath I went, and many many vials of blood later, she discovered the mono and put me on complete activity restriction. Become a couch potato, she said. So I did. Of course, this was just in time for the holidays and the abundance of treats my now carb-craving self needed to function. And so I ended 2012 in an exhausted heap.

Well that's enough for today, I'll work on 2013 tomorrow!

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T20:41:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106780>

Thanks for the warm welcome! It's nice to be getting back into the community again  Yes I'm wordy- and about to be significantly more wordy.  But I need to tell the whole story, or else something important may be missed.

OK, now on to 2013! After getting the mono and having lots of labs done in Dec 12, I knew some things about my health. I was confirmed as having Celiac disease, which I had suspected for some time and explained my appearance as a small child- skinny with a big belly. My poor little self was inflamed . I also was confirmed for the mono, but had basically normalish looking labs overall. I then had a consult with Dr. Kruse! It was like talking with a celebrity, I have no idea if I came across as a frazzled ditz or the intelligent professional that I am, but it was so cool! Dr. Kruse looked over my labs but found my family's health history more interesting. My dad had both a stroke and a heart attack before age 50. It's a miracle he's alive, the ER said they don't see people survive the type of heart attack he had very often. His dad has type 2 diabetes and had open heart surgery in his 50's also. My mom has suffered from depression and Bipolar disorder, and so does her dad and my sister. My homocysteine was also suspicious to him, so he referred me to Dr. Tim Jackson. Most of you know who he is now, but back then no one had really heard of him. He was connected to a possible diagnosis that Dr. Kruse had for me- MTHFR.

Dr. Jackson immediately asked me to take the 23 and me, he mentioned that my Thyroid was not optimal, that I probably had some gut issues and so asked me to take the Metametrix GI effects profile. H also had me switch to some more active forms of vitamin A, C and D, transdermal magnesium (ancient minerals) as well as stopping anything with folic acid in it immediately. He wanted me to reduce any sulfur containing foods (eggs, meat, garlic, onions, broccoli, cabbage, Brussels sprouts- basically the only foods that I ever ate!), and add ox bile and HCL Betaine.
I stopped all supplements for a few days before taking the GI test, took that and took the 23 and me test and started the long wait for results. The GI test I had to do 2 times. For some reason my first sample was so awful that it broke their test. They told me that whatever meds I was on made the results unusable, and that they'd never seen such a thing before. I wasn't on ANYTHING! Not even a single vitamin! I was really worried about what was wrong with me. I re-did the test and apparently my system needed some more time to clear up, it came back this time.
My Metametrix results were not very exciting- my good bacteria was low, but not awful. My bad bacteria wasn't out of control, but my short chain fatty acids were out of whack, my lactoferrin was very high and my triglicerides were really high. I also had many vegetable fibers. I basically was not absorbing anything from my food. I was fat yet malnourished! I was working on bringing up my HCL betaine and finally topped out at 8 pills, and began slowly working my way down. The Ox bile helped me massively. I finally was regular (from 1X a week to 1X a day!) and I improved on the Bristol scale to normal. I was full after eating just a little bit of food! It was amazing. But I wasn't losing any weight, despite eating less. I wasn't working out at all during this time, still feeling very exhausted and sleeping very poorly.

I received my 23 and me results and promptly sent them over to Sterling Hill at MTHFR support to analyze. She came back with a clear strong message- You are very messed up.
I basically had either hetero or homozygous results on most of the bad things I could possibly have. I have BOTH celiac genes! The one most celiacs have (DQA2), and the one that only the worst of Celiacs can have (DQA1). I'm Homozygous on the first one, and heterozygous on the second. She told me that if I was homozygous on the second, I wouldn't have even been born, it's that serious. And surprise surprise, I'm homozygous MTHFR- C677T. Sigh. My other mutations? CYP1B1, CYP2C19\*17, CYP2D6 S486T, CYP2E1\*1B G9896C, NAT1, NAT2, CTH, RARA, HLA,FCER1A, GSTM3, TRAF1, IRF5, IGF1R, MTC03P1, almost every single clotting factor mutuation, CBS, COMT, DAO, GAD1, BHMT, AGT, ACE, ADD1, ACAT1, MAO, MTHFD1 and D1L, MTR, MTRR, NOS 1 2 and 3, PEMT, TCN 1 and 2, TYMS, VDR, Thyroid issues- FOXE1 and CTLA4, APOE, ATG16L1, GSDMB, and IL5.
What does all of that mean? I have lots of immune issues, Celiac, MTHFR, inability to make, convert or store Vitamins, especially D and B12. And oh by the way my MTRR depletes the B12 as well. I have high risk for Asthma, Graves Disease, Alzheimers, Lupus, RA & Hashis. I have high risk of birth defects in my babies like cleft palate, neural tube defects, and lo and behold- tongue tie! I can easily get blood clots and have serious consequences from them- my risk of Deep Vein Thrombosis is high. I don't detox well, I don't convert hormones well from one form to another. I have a tendency to store metals and accumulate bad estrogens and high levels of other hormones get stuck along the conversion pathways. I have a risk of depression, schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. Basically? I don't work very well!

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T20:41:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106781>

Tim put me on more supplements- Here was my list!
HCL Betaine
Ox Bile
Vitamin D
Molybdenum
Cal-Mag Butyrate
Calcium D-Glucarate
GI Barrier Repair
Seacure
Sunflower Choline
Acid to Alkaline
Methyl B 12 and Hydroxyl B12
Fish Oil
Co Q 10

None of this seemed to help, and my fatigue got worse. I actually began to get alternatively angry and weepy. My stool also began to smell unbearably of ammonia. And then I began a many month long bizarre problem. I would sleep all night, falling asleep easily and not waking up until my alarm- however I was in lucid dreams ALL night. I could feel the hours ticking by. Nights seemed to last forever. And I was waking up more tired than before. I had to stop the methylfolate and Molybdenum and that went away. This really confuses me- if I'm MTHFR and I need the methylfolate, why does the lowest dose make me crazy? I don't have wifi on at night, sleep in complete dark with no electronics in my room, so it wasn't blatant EMF. It was odd.

During this time, I posted a cry for help on these message boards- some of you may have seen. My 13 year old sister was diagnosed with a very rare form of brain cancer- GBM- Glioblastoma Multiforme. Deadly brain cancer, incredibly rare in children. She had no leading symptoms that seemed out of the ordinary, although looking back they were clear signs. Decreasing vision acuity- although our family all needs glasses so it wasn't weird to us. She got sick a few times, throwing up- but what kid doesn't? She never had headaches, although she passed out a time or two- my mom thought maybe she was just dehydrated, she always was just fine afterward. Suddenly the weekend of April 27 she just kept throwing up, and got really weak, my mom thought she was dehydrated. They took her to the small hospital in their rural town and the doctors ran every test they had when they couldn't figure out what was wrong with her. The last thing they had available was a CT scan. They ran it and there it was- a 3 inch mass in the center of her brain, right at the top of the brain stem. She also had massive swelling in her head. She began to lose her memory- didn't even know her own name, and then she couldn't speak at all. They called life flight and rushed her to Doernbecher Children's hospital in Portland Oregon. They gave her emergency surgery- but could only get about 50% of the tumor, it was in such a sensitive place and had spread throughout the tissue so thoroughly, it was impossible to do more without killing her. She had a drain put in her brain which helped keep the fluid down. She didn't wake up for days. They accidentally pulled the drain out one day during physical therapy, but they didn't re-insert it so we thought maybe she would get better- we didn't know what kind of cancer it was just yet.
When they finally got around to telling us the type, she had been awake (still unable to speak or eat, she had a feeding tube full of the soy and corn syrup death in a bag they feed cancer patients) and learning how to move again- she began walking, and gaining strength each day. And so when they told us GBM we thought she was going to be one of the very precious few that survives. She was so young! She was strong! She was determined! But about a week later- disaster. They weren't paying attention to the brain fluid. The pressure in her brain built up so much that she had a seizure and then a stroke. She lost movement on her left side. You could tell looking in her eyes, that she gave up after that. She didn't want to cooperate in her therapy. She didn't want to be here anymore.
We took her home- the hospital said her cancer was growing rapidly and they felt there was nothing they could do- radiation and chemo often does not help this type of cancer, but does make the patient weak. They thought she had about 2 weeks to live. We took her home and taught my mom how to make the best epi-paleo nutrition you could fit in a feeding tube. Sardines, Kale, Spinach, all sorts of veggies, coconut oil. Bone broth, turmeric, coQ10- every bit of information I could find on Dr. Kruse's website that could help. She immediately began to detox, and after a week or so, she had beautiful glowing skin, shiny hair. She was rapidly losing weight but it's hard to fit enough calories through a tiny tube. My mom had to feed her 9 times a day just to get her enough calories. Too much coconut oil at a time and she'd be cleaning up a big mess, it just ran right through her due to lack of any solid substance to help it digest slowly. But you could tell, my sister was going downhill. She began losing motor function at all, and she just had completely given up, and let the aggressive cancer take her. 11 weeks after her diagnosis, 6 weeks longer than they told us she'd live, she died on July 8, 2013. My other sister's birthday, and the day after my daughter's birthday.
We were all devastated, but felt like we really did everything we could. And I believe that if she had chosen to fight, she'd have lived a lot longer. I worried a lot about my mom- suddenly an empty nester, in a house full of memories- walking past the spot she died, every single day. Remember- she's bipolar. She's attempted suicide before. I still worry about her every single day. All of that stress made me more exhausted if that was even possible, and I began to break out a lot- horrible painful cystic acne. I've never had acne before in my life! And I gained weight rapidly- all the way up to the current weight I've held now since the end of the summer- 196 lbs. All of my hard work for so many years- gone.
I decided I needed to find a local doctor to help me with my MTHFR and found one, she was helpful a bit, but asked for some more tests. At that time I had Kaiser insurance so I had to work through them to get tested, but she suspected that I had PCOS, despite never having blood tests to support that. I found a PCOS 'specialist' to help me and he ran a thorough battery of tests on me, including the ultrasound. Sure enough, my blood tests didn't really support the PCOS, however the ultrasound showed the pearl sign very clearly. There were the rows of tiny cysts, all lined up. He said that I must have a very mild case, and prescribed me metformin and spironolactone. They normally include hormones but with the MTHFR and the blood clotting risks, I can't take any hormones.
I took all of this back to the naturopath who added Estrosense, and Maca. I was now up to 17 supplements, handfuls of pills a day. And I wasn't getting any better, I was still exhausted. The Metformin made me incredibly nauseous if I took it for more than 3 days. I would take it two days, take 2 days off and then take it 2 days again. If I didn't do that, I would be throwing up.

I then heard the radio show on MTHFR blogtalk radio discussing the Zyto Scan. I was intrigued and so I arranged to take the scan. Wow. Best investment ever! She found all sorts of things that I had wrong- all revolving around something I had never even considered, living in the Pacific northwest. Lyme disease. She felt that a lot of my other diagnoses were actually complications of lyme and that if I worked on that, then I would resolve some things. She also found more thyroid weakness (still despite "normal" tests) and so told me to try some thyroid support, and some adrenal support. She found lots of heavy metals, but oddly enough I also needed a heavy metal called Germanium. I added Monolaurin, and a supplement to dissolve biofilms, and a thyroid support called Thyrochord- which also includes Germanium. I began the Monolaurin and then the biofilm medicine first, and I didn't notice any drastic difference, but began getting sick less often. Then I added the Thyrochord and BAM! I started sleeping! I had energy! I felt like a million bucks!!
I joined the gym again and began doing very heavy lifting, and gained muscle rapidly. My lean body mass is now up to 140 lbs! I'm still 196 but that's a great thing to know. I just hit a deadlift PR of 225, I'm now a strong girl!
Around mid December I went on a trip to Florida to visit some family and go to Disney world- and I decided not to take any of my supplements with me. I walked probably 8 miles a day, out in the sun, for a week. It was so much fun! We ate what we wanted (gluten free of course- my little girl has celiac too) and did what we wanted. We slept a lot and just had a lot of relaxing fun. I felt even better than before. And when I got home I didn't start up any of my supplements again. I still feel good! Although I brought my "eat whatever I want" attitude home with me, and we've been eating terribly, mainly living on Mexican food. I am working a lot of hours and starting up my own business on top of that, so I don't have any time to cook, and my husband refuses to help me, unless it involves a box that you just add water to.
I have tried and tried to re-start the leptin reset, and get back on track, but each time I do I make it until about mid afternoon and binge eat anything I can get my hands on. I also am having a hard time eating meat. I've never really liked it, and so forcing myself to eat large amounts of it again is making me crazy.

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T20:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106782>

At the beginning of January I went back to my normal naturopath and took her everything that had happened. She re-ran my thyroid tests and again they look fine. She also has requested a new GI test, and finally I got someone to check my fibrinogen. I took that test yesterday.
(I'll post all of my labs separately, keeping this just to narrative ). An odd development though- my daughter had been complaining that her stomach hurt all the time, so we took her in during October, and they gave her a urine test- it showed blood and protein in the urine although no infection, they sent us on our way. She got a tummy bug in December and the pain was really bad again, so we took her in and again, blood and protein in the urine, but no infection. My doctor just ran a urine test on me just for kicks- and what did they find but blood and protein in the urine- no infection. It's very odd that she and I are showing this same symptom. I still don't know what to think about that- she's having me take an herbal urine support for 10 days and then we'll re-test. My daughter has an appointment with her pediatric naturopath next week so we can explore her more.

Anyway, here's where I'm at today!
I'm overwhelmed with all of the diagnoses, I don't know what to believe I really have, or what to do about it.
I'm only taking the urine support for the next 10 days, and the monolaurin sporadically (mostly to prevent the flu). I sleep pretty well, I eat poorly right now, sugar, carbs, whatever I want. I know this is really bad, but see: overwhelmed. I want to learn everything I need to about how this all fits together but I'm struggling to find the right resource, and Dr. Kruse doesn't do consults anymore.
I'm about 28 lbs overweight at 196 if I use my lean body mass of 140 and add 18-20% fat. That puts my goal in the mid 160 range. Higher than before but definitely much more muscular.
And I guess I'm just looking for support and a little guidance! I have my sister who is working on being an ancestral health nutritionist and is a fantastic Dr. Kruse success story, but she's a little lost when approaching my entire disaster of a picture. And I have my naturopath who I love, but she doesn't have any patients quite like me- or at least know that they're like me!
My hair is shiny, and my hair and nails grow fast. My nails are strong. My hair is slowly growing back at the temples where it fell out, my acne is now mostly gone. I am not exhausted, I don't feel like I'm in a big fog but my memory isn't what it was and I do have a hard time concentrating- trying to read one of Dr. Kruse's blogs or listen to a webinar is difficult. I'm like- the xyz and the qrs equal the- Ooh look squirrel!
That's not like me. Other than that, I actually feel pretty good.
Now, On to labs!

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-23T20:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106783>

I love squirrels! they're so good at distracting us!!! Oh Look Hawk catching squirrel...even better!

I would go back to the basics. I know I am considering all the craziness I'm currently dealing with. so, what are the basics? Leptin Reset, Circadian cycles, Epi-paleo RX, EPCOT protocol, CT, grounding, and sunlight!

so first get back on the Leptin Reset (eat as much seafood as you can). 1) BAB w/in 30min of waking 2) Lunch 4hrs after BAB, 3) Dinner 4hrs before you want to go to bed. 4) no working out 5)

2nd Circadian cycles 1) look at the sun upon rising 2) get as much natural light as possible 3) avoid artificial light at night (wear your blue blockers) 4) if you need to use artificial light use redlights and wear your blue blockers 5) sleep in a super dark cave!

Once you get all of this dialed in and it becomes a habit. add in grounding and CT, then start applying Jack's protocols. Its ok to start small and add on. we'll help you out!

ask questions and read, read, read.

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T20:55:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106784>

Oops, the whole center part went missing in trying to post all of this. I will now have to remember what I wrote and maybe paraphrase a lot .
After the 23 and me Dr. Jackson told me to add a bunch of supplements:
Calcium D-Glucarate, Methylfolate, Methylcobalmin, Hydroxylcobalmin, GI barrier Repair, Seacure, Molybdenum, Vitamin D, A and C, and CoQ10.
I visited another naturopath who added estrosense.
I felt terrible on all of these, not sleeping well, alternatively angry and weepy. I began sleeping all night in a lucid dream state, waking up more exhausted than ever. I thought I had adrenal fatigue.
I went to another naturopath who thought I had PCOS- and so she sent me to get tested. All of my hormones seem to not point at it- other than my insulin which is suddenly out of whack. Within 2 months my FBG went from the mid 80's where it had been for years to 115! For no apparent reason! And sure enough on the ultrasound- there was the pearl sign. The OB/GYN prescribed Metformin and Spironolactone. The metformin makes me very sick. I can't take it more than 1 pill for 2 days without having to stop and let it leave my system for a few days. I stopped taking it completely a couple months ago- I just can't handle being sick all the time, with a full time + job and a small child.

I then had a zyto scan- this was the turning point- she found Lyme disease, and thyroid issues. I also had lots of heavy metals and needed germanium. I began taking Monolaurin, Bio Fibrin to break down biofilms, and Thyrochord for the germanium and thyroid support- these things made me feel amazing! I felt on top of the world again. It was a miracle. I stopped everything else, and have basically been that way since about November.

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-23T21:04:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106786>

Hi Shijin-
Well, part of my issue is this- I can't eat seafood, or even be around it. I've tried and tried and tried. I have gagged it down (the mildest fish) fresh as possible and smothered in so much spice that you can't taste anything but the spice (oh but I can still taste it). I then feel sick afterward for hours. It's not just the taste, even more so it's the texture and the smell. And my body seems to reject it on top of it all. Dr Kruse told me to find one that I could tolerate, and I can't seem to find anything. I can't do bone broth either, it makes me so nauseous, I can't stand the smell and after gagging it down, I want to throw up. I've always done the BAB, I get through that no problem. I have no wifi on at night, sleep in a dark room with a sleep mask, white noise machine on, and that's the only electronic thing other than my simple battery powered alarm clock.
One problem I have is the blue blockers at night- I'm a photographer on top of my 50 hour a week normal job. That means that I'm working in photoshop at night, after work and dinner. I work from about 7:30 to 9:30 at night, nearly every night, and nearly all day on the weekends if I'm not shooting. I have to see the colors in true form, so that I don't end up with blue photos. I don't know what I can do about this. I can't edit photos during the day, when I'm at my real job. I don't want to close my business either.

I am trying to read and listen to all that I can right now! But it's a lot

### Hope — 2014-01-23T22:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106793>

Hi Embyr42,

first, wow. This is amazing stuff, all of it. I am just starting to learn about MTHFR. Were you able to listen to the webinar on Monday? It is amazing like all of them of course. It sounds like you are doing a ton and seeking all kinds of awesome medical advice.

I do wonder, with your history...are you sure you can't afford to take a break from working so hard, outside of your job? Or perhaps, you can't afford not to take a break to help yourself heal? I love - of course - what Gretchen said.....and keep it simple. I have had many of the things you do/did, except I have never had a baby, or been pregnant. But I can identify with a lot of your issues. And it's hard. You are still so young and have a precious little girl, what you do now has such consequences for you and her too...

Has Dr Jackson talked to you about mold? Were you ever exposed? Just wondering.

I think Dr K will talk more about the redox potential....and one thing that stuck with me from Mondays webinar is if your redox potential is bad, or low, you can't handle any stressors very well. It's also why you accumulate metals, can't detox much of anything. I have that same problem.....and I think many of us here do. I'm so glad you started to share here. I haven't been around nearly as long as you but this is the most amazing place on the net.

Oh - and I would love to hear what job brings you to Germany, if you are at liberty to share. If you still go there a lot, have you caught the tips Dr K has given for traveling? Grounding on the plane, prepping etc? Not that I want to add to your to-do list, but if you go a lot. I go about once a year (I happen to be German) and I am always feeling yucky on the plane and for a day or two after. Once there I feel much better. I can't imagine going a lot...though it'd be a dream. I'm crazy I know, I just do miss the place.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-106880>

I started to listen to the webinar recording, but it takes me a long time to listen to them, I have to keep pressing replay to keep up with note taking. I got probably 20 minutes through it.
I am working hard on starting my business, and if I back off now I may as well give up on it, it's just the wrong time for it. I do have plenty of time with my daughter- I only work on it when she's asleep- she goes to bed at 7 PM (we wake up at 5 AM for work and to take her to school).
My job is my career- and I try hard to keep it only to 50 hours per week, although my boss would prefer if I worked 60.
Dr. Jackson did ask about mold. I live in Oregon in the Willamette Valley, so it's damp here a lot- I have no idea if I lived in a moldy house or not. I don't really seem to feel the vibe of the mold problems, they don't really resonate with me, and once I got my labs back, he didn't mention it again. So I don't know what to think about mold issues...

My day job is that of a CPA for a public corporation, I run the accounting department. Our major factory is about 30 minutes outside of Dresden, Germany. We go frequently when we have large projects, and about once per year otherwise, just to check on the operations and accounting staff over there. I love it there, Dresden is very beautiful! And the food is fantastic, and doesn't make me sick . Dr. Kruse did talk to me about the plane issues when I had my consult with him. I don't work on the plane, I try to sleep for the most part. My trips are usually very short, and they mess me up really badly. We usually leave here on Saturday, land Sunday. We have Monday through Wednesday in the office and then Thursday we come home. While we are there we are out late every night and wake up early every morning I feel like hell for about a week afterward, due to all of the drinking (which I don't do at all at home) and the complete lack of sleep. But it's the expected way when we're over there.
I love it there but the short trips make it kind of a chore.

She does the consultation for $50 and it's quite extensive, going over what each of the genes mean. I took notes like a mad fiend but couldn't even capture half of what she was telling me- she does talk really fast. I tried to record it but had technical difficulties.
I haven't talked to Dr. Tim since he kind of got "famous" on here- I'm sure he's pretty busy now that he's got a lot of consults. I was also frustrated by trying to do this remotely, I need to have someone in front of me, I have a hard time following conversations on the phone or Skype. I hate talking on the phone to no end. He's also quite an investment.

I haven't done CT- I'm so freezing all the time I'm having a hard time bringing myself to get into freezing cold water, when my hands and feet and butt are already ice cubes. The funny thing is that although I'm freezing and my extremities are like ice, my husband says I'm like a furnace and he can't get near me. I apparently give off a lot of heat.

It'll be nice to be in the 160's- although my goal has always been 135. It wasn't until I realized my lean body mass was 140 that I had the epiphany that I'd have to lose muscles, organs or bones to get that low. My healthy weight range for my height is 135-159, so a goal of 168 seems odd to me, although technically it's a healthy body fat %.
I'm interested to hear more about your journey too!

and
Betaine hydrochloride HCL (digestion)

### Embyr42 — 2014-02-05T20:38:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-108515>

So it's been a bit since I've been back, Work has been crazy.

I'm now on day 3 of the leptin reset... ish. I'm finishing up all of my "paleo" food this week, so I made as much of it into a stew that I could, the potatoes, yucca and plantains I made into Colombian Sancocho (my husband is Colombian). So that should about finish it up. But I've had no sugar, and the BAB each day. I'm feeling myself start the food obsession process begin. When I'm on the reset, all I think about is food. I'm not hungry- it's just the deprivation aspect of it. It's incredibly difficult. It gets steadily worse and by about week 3 I can't take it anymore, that's when I fall off. So we'll see how I'm doing at week 3.

An interesting thing happened this week. I had and epiphany!
I keep blaming a lot of my health problems on the time when I got into the car accident in Feb 12. What I didn't make the connection with, until the latest blog post (was it Monday), is that I sit on the floor above a semiconductor fab. There are all manners of giant machines down there. They do incredible things like turn precious metals into microscopic particles that are then magnetically attracted to a substrate to make the tiny wires. This is massive non-native EMF! And when did I move into this building? Sept 2011. About 5 months before I really noticed my health declining! Further- almost everyone who works here now is sick all the time since we've moved into the building. More colds, Asthma flare ups, chronic migraines, broken bones, etc. etc. etc. And it's most apparent to people who just start working here who comment that they've never been sick like this before. And everyone notices that after being off for a week, we feel massively better. Better than you realistically should from just a little R&R. We actually went through extensive air quality testing because of it- but the air is fine. It's the EMF. My Aha moment.
SO I bought a grounding mat for my desk, and I'm working on getting the magnetico for my home to help recover overnight (my EMF status at home is quite good actually).
But interestingly enough, after using it for a couple of hours, I feel like I have the flu and my teeth hurt. Does anyone know if that's normal? I'm assuming it means I'm really really bad off.... I'm taking breaks from it throughout the day to help.

### Embyr42 — 2014-02-07T18:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-108899>

I am able to work from home (or anywhere in the world for that matter), but I'm not ALLOWED to work from home. Company policy is that you are in the office at all times when working, unless traveling or ill (for when you're OK to work but just don't want to spread the love). If you must work from home for some reason other than that, then you need special approval from management and HR- each day you are working from home, you have to be re-approved.
Management is probably sick too, but we cannot move to another building, it would cost us tens of millions of dollars and 2 or 3 years time to move our operations- not that it would do any good because if we did, we'd just bring the problem with us. It's what we do. The semiconductor fab with all of the EMF is ours, it's our product. We're also required to be confined to specific types of industrial areas because of what we do, so there are very limited choices as to locations in our metro area even available to us. We just moved into the building because we consolidated 2 facilities. Our other facility was simply an assembly facility of one of our other product lines. We had to consolidate because we moved that manufacturing over to Germany, so we had to move into the fab building. I'm a CPA for the company, so administration can fit anywhere they make us fit.
The building is like you describe sooperb- it was built in the late 70's, some windows but small and high up. There are days when I get to work in the dark, and leave in the dark. I have no idea if it was raining or sunny or warm or cold outside!

The big problem for me about just getting a new job (which I know will be the suggestion) is that I LOVE this company, I love the people, I love what I do and the opportunities I have here, I just can't find anywhere else. Plus they pay me a lot more than I'd make in another company doing an equivalent type of job. So there is zero chance of me leaving.

I'm trying to read all of the threads about magnets, to see if any of that can help.

I will ask for that one- I have signing authority for that much money, I'll just sign off on the expense
My D is low, and despite taking lots of supplementation, (26 at my last test!). I'm looking for a non-magnetic tanning bed close enough to get to regularly. I haven't had time to read much about the red LED thing mentioned a few posts ago, but I may do that too once I understand the concept.
HS CRP is 0.7 but that test is about a year old now, so I don't know what it is today, I need an update. DHEA was 357. It's been in that general range for three years now.

My Genova stool test came back. I had no inflammation, no yeast, no parasites or amoeba, no C-diff, h Pylori, etc. All clean, no blood or mucous. Metabolic markers looked good What she did discover is that my gallbladder and pancreas are barely functioning, and I'm not digesting anything. They found whole seeds and nuts, meat fibers (she said she's never seen that before) and lots of vegetable fibers. My fats are not digesting at all either. She told me that I'm effectively starving to death cellularly, and gaining weight at a rapid pace because likely my body thinks it needs to pack on any calorie it can get. YAY me.
I gained 13 lbs in the last week, doing the leptin reset diet. I'm now at 209 lbs . I'm so frustrated- everyone around me is thinking I'm a lazy gluttonous sloth I'm sure.

Also- I have almost no good bugs (none of a couple!) and I have two bad bugs (Klyuvera ascorbata and Enterobacter cloacae) with very high levels (off the chart!). The Klyuvera one she's never seen before and had never even heard of! She's an instructor at the naturopathic college of medicine, it's pretty alarming to hear her say something like that!

SO- she has put me on a ton of stuff to kill the bad bugs. They came back resistant to almost every known antibiotic, except cipro- which I won't take. So she has me on several natural things that it did test susceptible to. And she gave me some specific things to help out my gall bladder and pancreas. So in 3 months I'll test again and see what has happened. If it's not working, she told me I'll have to do the antibiotics.

I feel like I'm really being asked to choose- being healthy, or a job I love. I know without a job I love, no matter how healthy I am I will be so unhappy. And I've had a lot of jobs in my field- It's not going to get much better than this, work wise. I feel very overwhelmed by the thought of giving up my job (which means giving up my house and my goals and dreams) to be physically healthy- mental health is important too!

### sooperb — 2014-02-14T20:10:55+0200

Oh embyr I sympathise a shed load, 13 lbs! Dr. Jack has done a blog on the gallbladder and how not having one affects your digestion quite a lot. ### Embyr42 — 2014-02-19T18:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-111196>

I love the support on here  All I get at home is "you're broken" or "your body is weird" or "I just wish you were fixed already so that you could stop talking about/spending money on your health" from my husband. He eats the same way I do (although a lot more volume!) and is in the best shape of his life, so he's perplexed as to why this is all happening to me.

The digestion does seem to be my nemesis. I was taking things that Dr. Jackson had me but when I stopped taking everything completely back in December, I finally started to feel better so I didn't start anything up again.
This naturopath put me on different things. I'm on something called Panplex which is a bunch of digestive enzymes, (ox bile, pepsin, hcl, all sorts of stuff) and something else for my gall bladder (Beta TCP) and then I'm on two things to kill the bugs. Berberine and then another thing with some other blend in it called Proent (oregano and stuff like that). I'm supposed to take a probiotic which I haven't yet had a chance to buy, and I'm adding trace minerals to my water a couple times a day, just to help myself get some of that in, in case I might absorb a little bit of it.

I'd love to take a 1 or 2 month sabbatical, but my company probably won't support that, especially in the job I'm in. But I'm really bad at time off- I pack it so full that I actually usually view going back to work as rest! I plan activities every second of my time off. I am not a "sit and read a book" type, I have to be go go go go go every second. My weekends are packed to the brim with stuff to do- partly because of working so many hours, but also because I can't stand to sit still with nothing going on. Last summer we had a day planned at the beach- after sitting and looking at the ocean for about 10 minutes I was ready to leave and do something.

What brings me joy? Well, my job, my daughter, my photography. I like spending time with friends but I really only have one, and there's only so much time you can spend with one person who you're not married to! We do see each other at least 1 or 2 times a week.

I'm so behind on webinars, they take me hours to listen to, so the thought of sitting still for hours after sitting all day at work is keeping me away. The same with reading the blogs- the time commitment looms so large, I can't get started.

I feel like all I do is make excuses but I feel unable to make any changes either. I feel very trapped right now by my life!

The worst part is the food wasting habit I've somehow developed trying to make myself do the leptin reset this time around. I'm making entire meals, looking at it, and then I'm unable to make myself eat it. So I either throw it away and go hungry, or I leave it sitting there on the counter and run to the nearest Mexican restaurant. Or I scrounge some random snack foods together into a meal- one day I ate a jar of pickles instead of the nice corned beef I made (I LOVE corned beef!). I ended up throwing away the corned beef because it sat out too long and no one ate any of it. I had my husband grill some meat, then I ended up just eating about 4 of the Brussels sprouts I had made and the meat sat there untouched, until it got dried out in the fridge and we threw it away. I don't know what is going on with me mentally about food! But it's costing me a fortune, and I don't know what to do about it... The weird thing is that if a friend or a restaurant cooks it, I have no problem eating it. If I or my husband cooks it, I can't make myself eat it. It's very weird. I almost panic at the thought of eating what I make, there's a lot of anxiety happening.

So it turns out the weird bug that I have, that the Naturopath had never seen before? That's taken over my digestive system? Well, it is potentially pathogenic, it has caused deaths and blood infections and things before- my wonderful sister found me information about it. And the kicker? I most likely got it from drinking Kombucha! I love Kombucha, but apparently my habit might have killed me! I guess it's a common strain in the Kombucha. Portland is a Kombucha heaven, we have so many little breweries, they are so awesome. But apparently dangerous for me.
Unfortunately, it can show up as unexplained protein in urine, with no infection. I showed with that symptom on my urinalysis, but so did my daughter . So I need a GI test for her to confirm but I think she may also have this bad bug as well based on that unexplained test. She loves Kombucha more than I do. Poor thing- no gluten, corn, dairy, rice, beans, and now no Kombucha. She feels like she's not allowed to eat anything- it's hard for a 6 year old to understand. Especially when she sees what her friends eat!
I'm not sure if I can get her to swallow all of these pills that I'm taking, some of them taste really horrible, they make me gag.

### Hope — 2014-02-19T19:49:21+0200

I hear you on the Kombucha. I am a recovering K addict. Though I had no problem once I heard Dr K said he doesn't drink it in winter.

You have much going on. You sound like I used to be...until my body said, enough. no more. it forced me to take a break, make hard sacrifices. I still am. I want to encourage you to be gentle on yourself...which I need also. I see myself a lot in you.

How many employees does your firm have? If > 50, you can apply for FMLA and your job should be protected. If not...you have to look hard at your life and see what you can do to allow your body to heal. What you have done till now has gotten you where you are. Einstein said if you keep it up, don't expect different results. It's not just a food story but a life story...our healing, I think.

You said,
But I'm really bad at time off- I pack it so full that I actually usually view going back to work as rest! I plan activities every second of my time off. I am not a "sit and read a book" type, I have to be go go go go go every second. My weekends are packed to the brim with stuff to do- partly because of working so many hours, but also because I can't stand to sit still with nothing going on. Last summer we had a day planned at the beach- after sitting and looking at the ocean for about 10 minutes I was ready to leave and do something.

Yeah, I get it. I was like that for years before my crash. And I can happily say you can change. I did. I was forced, it was hard, but I am doing it little by little. Of course my n=1 is different but sometimes we force ourselves to keep going like that bc if we stop....we crash. Think college kids getting sick after finals.

I hear you on being overwhelmed...but just pick one tiny little thing to do well today. Don't worry about the blogs and webinars. They are great but no good if they add to your stress level. Can you spend 30 seconds breathing deeply? Set a timer. Or can you put on Blueblockers tonight? Look at the sun right after you wake up tomorrow? Maybe come up with something better to do, or pick one of these tiny but powerful 'things'.

I wonder too about the food thing.......maybe it's not what you're eating, but what's eating you? If you had a magic wand, sort of, what would you change right now if you could? Maybe trying so hard to do all this is too much...maybe just try to do what you can, but not force yourself to eat so much food....just listen to your body. What does it want to eat? Up there, you wanted Mexican...that's a good start. Maybe not optimal, but better than cooking, tossing food and then feeling guilty about it? If you are?

Not sure any of this helps. I feel for you. And I know you will get better..

### sooperb — 2014-02-19T20:10:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-111215>

My humble offerings on your food dilemma, you are way too focused on it. You're translating all your doubts in your head and turning them into doubts about the food. You might fare better if you don't eat with your family. He makes it, you are scared to touch it, why? Does your husband watch you eating or do you feel as though he does? You push yourself to make something and again, before you even lift the fork you've already decided you're not going to eat it, can you reason out why? Do you hold food responsible for your other health issues, has your naturopath suggested this or anyone else?

It reminds me of my daughter years ago when she was seven we were having tea with the bloke who I later married. He cooked liver, she didn't want to eat it, he insisted, she didn't want to eat it but under threat she did. No sooner had she eaten the last bit than she vomited it all up again. It wasn't an act of defiance, she really didn't want to eat it and I think her subconscious rejected it. Now years later I wonder if there was something inside her that was troubling about this new man in her mum's life and somehow making her eat the liver caused an emotional response? I don't think your issue is with the food per se, I think it is centred round the dynamics of meal times. Don't eat meals, have a random selection of things you can dip into, nourishing stuff that you can eat a little at a time, boil a few eggs maybe and eat just one. Sliced meat so you can peel off just a little. Make soup with a bone broth base and sip a little from a cup rather than sit down in front of an audience and then take fright at the prospect of being watched over every mouthful.

Your obsession with filling all your time is perhaps an avoidance technique of never having quiet, mindful time where your head would start talking to you, especially if it's saying stuff you don't want to hear or deal with. Only you can change you and I feel that you have got yourself so worked up about it that maybe you need to approach this from a different direction. EFT maybe or rapid eye movement sessions. All those things you plan, don't, pencil in an hour just for you to sit and read. Half an hour maybe. I don't think it's going to be easy but do it for you, your daughter and your husband. But primarily for you.
