What is EFT/rapid eye movement?

I like the snacking all day idea. I tend to be a grazer naturally. I never really feel hungry except first thing in the AM. And that passes quickly if I just don't eat for a half hour or so. But doesn't that break the rules Dr. Kruse prescribes?
I prefer to eat vegetarian-ish. meat as a condiment, mostly veggies, beans, rice, lentils. I love Mexican because of the crunchy corn stuff. I know I'm not supposed to eat it but the crunch calls me! In fact, I know this about myself- many if not most of the foods I like have nothing to do with the taste, mostly to do with the texture. It's odd, I've never known anyone else to say that .
When I was pregnant with my daughter, I loved Almond M&M's in the freezer- I didn't particularly like how they tasted (almonds for me are- meh, and the chocolate after eating chocolate from Germany for so many years is disgusting actually), but I was addicted to the feeling of how they explode between your molars. LOL.

I think I just realized that some of this is my perfectionistic personality. I have a base MO in life of- If I can't do it better than anyone, it's not worth doing. Oy. How do I get rid of that?!

### Hope — 2014-02-19T20:54:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-111222>

I can't imagine them saying no if you go through the proper steps with notes from your MD. Just a thought. I'm not sure if it part of the solution for you but I wonder how you would fare if you had even 4 weeks off. If they value you, I can't imagine they'd tell you to quit. Of course I know nothing. Except this is YOUR body....

### Hope — 2014-02-19T21:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-111224>

> [Embyr42 said:](/goto/post?id=111221)
>
> I am taking this to heart sooperb, this is probably right on the money- I just have to figure out how to make it resonate a bit more. I absolutely blame food for my health problems. If I could just eat perfectly, then I wouldn't be fat. And I wouldn't be sick and tired. That's what I believe anyway.
>
> If that were the case, I'd have been in perfect health a long time ago. It's the environment and how we live, too. Remember what Jack says - it's all about sun, magnetism, water. Food is important, but not everything. Think big. Outside the box.
>
> When I was pregnant with my daughter, I loved Almond M&M's in the freezer- I didn't particularly like how they tasted (almonds for me are- meh, and the chocolate after eating chocolate from Germany for so many years is disgusting actually), but I was addicted to the feeling of how they explode between your molars. LOL.
>
> Nothing like German chocolate. Though I must say, even it is too sweet. Or maybe I know my bod doesn't like the sugar in it.
>
> I think I just realized that some of this is my perfectionistic personality. I have a base MO in life of- If I can't do it better than anyone, it's not worth doing. Oy. How do I get rid of that?!

This is tough, but you can do it. My old ballet teacher told me when I was 7, "Self-awareness is the first step to improvement."

I think a lot of sick people are Type As. I know I am/was. Of course environment didn't help. I think acceptance is key. My TCM mentor often reminds me, "Disease begins with resisting what is". Ugh. It is a BIG big struggle for me for sure....no question about it.

Good luck! I think you are doing well as long as you can stay this open

### sooperb — 2014-02-19T22:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-111236>

Yep, Hope's right, acceptance of a problem is the start of the road to recovery. Be aware you can't race along like an express train, take the time to create an action plan based on how much or little you can cope with. You want it, you can do it

Food is essential to life but what you eat, although important, only needs to nourish and sustain you, there are so many things that you can choose to do that but the one common denominator is - you have to like what you eat. Trying to crowd down stuff you wouldn't eat, didn't eat, actively dislike or don't want to eat is never going to work. With the reset I am sure there are plenty of options that will delight your taste buds and not offend your sensibilities  The trick is to be prepared, ensure that you actively buy in what you like, you can subdivide it and freeze part of it so you don't waste it. For example if you love avocado but aren't strong on prawns, buy a packet, take a few out and freeze the rest. From the few, eat one or two, they will last in the fridge for at least 3 days. Dip one in mayonnaise if that's how you like them. A little is better than none, but a bit here and there will mount up without you choking on them because the sight of a plateful makes you want to heave.

Emotional Freedom Technique is a fairly simple set of tapping actions that can help you overcome phobias etc. there are books and websites which can give you the basics. Rapid eye movement is explained here : [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye\_movement\_desensitization\_and\_reprocessing](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye_movement_desensitization_and_reprocessing).

Don't rush at this any more, I think taking a calm, reasoned approach would suit you, you obviously have excellent organisational skills, put them to good use. Good luck, you won't need it though because I think you're going to get a grip of this.

### Embyr42 — 2014-03-04T01:41:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-113250>

Well! A lot's happened in the last week or so since I was on here!

So I went to the dentist for a cleaning, and as it's a new dentist (a biological dentist) I asked him to look for cavitations, and I have a tooth with a crown that I've never really been able to chew on since they put the crown on about 4 years ago. Well, above that tooth there is a big black spot of necrosis, almost up through my sinus! And I have three more areas from my wisdom teeth (or where they were anyway)!! So I need a root canal on that tooth with the crown, and he's going to do ozone treatments on that, as well as the three cavitations. I wonder how many of my issues will resolve after this?
I'm not sure what I think about the root canal, I don't have much comfort from half of what I read that it's going to be a good idea, and then I get some comfort from other things I'm reading about how the ozone really does kill the bacteria in all of the tiny little canals... I'm kind of on the fence because it seems that removing the tooth can have just as many issues as a root canal. So I'm going to try this first- As the dentist said, we'll monitor it and if it's a problem, I can always pull the tooth later. But he wants to start conservatively and do the root canal.

Well the cleaning itself kicked up my lymph nodes on that side, and the bottom gums near that tooth are all painful and inflamed for some reason. The lymph node is very swollen and grumpy right under my jaw.

I'm trying to just eat Paleo, without worrying about the leptin Rx as it really was making me crazy and worse. I can't handle the restriction with everything else going on I guess. I need to get better a bit before I try to eat so restrictively. I don't lose weight unless I eat zero carb, so it's frustrating, but I've had some sweet potatoes, and some fruit and a little dark chocolate here and there over the last week and I feel more relaxed about food, not so panicky when I look at the food. I actually lost a couple of pounds. Although that may have been due to killing off the bad bugs and getting my digestion going better. I don't know.

The last couple of nights have been interesting- my heart is racing like mad when I'm trying to sleep! It's keeping me up, pounding, running away like crazy. I read that it can be an anxiety attack, which I don't think I'm having but I suppose it's possible, or it can maybe be low potassium- I guess that could be, so I took some concentrace minerals today (mag, chloride, sodium, potassium, sulfate, lithium and boron) and WOW did my general lower abdomen hurt about 10 minutes later. What the heck? Very bizarre.

So in the last month, I've now had a doctor and a dentist tell me that I'm "a unique challenge". Seriously, that is not something you want to hear from "experts". I don't want to be a unique challenge, I want to be normal and fixable. Sigh.

I'm trying to work up my courage to ask for a sabbatical in the summer. I am so terrified to ask for it, I don't want them to look at me as a liability for doing so- someone weak, someone who's a "risk". But the thought of taking one and just doing whatever I want for weeks on end sounds like heaven. Swimming in a river with my daughter (or just spending time with her- she's been in daycare since she was 11 weeks old), visiting my grandma and my mom, going camping- all things that I can't really do most of the time- heck I see my grandma and parents maybe once or twice a year. They live nearby (within a couple hour's drive) so it's not a distance thing, it's a time thing. I haven't swam in a river since college! I used to live in the water like a fish in the summers.

So I'll see how that goes, if I can bring it up. For some reason every time I think of bringing it up, I have to hold back tears, and it will be a cold day in hell when I shed tears at work, so that's part of what's stopping me- I do my best to make sure to never cry about anything in front of anyone. Not my husband, not my friends, and absolutely not at work.
Although I have no idea why I want to cry when I try to talk about it. Maybe relief? Pent up emotions? It's very odd.

### caroline — 2014-03-04T02:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-113259>

You have a lot on your plate .... a lot to think about.....

Do you read PrAna's posts?

All I know for sure is ... we just have this second. what is important to you?

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T13:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-113326>

Getting your teeth fixed is a good start, one area, a bit at a time is, I think good for you. Be guided by your dentist but if you have doubts, google and then question. Sometimes we do have to compromise, keep the gangrenous toe because surgery is dangerous or have it removed to save the leg? Neither is a choice we'd want to have to make but we have to in order to sort a problem that's out of our control.

If you can't ask for time off, why not put it in a letter? That way you can make a good case, give your reasons and explain why it's necessary. Perhaps even suggest that your working environment is part of the problem and that either you have a break from it or you will have to have a serious rethink about staying because your health is not just your problem it impacts on your family too. If you write it all down, it gives them time to consider it even if their gut reaction may be "No way". If you just ask face to face you might not make the best job of it, or as you hinted, you may become emotional and risk them thinking you are just another blabbing female who can't cope! I know that's not the case so sidestep it. You know, you might just be surprised at how much they value you as an employee and helping you fix yourself is worth it in terms of employer/employee relationships. There is also the Work/Life balance movement, you could include a reference to that too.

Crying isn't a sign of weakness, humans are emotional beings, crying is normal. We laugh in public, get angry in public so we should cry in public too, funny though it often embarrasses us doesn't it. If you're to move forward you need to loosen your hold on your psyche, maybe watch a weepy movie and have a good bawl  then watch a comedy and have a good laugh. You're you, a good person, try to let go of the "I am not worthy" approach.

### Embyr42 — 2014-03-31T22:02:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-119115>

So it's been a few weeks! Time sure flies. I've been heads down focusing on my teeth. Wow what a difference it's made! I got the root canal on the 14th, I had Halcion (sp?) to help with the anxiety, it didn't really kick in much until I was nearly done, so it was a bit of a waste, but I got through the procedure just fine. It was a homeopathic root canal- they filled it with Calcium Hydroxide, and they treated with ozone. They also started ozone treatments on my wisdom tooth sites. When they injected the ozone in the top two areas, I felt that stuff fly right through my bones! It felt like it traveled up behind my eye, through my sinus and down my throat! Those bacteria had been tunneling along just happy as a clam apparently. On the other side it wasn't very exciting, a bit down into my jaw but nothing like the left side. Apparently the ozone travels along the little "mole tunnels" eaten by the bacteria, so that's what I'm feeling. The Halcion helped me sleep for 15 hours after the procedure though, that was awesome!!
I go twice a week for treatments so that there are 5 total treatments in each site- I have to alternate left side and right on different days. They won't numb the entire mouth at one time, only one side at a time- so it's a bit of a time commitment. I have 5 more visits to go. Last week they injected in a slightly different spot on the top, this time the ozone flew around my entire top jaw from the wisdom tooth site, around my front teeth to the left side of my mouth! It's crazy what those buggers got up to!
Anyway, I feel MASSIVELY better! I am sleeping better, I am not achy all day, the sore ear/sinus/lymph nodes on my left side are gone. I require a normal amount of sleep instead of 12 hours or more. I wake up naturally on the weekends after about 9-10 hours instead of 14 or 15. And I wake up feeling like I can function, not like I simply woke up because sleeping longer wasn't acceptable anymore. I feel so hopeful that as I continue to rid my body of this apparently very bad infection that I've had for at least 2 years if not longer, I'll be able to reclaim my life and my health.
So it's nice to have some positive news!!

I just had a long weekend in Vancouver BC this last weekend though, and it was so nice to get away and just spend time with my family doing whatever we wanted to do. It rained like crazy which is no big deal for us Oregonians (we laugh at people who use umbrellas! Half the time we don't even put on a jacket in the rain), but the last day it started raining so hard it hurt your face when it hit, and that's no fun for even us. So we stayed inside and I got to watch HGTV all day (I have no cable at home) so I was in heaven! It was a nice rainy lazy day. We drove home yesterday and then just hung out and went to bed early. What a nice treat it all was.

I still haven't been able to ask for the extended time off. I don't think I can make it work this year. I'm thinking maybe next year? I don't know.
I have realized after a lot of thinking, that a TON of my wanting to get away from work is related to my boss. While I love my work and I really do like him as a person, I realized that I just can't work with him anymore. He's the one who creates the environment that I feel the need to escape from. He's the one that encourages (expects) long hours that aren't really necessary, he's the one that refuses to let us work from home.
And so I told my CFO during one of our 1:1's that I want to be a business analyst for the company in another group and work toward that move over the next year, which would give me massive flexibility and freedom. He said OK, let's work toward that.
He also suggested the possibility of being an Expat to our Germany factory, which is intriguing but would be really hard on my family- my husband is just a year into a career he loves (finally at the age of 38 he's found a purpose in life!) and so he won't leave it and I can't ask him to. That means my daughter has to live with one of us- the right choice for her would be the international experience for sure- a new language, new culture, how amazing for her to get that opportunity! But that means she won't see her dad for a couple of years other than maybe once or twice per year, and I'll be on my own during that time too. It's a hard place to be. But it's just a possibility, not even really a proposal, just a thought. But it's an option that would let me really change my life in amazing ways. So wheels are turning for me, I just don't know where they are pointed! Or what will come through.

On the really positive side- they posted a position for another manager at my level, who will take a big part of my workload off of me to allow me to go toward more of the work I like and a little less under the control of this manager- which may allow me to also have more flexibility- time will tell. Hopefully they can fill that position very soon. Any experienced CPA's in the Portland area looking for a job? Ha ha!

### fitness@home — 2014-03-31T22:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mthfr-and-pcos-and-hormones-oh-my.9438/post-119116>

Embry, this was a great update. So glad the dental infection treatment is going well. Your description of it was very vivid

Life is full of twists and turns...you never know what's around the next bend!

Hi everyone,

I've been on the leptin reset and CT for about 4 weeks now. I've got more history in my journal. I had some labs done a few months before I started the reset, and then just a week ago (about 2 weeks into the reset). Some of them don't seem great and I'd love some advice on what to do next!

I know I haven't done all the recommended labs yet, as I haven't yet told my Dr or endocrinologist (metformin for insulin resistance) yet, as I'm concerned they won't support this approach. I know I will need to find a way to get them to support me, but any help on the labs in the mean time would be wonderful! Thanks in advance

labs before reset:

Vitamin D: 65nM/L (currently on supplementation 10,000iu/day) (ref: 51-140)

TSH: 1.8 mU/L (ref: 0.50 - 4.4)

### Eliza — 2014-02-07T00:22:55+0200

### JanSz — 2014-02-08T00:18:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-with-my-labs.9439/post-108965>

> [Eliza said:](/goto/post?id=108767)
>
> Thanks so much for your reply Jansz. I have been doing the reset and ct for about 6 weeks now. Feeling better in a lot of ways but possibly only a little weight loss.
>
> Should I continue until I see further benefits or get retested now? I don't want to get all my labs done if I am still detoxing and they're worse. I'm hopeful that the reset and ct is sufficient to fix my hormones without supplementing. What do you think?

As far as I know, anything else that you may be doing, will not raise your DHEAs.
Among most commonly analyzed steroid hormones in the blood, DHEAs participation is about 95%, morning cortisol participation is about 4.5%, the remaining 0.5% is for all other.

Your DHEAs is an elephant, take care of it.

Read up here:
[http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm)
DHEA Restoration Therapy
[http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic\_health/dhea\_restoration\_01.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic_health/dhea_restoration_01.htm)
**Life Extension Suggestions**

****_Optimizing DHEA Blood Levels_****
[http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic\_health/dhea\_restoration\_les.htm#AnchorSug](http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic_health/dhea_restoration_les.htm#AnchorSug)

Women 275–400 µg/dL (this is blood test)

//////////////////////
You practically do not have any DHEAs.
DHEAS: 4umol/L (ref: 1.7 - 11.5 premenopause, 0.8-6.6 post menopause)

Post your age, and if your status (menstruating or not),
but you want good DHEAs levels regardless of your age.

...

# Thread: Ridin' the JK Express to Optimum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 244
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Grmoline — 2014-01-23T16:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-106757>

Enrolled in the 2014 Optimum Reset, which is kind of a new start, but not really. I've been reading, digesting, testing, and applying from this site (and sending others to it) for 2-1/2 years, just not visibly. I got here through my own body hacking, after hypothyroidism, inability to lose weight, poor sleep, night sweats, joint/muscle pain, very high inflammatory markers, and Mild Cognitive Impairment were being under treated or ignored by a series of docs. The tipping point for me was when the rheumatologist who was following me for 2.5 years told me that he couldn't figure out what was causing the inflammation and pain and that I should just come back in a year and be tested again. Add to that watching both my parents go down in spectacular fashion with obesity, diabetes, CV disease, crippling arthritis, kidney failure, Alzheimers Disease, breast & colon cancer, and little that their specialists seemed to be able to do about it. That's when I decided that I am on my own in this, but..... I used to be an R.N., I have 20 years experience in physics research, and I knew that there was a better way than managed decline. I stumbled onto Tim Ferris' book, changed my diet, and within two weeks the pain, night sweats, and fatigue all went away and I started sleeping like a baby. Just like that. I wanted to know why, and that search led me to Robb Wolf's Paleo diet, JK's Leptin Reset, and 4 months later my inflammatory markers were normal for the first time in over 3 years and I kept wondering when I would stop waking up feeling fabulous.

Spool forward one year - now down just under 30 lbs, cold-adapted, and about to go on the trip of a lifetime. 6-1/2 months around the world, starting in September and following summer around the world (seasonal/circadian mismatches), 39 flights and tens of thousands of air miles (EMF beyond belief). Add to that going from a ketogenic diet (trying to save my brain from the AD that has ravaged the women in my family for 3 generations) to a carb-loaded 4 months in Asia. It was unspeakably beautiful, wherever we went, whoever we met, and... my body paid a toll - 30 lbs weight gain, some inflammation back, even with trying to maintain Paleo through the western countries. I didn't lose all the gains I made, but it was a real learning about the effects of mis-matches on my body, especially after listening to Jack's interview with Dave Asprey and the effects of long-haul flights and hanging out down under where the ozone layer is thin (I spent 3 months in Antarctica before, so that wasn't my first exposure..... but I was cold-adapted for sure, doing field experiments stripped down to my long johns!). Even down under, I kept reading Jack's blogs courtesy of my i-Pad and kept learning!

So, I'm back to hacking me again, Epi-paleo, hitting CT hard for the past month, grounding and EMF reduction for the past 2 months, and my test results are going in the right direction. For whatever reason, it's been harder for my body to come around this time than it was for that year before the travelling. Maybe I permanently cooked my mitochondria. But we'll see where I am after 90 days of Optimal Reset and what happens to my labs (I go what I could over here as a baseline). I know i can burn away the extra fat (overweight but not obese). My biggest motivation is to prevent my brain from looking like swiss cheese in a few years. I'm already ahead of where my mom, her sisters, and her mom were by this age (61) so maybe I'm doing something right. I'm going for as optimum as is possible for my age, history, genetics, etc.

PS: My job now is to teach this stuff to others. How cool is that?

My mum was pretty fit but she died from a stroke as did her father. My father died from a brain tumour and both my mum and sister had pernicious anaemia. I'm creating my own business. I searched all over for a programme that taught this stuff but couldn't find anything that went much beyond CW, so I created my own. The entire reading list of two of the big programmes, plus my own in depth researching the leading edge stuff following leads from Jack's blogs and webinars plus others. Basically 2-1/2 years of soaking myself in it while doing my own hacking (I've had some sabbatical time). That was Jack's vision on his original website home page that drew me in from the beginning.

### caroline — 2014-01-23T22:10:05+0200

### Grmoline — 2014-01-24T13:12:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-106901>

So here are the interventions I am doing right now:
Diet - Epi-paleo, trying for ketosis again after a 21-day detox that had a lot of vegetable carbs in it. I have become the queen of bone broths, and I do seaweed a lot. Learned to love raw oysters on that round the world trip, and will be seeking them out more.
Sleep - Low lights in evening, blue blocker lenses, ready to sleep by 10pm, completely black room, and eyeful of sunlight first thing in the morning (well ok, this in England, the land of perpetual flooding, so it's the best daylight we can find over here.....)
EMF - turning off all devices in the bedroom at the outlet, no wifi at night, grounding pad during daytime work, grounding sheet at night
CT - cold showers (really cold this time of year!), spot ice packing (back, shoulders, abdomen), ice water and grape seed extract

But here's a question someone might be able to answer. I had a bout of diverticulitis this past fall that put me in the hospital for the first time in my life, five days of IV antibiotics. This followed over two years of not even having a common cold, even with that long round the world trip with all of its interesting exposures. Didn't even know that I had diverticulii. That, I think, was the reason for my hsCRP being elevated. I had one recurrence a month later, which was treated with antibiotics and was thought to be a result of the first one going subclinical and which resolved quickly with the antibiotics. For 3 months nothing, no recurrence, no problems. I've been working hard to heal my gut (bone broth, probiotics, liposomal glutathione, etc.) since I have AI issues. I was suspicious when my latest hsCRP came back high, although less than before. Last night the diverticulitis symptoms came back, and the only non-surgical intervention known is antibiotics. I'm told that if this keeps recurring I would be advised to have surgery to remove that section of the colon. That's really radical, and not my idea of anything I want done to my body. So I'm wondering..... have others of you had this issue, and have you had any success with the protocols helping to resolve the problem? If I have to live with antibiotics again this round, there are some things I can do to protect/rebuild my gut biome, but I don't want any cutting. I'm really bummed about this, given how hard I'm working on me!

Hi Grmoline, good to read about your progress and how close are you to being "ready for business?''

Also had 2 bouts of diverticulitis about 3mths in on Leptin reset 2 1/2 yrs ago with much the same Rx as you. Am of the same vintage! Lots of reasons why, but for me, I believe the major factor was my previous life time carb diet coupled with an upside down Pg/E ratio. Somewhere on one of his blogs JK shows how that actually impacts on gut flora cause they respond to..ie ebb and flow with these hormonal levels..or lack of=lack of flora. JK also mentioned that he used large doses of magnesium for some time, to stop formation of this bad bacteria biofilm (basically loose stools). I was fortunate in not having any further issues, just kept really keto and stuck with some coconut oil and crossed my fingers.

Just wondering, if you are sub clinically fomenting in the diverticuli, the mag Rx could be a quick measure to remove those pesky bacteria. My sister had no probs with her diverticuli cause she just did what JK did, ( didn't realise it was the mag causing the problem with the loose stools).....but I did ...and I reduced the mag ....and got diverticulitis..JK's explanation was too late for me!

### Embyr42 — 2014-01-29T17:33:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-107316>

> [Grmoline said:](/goto/post?id=107140)
>
> Should be getting the data from the 23andMe test back in a couple weeks. The company was stopped from providing an analysis of the data beyond ancestry, however, so I'm at a loss for how to get it interpreted. Anyone out there know of a source for interpreting the data?

Yes, contact Sterling Hill at [http://www.mthfrsupport.com/](http://www.mthfrsupport.com/) or [beclotaware@gmail.com](mailto:beclotaware@gmail.com) and she'll interpret it for you for $50.00.
You can also run it through her app on the site for $20- although it doesn't tell you what each SNP does, so you'll have to go online to snpedia.com to look them up one by one.
Or you can run it through promethease and it will do a pretty good analysis for you- I found that having both was most useful to me though.

### Grmoline — 2014-02-03T14:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-108086>

I've been reading the stuff about reducing EMF in the home, and how the laptop could possibly be the biggest source. I don't have a desktop computer - I use the laptop for all of my work. I know that I could ground the laptop and that it has been shown to bring the skin voltage from several volts to near zero. But I sit with bare feet on a grounding mat whenever I use the laptop. Shouldn't that already be bringing my voltage to zero and therefore making grounding the laptop redundant? Seems like it should also be protecting me from any other electronics I use while I am grounded.

Also had a chance to listen to some of the CT webinar over the weekend, but was away for a conference and only had late night to listen, so only got the first hour. Very disappointed, as that was the one I have been especially waiting for and the intervention I am most focused on right now (I've already been implementing others) and where most of my questions have been. I did hear JK say that cold showers are the hardest method, I guess because you don't get the benefit of formation of the EZ in the water next to the skin like you do with immersion. It didn't sound like he was saying that it was ineffective, just harder to adjust to the cold. I was interested because I do cold showers, in addition to ice packs (not at the same time). I've also turned down the heat and wear less clothing inside and out, mostly because I have adjusted to lower temps.

>
> Have you read Dave Asprey's fat fast? I love IF and feel so much better with much more fat and less protein - I am back eating a fair bit of butter again...

When I was doing my earlier hacking and so tracking macronutrients to see how my body responded, I found that I seemed to do best with shredding fat and sustained energy when I had around 2/3 of my food energy coming from fat. (So much for the "fat makes us fat" misconceptions!!) I also know that when I did that big around the world trip, which was carb loaded (even though I was in perpetual summer for that year), I gained fat weight easily, and that is still the case if I even sniff starchy carbs. So ketosis suits me, and I do well on the High Octane. I haven't read about his fat fast, but I will. Right now I am also trying to work out if I do better with more or less protein as well. It's just hard for me to tell where I am in the healing process at the moment - whether I am still LR with some IR, and whether or not the PPP pathway is working. I just know it's darned hard to shred the belly fat (I currently have about 15 lb more than in that picture) no matter what I am doing. So I just keep going with reducing EMF, CT, blue blocker glasses, and a ketogenic epipaleo diet.

Foodwise, I do some version of BulletProof coffee in the morning, loaded with VCO and MCT, plus wilted greens of some sort, eggs, and meat or fish. I keep bone broths going continuously, so if I do lunch it's usually a mug of broth with MCT and possibly fish of some kind (smoked salmon, tinned wild salmon, cod roe, prawns, potted shrimp). Dinner is a steamed vegetable (usually something in the cabbage/broccoli family) with meat or fish. All meats are grass-fed, poultry and eggs are free range. I use a variety of fish, and try to get in shellfish a few times a week. I don't count calories (meaningless) and I'm no longer tracking macronutrients. I do check my urine for ketones, and I'm pretty much continuously in ketosis by that measure. Oh, and I do throw in some IF days which are basically a high protein and fat breakfast and then either nothing until the next morning or I have some lunch and then nothing until the next morning. Its not hard to do.

I know what you mean about the AD though, although I have much more optimism about that since finding Jack's blogs. I got from him that it's not written in stone, and I know that whatever degeneration was beginning to happen with me seems to have stopped in its tracks and even reversed some since doing the protocols. So I think I'm doing my part, and the rest of it is beyond my control.

### Grmoline — 2014-02-14T16:19:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-110187>

Just back from the market (outdoor fish market and meat market) and scored big time. Lots of shellfish for a nice Valentine's dinner, but even better still - a great source of grass fed bones for bone broths, for free!! I called their main bricks-and-mortar market and asked if they had beef and lamb bones for stocks. They do, so I asked if they could send some over to their outdoor market which is nearby, enough for 3 big pots. Turns out it was enough for 6 pots, and he didn't charge a cent. Said he was told to give them to us, and maybe we would buy some meat (which we did). I have the bone broth method down to something that works for me on a gas stove without too much fuss, which allows me to have a constant supply of broth in the fridge.

### Grmoline — 2014-02-16T17:41:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-110604>

I read Dave Asprey's fat fast and I can see where it would help to shred fat, but here's the part I get concerned about. I think it would probably be alright if your redox potential is okay. But if I understand what Jack has been telling us correctly, if your battery is bad then you need to provide the body with a continuous source of good protein along with the good fats in order to help your redox. He keeps saying that protein synthesis is the most energy demanding process, so that means it drains the battery faster. If your battery doesn't recharge well or hold its charge, then you need to help it along by providing good proteins so the cells don't have to work so hard. I go ketogenic, but I think I'll pass for now on Dave's protocol.

### Grmoline — 2014-02-24T17:35:50+0200

Yes, so true. This is an attitude that I address with my clients. We can point a lot of fingers at the western medical establishment and the role that money and politics is playing to keep real progress from happening, but we the public have our part to play in keeping that system going as well - in not asking questions, not challenging, and expecting a pharmaceutical "fix". A lot out of ignorance, to be fair. When we know better, we do better - as long as we take action..... else, understanding is just the booby prize.

### caroline — 2014-02-25T01:06:40+0200

### nonchalant — 2014-02-25T01:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112007>

If I hadn't gotten the power boost and hope from low carb Paleo, I don't think I would have had the energy to keep searching for a better path to optimal. If you are at the bottom, and your adrenals are crashed, it is difficult to move or consider other options...

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T12:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112087>

> ^^^^ most people want change delivered to the front door or uploaded to the their brain without any work. This is why people come to the doc expecting a pill for every ill **and my profession obliges.** Be careful about what you ask for, you might get it.

I can understand that but what came first, the patient putting their trust in a doctor to make them well or the doctor stopping learning the day they qualified? As a patient you expect your doctor to know more than you, know what causes your problem and know how to fix it. These days doctors appear to be content to have their practice governed by what their government says passed down by, in the States, the FDA and, in the UK, the BMA. They are stuck in a rut well ground by their predecessors and most seem content to underpin their practice with outdated and outmoded models. It's a rare doctor who will step outside their comfort zone and challenge the establishment and point fingers in the direction of those whom they believe haven't moved on.

In these days of google, a lot more patients are better informed than their doctors but such is the arrogance of the medical profession they are ignored or treated with contempt for daring to question. My husband is a classic case, type ll, his GP is still insisting he needs to take statins, told him he needed to lose weight and then gave him a diet sheet that included carbs with every meal and low fat yuk. When you aren't medically qualified it's hard to challenge a GP so certain are they that they are correct in their advice. How long will it be before this changes?

### kovita — 2014-02-25T13:59:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112091>

sooperb, spot on! I just came back from visit to my GP with another prescription for antidepressant. I tried my best in the 15 minutes assigned slot but failed again big time. I received a label "prediabetic" because I refused glucose oral test which I would fail since I am highly adapted to fat burning and my carbs intake is very low and My fasting glucose values are in late nineties due to the ketosis. She would disrespect me completely ignore my BG charts showing nothing like diabetes but physiological insuline resistence caused by my diet. According to my GP macronutrients ratio in your diet has nothing to do with your BG and less with your ability to pass test designed for carb burners and taken as a golden standard.

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T15:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112100>

Indeed Kovita, why are these GPs so resistant to at least reading more widely and then evaluating what works/might work and considering that alongside conventional wisdom? Anti-depressants should only be a short term solution to any but the most entrenched depressives. Time spent listening is more valuable with enough insight to recognise that depression is not an illness cured with a pill but a set of circumstances that overwhelm someone who then struggles to cope. I hate that depression is such a widespread condition that so few GPs "get", they think 5 minutes and a script suffices when what most depressives need is help to work through their issues and support to move forward. They don't get it unless they have exceptional family and friends. My daughter is a depressive, her life was pretty much wrecked at one point but I've stuck with her because I could see behind the behaviour the same lovely girl that was always there, she just needed to lean on someone till she worked her way through it. She's thriving now but the black dog still lurks occasionally.

### Grmoline — 2014-03-01T16:06:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112823>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=112087)
>
> I can understand that but what came first, the patient putting their trust in a doctor to make them well or the doctor stopping learning the day they qualified? As a patient you expect your doctor to know more than you, know what causes your problem and know how to fix it. These days doctors appear to be content to have their practice governed by what their government says passed down by, in the States, the FDA and, in the UK, the BMA. They are stuck in a rut well ground by their predecessors and most seem content to underpin their practice with outdated and outmoded models. It's a rare doctor who will step outside their comfort zone and challenge the establishment and point fingers in the direction of those whom they believe haven't moved on.
>
> In these days of google, a lot more patients are better informed than their doctors but such is the arrogance of the medical profession they are ignored or treated with contempt for daring to question. My husband is a classic case, type ll, his GP is still insisting he needs to take statins, told him he needed to lose weight and then gave him a diet sheet that included carbs with every meal and low fat yuk. When you aren't medically qualified it's hard to challenge a GP so certain are they that they are correct in their advice. How long will it be before this changes?

I'm not sure that it really matters which came first, as it doesn't affect the outcome - both patients and healthcare providers are stuck in a way of thinking that needs to be broken. I hate that doctors have to fear for their professional licenses and loss of reputation for bucking the system as much as I hate that there are those doctors who refuse to even look at other possibilities. I am lucky to have a GP in the NHS who has said she is always willing to learn from her patients and will do all she can within the limitations of the NHS for me. I respect her for that and the limits she is faced with, and the rest I have to go out and find myself. But I didn't know what I didn't know until I knew it, and then it was up to me to take action. I think what Dr. K is talking about is what happens with some when they are shown the way out but they don't like the steps and choose to hope instead for a simpler option that doesn't require change. That's why one of the biggest changes needed is a change of thinking that allows those first steps to be taken.

### sooperb — 2014-03-01T16:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-112826>

"That's why one of the biggest changes needed is a change of thinking that allows those first steps to be taken."

Couldn't agree more. You also need a great deal of faith that what you're going to try will work after so many disappointments and you also need to be ready to defend yourself against the naysayers. Of which they are plenty, composed of those who would like to change but are too frightened to step out of their comfort zone or who don't have the intellect to understand why what they do won't work.

### Grmoline — 2014-03-05T12:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-113556>

A week and a half of respiratory infection has put the kibosh on my CT for a while. I suppose it would have cooled the fever but probably not a good idea. I hate that it is taking so long to get over and hate that I even got it in the first place. I went for 2 years without so much as a sniffle after I made all the diet and lifestyle changes, but these last few months I've been more susceptible to stuff it seems.

Anyway, I got my 23andme results back and have run it through two different analysis programmes. Lots of colours and SNPs identified, but otherwise pretty meaningless to me without some other information or input. I've got a consult set up with Dr. Jackson later this week, so that may help unmuddy the waters. I've gone about an far as I can go in my own biohacking without some outside input and guidance. So here's hoping.

And the biggest challenge is getting the kind of tests that are so much more available in the US, especially without a doctor's request. Some are just not available, even with a doc on board and willingness to self pay. Telomere testing, for example, is an impossibility here. Even getting the Metametrix GI Effects ordered was an expensive and difficult process. Genova has scooped up Metametrix and offer a GI screen that is not as effective, and still requires a doctor's request.

That's how they do it at bloodtestslondon.com (Blue Horizon Medical), where I have gotten quite a number of tests done. To do a salivary ASI and hair analysis I had to find a local practitioner to order them through, done through a google search. That's also how I'm doing the Metametrix GI Effects profile, through a lab here but through a practitioner in the US. Where's Preston?

### sooperb — 2014-03-19T18:13:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-116446>

I have had pulsatile tinnitus in one ear, constantly for over twenty years. The noise doesn't matter as such but the fact that it keeps time with my pulse, irritates me to death. As an anxiety sufferer who has palpitations it makes it 100 times worse, so I sympathise enormously. Some forms of tinnitus are curable, others appear not to be. Pulsatile can be cured if it is caused by a defect that can be put right, surgical intervention is required though. Things have moved on since I was first hit with it, then it was you just have to learn to live with, now there are a few things on offer although not suitable for all. Google has a lot of info

The more I test, the more I find. Blimey! So the GI Effects test showed a parasite, some bad bacteria, low levels of some good bacteria, and mild fat malabsorption. But, the candida that I worked on for a year to eradicate is still gone, and other things look pretty good including inflammatory stuff. The viral assay showed active Epstein-Barr, along with some previous exposure to other viruses that are not currently active. So along with methylation/detox issues, I now have these bugs to handle, which is probably at least part of why my hsCRP is still elevated. Also, my thyroid has gone further south since January. I feel like I'm patching new holes constantly to keep the ship afloat while I shore up the legs of the stool that will turn this ship around. All the while massively stretching my brain to try to piece together the threads from the latest blogs and webinars so that I can understand what is happening and turn it into direct interventions. Hashi's may be a gateway disease, but I am determined to halt it if that is within my power to do.

And..... I need to get out of central London. It's way too much EMF pollution and noise pollution and air pollution and light pollution. I can almost feel those electrons being sucked out of my body. What Jack says about zip codes surely must apply to post codes here, and mine can't be good......

I don't have a specific location at the moment, but there are some criteria. First I look at where all the recent flooding was, and don't buy anywhere that sat underwater for three months. I've bought into the idea of grounding and CT in water, but not in my living room. Second, I look at flight paths to Heathrow or Gatwick, nearby towers, masts, and regional power lines. All those are no go. Then I look for trees, open spaces, lots of green, and land to play with. I want feet on the ground, hands in the soil, and my own seasonal vegetables and herbs. Then I look for the strong EMF sources that I can't see. When those things are satisfied, then I have the luxury of looking at access to a train line, proximity of friends, etc. This is all just to find location, never mind what is in the house itself! We can always add features, but we can't take away a bad environment. I'm looking west or southwest. It would put me closer to the sea. Further north is not a choice.

### Grmoline — 2014-07-10T19:21:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-133850>

I don't post much in the forums, but I am here all the time. I've been a student of this website/blogs/forum/webinars for almost 3 years now. I've read volumes over the last few years, partly trying to self heal since the medical establishment gave up on trying to figure out what to do with me. In truth, they didn't know what they didn't know. I've been healing myself (or helping my body to heal itself, in truth) ever since. I think there are possibly a lot of my kind, and I am grateful for those of you who post a lot (e.g., Audrey, Nicole, Inger, Gangrad), who add other dimensions (e.g., Neil, Josh, Gretchen), who interpret for us (e.g., Yew, Jansz, nonchalant) and so many others who bring what Jack is giving us to life, share you experiences of it, ask the questions that help Jack to distill it, and basically help me to understand and apply it for myself and others that I talk to. There are ripples being created here that extend far beyond the conversations that are happening here.

I have worked for years in the public and private sector, teaching methodologies that help people to identify unconscious mindsets that blind us to reality and truth, and how to shift those mindsets in order to open to the truth (and gift) that is being offered in every experience and every moment. Prior to that, I had a long career in research, specifically environmental geophysics, hydrology, and biochemistry. Prior to that, I worked in the western medical paradigm as a nurse. Strange combination, to be sure, but look at the combination and what is possible if I bring all of those experiences together. I don't think it is an accident that I happened upon this website, and I am optimistic about how to make this next, possibly last, phase of my life count. Thank you.

### Grmoline — 2014-08-07T15:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-137122>

I've been having some very interesting rambling thoughts about molecular vibrational frequencies as a means of cellular signalling, ala Benveniste, Montagne, et al. This concept opens up so many avenues of understanding for me, and has me thinking about a whole collection of biochemical/biophysical effects. I'm thinking of this as I am doing a biohack with homeopathic HCG and the mechanism by which water can have "memory". So the thinking goes that molecules have very specific vibrational frequencies that depend not only on their chemical compositions but on their shape as well. Change in shape = change in frequency. Water, with its uniques ability to form structures around hydrophilic proteins, is able to structure in a way that it is able to replicate or somehow transmit that frequency over a distance, enabling the receptor to be stimulated without requiring the protein to touch the receptor. This would explain the statistical conundrum that would require direct contact, when the probability of that 'random' contact within a cell is exceedingly low. Presumeably, in homeopathy, it is this vibrational frequency picked up by the water that is preserved over multiple dilutions, in fact getting stronger with each dilution. It's the sequence of dilutions, rather than the end concentration, that is key to this process. It makes so much sense, in my mind, why homeopathy works - beyond any placebo effect. (It works well on animals, too.)

Then I got to thinking about bent proteins, and how that would affect signalling. See, I figure it's all electrical signalling that's going on in the body anyway, and chemical reaction in the classical sense has very little to do with anything, including enzyme reactions. Bent proteins would not only not give the signals that they were meant to give, but would give out other signals that may be detrimental. I begin to see how having bent proteins can open a Pandora's box of troubles.

Then there's transfats - 'trans' because they are differently shaped from their otherwise identical cousins, and therefore would behave differently if they happened to find themselves embedded in your cell membranes instead of the cholesterol they are meant to replace. Bad signalling in the membranes is bad news all around, as Dr. K has been showing us.

Now here's the wild one. You know how Montagne did those experiments with the two test tubes, where he had purified water in both and a specific DNA strand in one. He left them in close proximity for a bit, then put some DNA bases in the one with just water and an exact replica strand of DNA assembled itself. Transmission of the vibrational frequency from one test tube to another that effected a reaction in the other. I got to thinking about not just what happens within one body but between human bodies in proximity to one another. If that happens with test tubes, why not us? So maybe that phenomenon whereby females that work in close proximity to one another in the same office (or in families) end up having their menstrual cycles get in sync. Estrogen/progesterone vibrational frequencies being shared? My mind can go down a lot of rabbit holes with this concept! But I'll tell you, from the view point of this former physicist, this doesn't all sound so crazy.

### Grmoline — 2014-08-31T13:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-139465>

I was having a look at my previous labs, and I realised that I made an accounting error which affects my BUN:creatinine ratio. I had calculated that I dropped my ratio from 18.9 to 13.7 over the course of a year, and attributed that to cutting deeply into nnEMF exposures. Turns our that here in the UK they measure Urea, not just the nitrogen containing fraction (BUN), which is about double the value of BUN alone. That means that my corrected BUN:creatinine ratio is just under 8! That is hugely better when it comes to being an indicator of cellular hydration and redox potential. Cool beans!

I'm in the midst of a hack with HCG, since I have been unable to move body fat for 9 months despite being ketogenic for years and following Jack's protocols. I'm sure there are still issues with the environment, but I'm limited in what I can do until I can move from London. This place has to sell before I can buy in the country. I'd been looking at the HCG protocol with some interest, particularly coupled with the protocols and better redox. I've done one short round earlier in the summer, knowing I would need to interrupt for a trip to the US, and now I am on a second short round, this time bringing in more seafood and a small amount of MCT oil. The first round was good, this second round so far is even better.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3871217/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3871217/)

Interesting.... "the role of aerobic glycolysis, referred to as the “Warburg effect,” is creating much excitement again in the field of cancer research. Warburg hypothesized that irreversible mitochondrial dysfunction is the underlying reason behind the metabolic shift to aerobic glycolysis, which results in transformation of the cells." This was before any knowledge of the impact of nnEMF on the mitochondrial membranes which, itself, can block the 2 essential change programmes directed by mitochondria. He was looking at the role of glycoloysis as the instigator of oncogenesis.

Jack said in an earlier response about skin tags that glycation blocks autophagy. Just putting the links together: high carbs + nnEMF = increased potential for oncogenesis. This isn't talked about in the paleo blogosphere......

[http://www.reuters.com/article/2014...Y2HO20140903?feedType=RSS&feedName=healthNews](http://www.reuters.com/article/2014/09/03/us-airplane-cancer-idUSKBN0GY2HO20140903?feedType=RSS&feedName=healthNews)

Looks like they're starting to zero in on the causes, at least leaving open the possibility that they don't have all of the picture. It's a start.....

Audrey, my trip this time is pure pleasure. Some driving involved, but every stop is located on a beach with the exception of the first 2 days in the Blue Ridge Mountains. And the latter has its own rewards. Unfortunately, the trans-Atlantic flightpaths from the UK follow a northward swinging arc, so more cosmic ray exposure. I hope that the Jet Lag Rx and a lot of days of good living (from an EMF/DHA/photon point of view) will help offset the damage. In any case, to see my family in the US requires exposing myself to the risk.

It sounds like you are doing well. How do you manage to chime in on so many of these threads and support so many people while limiting your EMF/blue light exposure? I could ask that same question of many people on this site, including JK......

### Grmoline — 2014-09-04T13:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-139897>

Just reading some "rambling" thoughts from Josh re T4 and the Q&A on a different thread that mirror the way some of this information is beginning to coalesce in my brain. It's such a fine lens for looking at modern day health and disease. As I look back over the past 3 years I can see how my focus and my interventions have shifted continuously over that time, as my understanding has grown. Well, first there was the glimmer of a new reality (change in thought), followed by a change in behaviours (adopting the protocols), followed by a deeper understanding (likely aided by increased DHA and brain function), repeat, repeat (upward spiral)....

### caroline — 2014-09-05T03:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-139969>

I, of course, should be doing a much better job of limiting EMF/'bluelite.....

but ...jackkruse.com has changed my life and I want to pay it forward in some fashion - if I can.....tell me if I drive you crazy!

I also live at the ocean..... and there is a lot of grass/shrubs/flowers/rocks and conifers in my area - and I walk everywhere.

will you keep a diary of your trip? it will be interesting to see how the protocols positively impact you ...as opposed to other trips.

One thing, in particular, from the jetlag blog that has really stuck with me..... Jack says he makes a list of things he wants to do/accomplish .... in case he starts losing too may electrons and starts making poor decisions. I can clearly see that his happens to me ...all my good intentions fly out the window!

Have loads and loads of fun ...and oysters!

### Linz — 2014-09-05T12:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-139984>

Grml, I love reading about the information coalescing in your brain. Sometimes I seem to string together some amazing loose ends now but half an hour later I think how ever did I do that and it's gone again. Audrey, thanks for the reminder about lists, I need to find a way to order thoughts better ( just can't be bothered with filing them where I will ever find them again - Josh I need help, or is it just oysters?). My electrons must be passing through far too fast. I just hope they are handing on some good information. I don't like to think it's lost.

Have a great trip, and treat yourself to a big bag of oysters from Borough Market to beat the jet lag when you come home.

Yes, I get wanting to pay it forward. I share with as many people as I can, informally and formally through my business. Is that a little bit of starfish?

That's a good idea about the list. Maybe the cosmic rays/excess UVA/EMFs/Jet Lag when I fly are what makes it easier to ditch all of my good intentions when I go on holiday. I've shed a lot of fat over the past month and a half and I'd like to hang onto that and the redox improvements I'm seeing.

By the way, no, you don't make me crazy. I may be red dress material in the making, but I'm not there yet.......

### Grmoline — 2014-11-07T19:40:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-146457>

Blimey! I can't believe it has been 2 months since I last updated here. (I've had entries elsewhere, however....) Got some updated labs back in early September, some I'm happy about, others not so good. Some will be repeated later this month or early December, so will see if there is any improvement.

**HAEMATOLOGY**
Haemoglobin (g/L) \*164 g/L 115-155
HCT \*0.482 0.33-0.45
Red Cell Count \*5.27 x10^12/L 3.95 - 5.15
MCV 91.5 fL 80-99
MCH 31.1 pg 26.0 - 33.5
MCHC (g/L) 340 g/L 300 - 350
RDW 12.4 11.5 - 15.0
Platelet Count 334 x10^9/L 150 - 400
MPV 10.1 fL 7-13

White Cell Count 4.07 x10^9/L 3.0 - 10.0
Neutrophils \*1.90 x10^9/L 2.0 - 7.5
Lymphocytes 1.77 x10^9/L 1.2 - 3.65
Monocytes 0.31 x10^9/L 0.2 - 1.0
Eosinophils 0.07 x10^9/L 0.0 - 0.4
Basophils 0.02 x10^9/L 0.0 - 0.1

ESR \*24 mm/hr 1-20

**BIOCHEMISTRY**
hsCRP \*5.7 mg/L 0.0-5.0
SODIUM 144 mmol/L 135 - 145
POTASSIUM 4.6 mmol/L 3.5 - 5.1
CHLORIDE 99 mmol/L 98-107
BICARBONATE 24 mmol/l 22-29
UREA 5.3 mmol/L 1.7 - 8.3
(14.8 mg/dL)
CREATININE \*92 umol/L 49-92
(1.04 mg/dL)
estimated GFR \*56
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

BILIRUBIN 7 umol/L 0-20
ALKALINE PHOSPHATASE 90 IU/L 35-104
ASPARTATE TRANSFERASE 20 IU/L 0-31
ALANINE TRANSFERASE 17 IU/L 10-35
LDH 176 IU/L 135 - 214
CK 100 IU/L 26-140
GAMMA GT 17 IU/L 6-42
TOTAL PROTEIN 74 g/L 63-83
ALBUMIN 47 g/L 34-50
GLOBULIN 27 g/L 19-35

CALCIUM 2.58 mmol/L 2.20 - 2.60
(10.32 mg/dL)
Corrected Calcium 2.55 mmol/L 2.20 - 2.60
PHOSPHATE 1.21 mmol/L 0.87 - 1.45
URIC ACID \*513 umol/L 175 - 363
(8.62 mg/dL)

FASTING TRIGLYCERIDES 1.2 mmol/L < 2.3
(106 mg/dL)
FASTING CHOLESTEROL \*5.8 mmol/L Optimum <5.0
(224 mg/dL)
HDL CHOLESTEROL \*1.0 mmol/L 1.2 - 1.7
(38.7 mg/dL)
HDL % of total \*17 % 20 and over
LDL CHOLESTEROL \*4.3 mmol/L Up to 3.0
(166 mg/dL)

IRON 18.6 umol/L 6.6 - 26.0
T.I.B.C 74 umol/L 41-77
TRANSFERRIN SATURATION 25 % 20-55
FERRITIN 126 ug/L 13-150
Optimum Ferritin level for females : >27 ug/L

FASTING BLOOD GLUCOSE \*3.6 mmol/L 3.9 - 5.8
Haemoglobin A1c 5.0% 4.0 - 6.0
HbA1c (mmol/mol) 31 mmol/mol 20 - 42

**ENDOCRINOLOGY**
Fasting Insulin 9.6 mIU/L <10
TOTAL THYROXINE(T4) 105 nmol/L 59-154
TSH \*0.09 mIU/L 0.27 - 4.2
FREE T4 20.5 pmol/l 12.0 - 22.0
FREE T3 3.2 pmol/L 3.1 - 6.8

**IMMUNOLOGY**

Thyroglobulin Antibody 47.6 IU/mL 0-115(Negative)
Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies \*128.2 IU/mL 0 - 34

ARA Omega-6/EPA Omega-3 ratio \*8.98 1.5 to 3.0, optimal=2

Clearly there was still inflammation going on somewhere. The good news is that I am now about 4 months without any diverticulitis flares, treating with acupuncture and Chinese herbs. That's the longest since it all started a year ago. The inflammation may be related to that, so am hoping to see a decrease in the next round of tests.

These values are in many ways reflective of a continuous ketogenic diet, although I would have expected fasting insulin to be lower given the glucose levels. Also not sure if ARA:EPA is the most reflective ratio if I am pushing DHA over EPA in the diet (my DHA is much higher). Kidney stuff is suspicious, and high RBC/HCT/HGB. Bloodletting maybe??

### Grmoline — 2014-11-10T13:59:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-146747>

The trip itself was great - lots of sun, seafood, and grounding in the ocean daily. I doubt that the flights helped things, given that transatlantic flights from here arc toward the north pole (big cosmic ray exposure) and the trip out was in that window of a few days when we were advised not to fly because of very intense rays from recent solar activity (bigger still cosmic ray exposure). So I guess it was a mix for my poor mitochondria. But, I didn't use a drop of sunscreen, I had zero burn, and I got a lovely shade of tan. So maybe something is working right in my skin? I was picturing my collagen dancing with the UV and IR I was soaking up!

No red dress...... sorry......

### Grmoline — 2015-04-08T12:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-160641>

Wow, I haven't added anything here for a long time! I've been on the site but just too caught up in life to do much other than try to stay current on blogs and webinars. Been kept busy doing cancer care and trying to sell our flat for the last few months. First it was my friend with the Stage 4 cancer (whose tumors are continuing to shrink, thanks to options outside of standard western treatment). Then my own partner was diagnosed with uterine cancer the day before Thanksgiving and had major surgery in December. And that was after having the best lab results ever in October after years of following the protocols. It didn't stop the cancer but maybe it would have been far worse if we hadn't. We're dying (pun intended!) to get out of central London but the exchange on our property that was about to happen has stalled and we're having to put the flat back on the market again. Arghhhh!!!! So we do what we can to mitigate all the emf and various pollutions while we wait.

### Grmoline — 2015-05-10T18:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-163779>

Okay, so it is finally going to happen! We sold our flat in London, and we are now looking for a home in the countryside. I can't believe it has taken this long, almost 2 years. We just spent 5 days away at a spa after 9 gruelling weeks of negotiations with the buyer. Every day we had time in the sauna followed by time in the plunge pool - I didn't plunge, I soaked for a looooong time and raised a lot of eyebrows - plus time wandering around barefoot in the grass, along with the other requisite treatments. That was enough to convince me that wherever we end up will eventually have a sauna and a cold tank of water. I expect I will look a bit weird taking my EMF meter to do property viewings, but there you have it. And, I expect to be closer to the coast as well as access to pastured and organic meats and produce. But just to be out of EMF hell will be bliss. Just to be able to see stars at night will be bliss. Just to be able to hear birdsong in the mornings instead of trains, traffic and sirens will be bliss. Just to be able to hug our own trees will be bliss. Darkness, silence, fresh air, grass under my feet and soil to put my hands into. That's my vision, not 10 years from now but maybe 10 weeks from now if we can find the right place. Else, we rent until we do. Woo-hoo!

She doesn't do boats on oceans or she'd be there as well....

### Grmoline — 2015-05-31T15:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-166020>

Wow, I had a long stretch where I couldn't get onto the forum. My attempts just brought up a strange home page and then gobbledegook when I clicked on forum. Still can't log on but at least I can now connect here.

We're in the hunt for a new home, and we are the weirdos that ask all the strange questions about masts and power lines and who explore homes with a trimeter! We think we have located someplace, mostly surrounded by fields and near to the Itchen River, but there are a few neighbours (something like 5 dwellings in a rural cluster) so the next visit will be an in-depth EMF assessment. We only had the "internal meters" when we went the first time - the ones that indicated we liked the energy of the place and felt good just walking into it. I hope that is indicative of something because we may very well buy this place.

Healthwise, I've been noticing interesting patterns, having been logging a lot of data since 2011. The first is that my weight has a distinct seasonal fluctuation, where I always gain weight over the spring even if I don't change a thing, and then start to lean out in late summer and autumn. The second is that I have seasonal fluctuations in thyroid labs, with my thyroid function apparently dropping in winter (which is usually when my thyroxin was increased in the past) and then correcting itself in the summer. I've stopped adjusting the meds and am just observing how my body regulates itself. The good news is that my hsCRP is finally down to just over 1, still not optimal but I couldn't get it below 5 for a long time so that is a massive improvement. My BUN/Creatinine has gotten worse, but I don't think that will change until my postcode does. I get 15 wifi signals just sitting here in my home office, never mind all of the smart meters that are going into this complex. Drinking more water isn't the answer if I can't keep it in my cells! Maybe my tinnitus will finally go away when I get out of this urban mess.....

I just printed off all 15 posts in the Ubiquitination series. I need to go through it more than once to digest it all, and its just too much staring at a computer screen even with my blue-blockers on. That series is a treatise in itself! A mind-blowing one.

### Grmoline — 2015-06-06T17:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-166819>

So we finally got to get a second viewing of the property we are trying to buy. I could have wept at how beautiful it is, the luxurious lawn that I can walk out on in my bare feet to watch sunrises and sunsets, the absence of planes and sirens and trains and traffic, the near-absence of wifi signals (only two, and one belonged to the current owner), no high power lines, no nearby phone masts, no flight paths. Just sheep across the lane, wheat fields in front of us, wood/stone floors, lots of natural light, and get this - an unfluoridated water system. Yay! We're on mains water and I can filter, but can't do the fluoride. Plus, it's much nearer the sea. We don't have any meters at all, so I don't have to worry about the smart kind. We've got several farm markets in the vicinity and a fishmonger in the local village. Nicld says country living rocks, and I can practically taste it now. We may even exchange contracts while I am away on the cruise. I'll be toasting that if it happens!!

### Grmoline — 2015-06-09T16:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-167223>

Yes, well-meaning isn't always well-doing. And we all knew less back then! We've just got the survey report back, and it comes out pretty clean. The 'rooftop garden' (a small tree growing on top of the chimney!) needs to be taken care of as well as a blocked drainage pipe, but that's small stuff. There won't be any chain to speak of, if the owner keeps his word on exchange of contracts. Our current place is sold so we have to be out 31 July no matter what. The owner of the place we are trying to buy has nowhere to go, but will commit to being out no later than 30 August. So that makes us likely to be homeless for about a month. I'm sure we can find somewhere to hang out. Maybe we'll exchange on the 4th of July - Independence Day. Freedom from the Big Smoke, as London is sometimes called. That would be too cool! Except, of course, we don't celebrate the great separation from the Motherland over here. (I'm dual citizenship, so I'm allowed to..... quietly......).

### Grmoline — 2015-06-10T18:14:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-167418>

Just listened to the June webinar. Had oysters for breakfast to prepare, but I'm not sure the extra DHA helped. My, oh my, what a long, strange road it has been for the last 4 years. When there is a paradigm shift, then it is impossible for me to see the world in the same way even if I wanted to go back (which I don't). But just maybe, when we move to the country, I might stop seeing the world around me as a minefield of electromagnetic, chemical, and blue light smog and an assault on my senses. I'm sure that it literally steals joy as it shreds DHA, cellular hydration, and circadian timing. No wonder so many people in this city are so bad tempered. Maybe in August, when I can lie in my back yard butt naked if I want, full-body grounding, and see stars at night - maybe then I will remember how it feels to have joy oozing from my pores. Maybe, after a year and a half of listening to my ears ring, I will finally hear silence.

### Grmoline — 2015-07-13T18:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-171833>

So, it appears that I am an Inuit, or so Jack says. I re-read the last chapter of Jack's book, and honest to God I swear that he slipped in more information since I read it the first time! I'm beginning to understand the connection to why I tan so easily and keep it for so long. If you look at pictures of me in Antarctica, any bit of skin that got exposure is black. My immediate ancestral line is northern Minnesota, where summer is but a blink of the eye, and further back it gets worse than that - all Scots and Swedes as far back as we can trace (including the 23andme genetic tracing). If cold induces alpha-MSH, and alpha-MSH stimulates melanocytes, then the penny drops. Also, why I have no trouble with cold showers and feel best when I am cold and ketogenic (except for those warm ocean holiday top-ups). I grew up in Minnesota winters, back when we actually walked a mile to school even in blizzards, and we were outside all the time, every season. I can remember going out to the outdoor rink, skating until my hands and feet were numb, practically having to crawl the whole way home, and then going back out and doing it again. And look where I moved to - NORTH!! The winters may be milder here (for now, but wait till the Gulf Stream changes.....) but those winter nights are looooooong...... I also have a real 'intolerance' to carbs, in the sense that I gain weight at the drop of a hat when I go non-keto like I started this spring/summer to get the autophagy boost. Maybe the weight gain is just a necessary evil?

So here's the deal with my labs. It's so cool to look at them through a new lens. My H/H are at rockstar levels. If anything, they run high according to lab ranges (but who believes lab ranges....). My battery is apparently pretty good, helped by tons of DHA in the diet, and probably by nasal IR. But the UV side of things is poor (think northern latitudes, maybe a couple months of decent sun at most) so not enough current push (low voltage). No problem capturing electrons, but they're not moving much. High H/H is probably adaptive, adding more porphyrin rings to soak up any little bit of UV they can get. That means I need to get "The Girls" exposed to the sun every day that we are in that optimum window. I've been reticent, being modest, but with the size of mine that's a lot of solar panel to capitalize on. And it also means finding a source to augment the UV exposure - NOT the infrared that I thought I needed.

So my new hack has to do with driving current. UV light, TENS stimulation to upper back, CT to the carotids, meditation and music in the evenings while I CT and TENS, and perhaps TCS. Then I'll see what that does to cognition, as well as anything else that is controlled by the brain. Oh yeah, that's pretty much everything else. And when the seasons change, I'll live like an Inuit as much as I can. Should be easier in the country. I'll let you know how it goes.

### Grmoline — 2015-07-15T13:32:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-172003>

I think some big pennies are finally dropping, having had a few "duh!" moments of late. I was thinking about the view of high H/H as being an adaptive change, some earlier blogs about type 2 diabetes as being adaptive, cataracts as an adaptive change in the presence of too much blue light, and the discussion in the Ubi17 thread about adiposity generation in obesity as an adaptation to the body's inability to offload excess energy. Outside of "hardwired" genetic conditions (e.g., CF), I need to be looking at every lab result that is off and every neolithic disease symptom and asking the question,"What is this adaptation telling me about my environment?". I know, the rest of you are probably thinking, "Well hello, hasn't this been bleedingly obvious for some time?!". Yes, in the need to look to environment to fix the underlying conditions, but not to the full implications of this new paradigm. It means ditching the old myopic view and rebuilding understanding from the bottom up through the lens of the interactions of sub-particles and light within the human organism. There's no other way to "see" what's really going on.

And, as it appears that cognition is at its best in the first half of the day, that's when I need to do my looking......

### Grmoline — 2015-07-17T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-172187>

I've been starting CT to the carotids to try to stimulate more current in the brain. It's early days, so may be hard to tell yet, but I have to say that on re-read of the Ubi series the picture is dropping into place OMG, Jack, it's just the coolest thing! I think I have jumped to a completely different level in understanding. Its like when Leo suddenly sees what the Matrix really is. I used to use fibre optics in my geophysical research to transmit signals that were more protected from electrical noise than standard electrical signals (the Antarctic is very electrically noisy....). But I never fully appreciated the concept of light carrying information that can translate between photons and electrons. I was just glad that I had developed a system that would allow me to get signals that were bigger than the noise. But now there's this parallel between my experiments on "the ice" and what's happening in my RBCs. Way cool! I'm having to reach into the depths to retrieve the physics foundation that underpinned all my research, but it seems I can find it if I'm patient.

### Grmoline — 2015-07-20T23:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ridin-the-jk-express-to-optimum.9441/post-172452>

Re-reading with new understanding seems to be the key for me. I get what I get out of a blog on the first pass, at the level of my understanding at that time. And that's okay - it's what I need then. But the next pass, with a different lens, gives me so much more. I used to work with fiber optics, but never really understood the story of light. We're wired for fiber optics. I don't get the whole integrated picture of the quilt right now, but my understanding is so far from where I started. I'm beginning to get the exquisite dance between light and cold and how we respond to each.
