### PaulG — 2014-07-09T06:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-mamagrok-still-visit-these-forums.9537/post-133659>

Four years ago and coinciding with my interest in this forum I was an avid watcher of the TV series [LOST](http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lost_\(TV_series\)) which has a number of references and themes relating to QED theory. The final few episodes were disappointing because the writers and producers had to collapse the waveform of multiple possibilities into a deterministic 'finale'.. And the lead character was called Jack Shephard...

> Jack is very much a man of science. He serves as the antithesis of man of faith John Locke; however, his experiences in season five mold Jack into a believer, to the point of becoming John Locke's spiritual successor in the final season. His leadership role culminates in him taking briefly over as protector of the island from its immortal guardian, Jacob, and being the one to engage one of the series' villains, the Man in Black, in a climactic battle to the death.
>
> Despite Jack's gifts as a physician, he is haunted by his relationship with his father, a neurosurgeon at St. Sebastian Hospital. Jack exposes his father's alcoholism, ending his career. In September 2004, Jack's mother, Margo, (Veronica Hamel) orders him to find Christian, who has exiled himself to Australia, and bring him back. Jack goes to Sydney and finds that his father has died. Once Jack identified his father's body, he made arrangements to bring him home. Jack boards Oceanic 815 bound for Los Angeles on September 22, 2004, with his father's casket in the cargo hold

See
[http://sinema7.net/2010/04/10-comparisons-between-lost-and-quantum-theory/](http://sinema7.net/2010/04/10-comparisons-between-lost-and-quantum-theory/)

[http://scitech.blogs.cnn.com/2010/04/06/geek-out-lost-time-and-quantum-mechanics/](http://scitech.blogs.cnn.com/2010/04/06/geek-out-lost-time-and-quantum-mechanics/)

### SeaHorse — 2014-07-09T14:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-mamagrok-still-visit-these-forums.9537/post-133680>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=133635)
>
> When Jack brought up the curling analogy, I thought of Strong and Weak forces. When the Newtonian forces become weaker, other forces come into play. Or at least we start noticing them more. I'm not sure what to make of it yet, though. That, and Jack said to listen with the intent of understanding, not responding. Well, it's been a few hours, perhaps I can respond just a bit?
>
> My dear father, when I was very young, used to make me wait. I would ask some question, and he wouldn't reply for what seemed an eternity to my young mind. When he finally spoke, it was to start telling a story. I would listen, in a bit of confusion, to his new story. Finally, he tied it in with my question, which I had all but forgotten in the mean while... I think he was trying to teach me to think. Not sure if the lesson stuck, though. Hey, I was only about 8 years old!

Something about your story about waiting has made a connection for me between curling, your father, spin and the horse work that I'm learning…..

We practice waiting with the horses all the time….allowing something to happen instead of making it happen. We are not using physical force to make the horses do anything, we are working with their thoughts so that they don't get split between their bodies going one way, but their minds going another. There is a phrase that my teacher uses when we work with understanding their behaviours and getting their willing participation in being with us…the phrase is "figure out what happened before what happened happened."

In the immediate sense this is simply developing an observation of when something changes and what does it lead to….in the bigger sense it actually puts you in a different time relationship with your horse. The last workshop I was in it was like I had entered a new universe…I had all the time in the world to notice, think something through, adjust my thoughts, change my actions and keep awareness and mindfulness going between myself and my horse….nothing seemed "out of the blue" but things could be happening quickly and slowly at the same time…I could feel it coming and going…

Hard to describe, but I think it is actually a lot like the curling….you are shaping what's going to happen with the rock as it travels….but it is also shaping and responding to the ice in a way that a good player will see before it starts to travel beyond where they want it to go….AND there is always that piece of dust that sends the rock spinning out into a different trajectory….and you win or lose the game. With horses….the piece of dust is the event where you learn if all your work has given you a relationship that means your horse looks to you for support in a life-threatening moment or just goes into self-preservation and takes off "into the blue"

### PaulG — 2014-07-09T15:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-mamagrok-still-visit-these-forums.9537/post-133688>

> Sara said:
>
> In the immediate sense this is simply developing an observation of when something changes and what does it lead to….in the bigger sense it actually puts you in a different time relationship with your horse. The last workshop I was in it was like I had entered a new universe…I had all the time in the world to notice, think something through, adjust my thoughts, change my actions and keep awareness and mindfulness going between myself and my horse….nothing seemed "out of the blue" but things could be happening quickly and slowly at the same time…I could feel it coming and going…

This reminds me of an experience I had at university. As a kid I was a football/soccer nut, I lived in a small UK village and my parents ran a pub so I spent much of my time dreaming about, practicising and playing football - I got very good and had many achievements, after age 15 as I got more 'mature' I seemed to lose my joy and passion and my skill and imagination playing the game dwindled. Fast forward a few years and playing for my university team I was a mediocre player. At Uni I spent much of my time with my mate bemoaning why we were so unsucessfull with the ladies but in my last year I fell madly in love with an amazing young woman. One day shortly after I fell in love and had a girlfriend my mate asked me to go for a kick about in the nearby soccer pitch.

Well on this particular day with him in goal and me shooting I entered into a peculiar state of mind or flow where I could do no wrong. So let me explain this.. as a kid dreaming of soccer you dream every night about hitting the perfect volley or free kick or being able to curl a ball into the top corner or hitting a ball with the correct power and topspin to make it dip over the reach of the goalie...but in reality you pull off such a thing of beauty infrequently. Well on this day, every single shot I took was like how I imagined it 15 years ago in my dreams, perfect and unstoppable - it was like my imagination and my ability to enact it were no longer seperate, that is in between the space between imagination and action there was no fear or doubt only a creative flow that seemed to flow forward and backwards in time and reconnect with my younger self that was burried deep inside. I was in love after years of repressed emotion and loneliness, I was so full of joy and so lacking in fear and doubt and self loathing and I was one with the universe and like my 12 year old self I had a smile from one ear to another as I hit shot after shot that sailed effortless into the back of the net. I could actually visualise the result before it happened. I imagined that I would hit a shot straight at the goalie but at the last minute would turn or go under his legs and it happened just as I imagined every time.. it was very freaky.. I am sure great artists or musicians or sportsmen or writers can relate to this flow state.

I have experienced a similar state but no way as profound as before in tennis usually after 3 hrs of hard play and a couple of pints of cider on a sunny day when the eye is in and the juices are flowing.

I eventually went on to marry that woman and have 3 wonderfull kids but I have never forgotten that experience.

Fast forward a few years and playing for my university team I was a mediocre player. At Uni I spent much of my time with my mate bemoaning why we were so unsucessfull with the ladies but in my last year I fell madly in love with an amazing young woman. One day shortly after I fell in love and had a girlfriend my mate asked me to go for a kick about in the nearby soccer pitch.
>
> Well on this particular day with him in goal and me shooting I entered into a peculiar state of mind or flow where I could do no wrong. So let me explain this.. as a kid dreaming of soccer you dream every night about hitting the perfect volley or free kick or being able to curl a ball into the top corner or hitting a ball with the correct power and topspin to make it dip over the reach of the goalie...but in reality you pull off such a thing of beauty infrequently. Well on this day, every single shot I took was like how I imagined it 15 years ago in my dreams, perfect and unstoppable - it was like my imagination and my ability to enact it were no longer seperate, that is in between the space between imagination and action there was no fear or doubt only a creative flow that seemed to flow forward and backwards in time and reconnect with my younger self that was burried deep inside. I was in love after years of repressed emotion and loneliness, I was so full of joy and so lacking in fear and doubt and self loathing and I was one with the universe and like my 12 year old self I had a smile from one ear to another as I hit shot after shot that sailed effortless into the back of the net. I could actually visualise the result before it happened. I imagined that I would hit a shot that straight at the goalie but at the last minute would turn or go under his legs and it happened just as I imagined every time.. it was very freaky.. I am sure great artists or musicians or sportsmen or writers can relate to this flow state.
>
> I have experienced a similar state but no way as profound as before in tennis usually after 3 hrs of hard play and a couple of pints of cider on a sunny day when the eye is in and the juices are flowing.
>
> I eventually went on to marry that woman and have 3 wonderfull kids but I have never forgotten that experience.

Wow, Paul, I didn't know you wrote poetry! Your love for her literally took you to another dimension. That is awesome. I hope DW knows this story?

### NeilBB — 2014-07-10T22:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-mamagrok-still-visit-these-forums.9537/post-133862>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=133437)
>
> Future information, which is impossible to know now, in principle, maybe that's already relevant to the present moment. Sounds nuts.........until you realize that we know the future of life in seasons........because they always repeat. It also becomes clear life organizes around these future events.

Everything in cosmology and chronobiology basically tells us that "time" is a periodic or oscillatory phenomenon. Whether it be the motions of celestial bodies. Change of seasons. Circadian rhythm. Molecular genetic oscillations of proteins. It may seem linear, but it's always actually cyclic.

The survival value of time-recognition from an evolutionary standpoint is clearly that it is predictive, ie, helps an organism to "anticipate the future" and act accordingly.

Time is likely fractal just as space is. Cycles upon cycles. Superposition of multiple sine waves creating a complex mathematical function.

Note that if you focus on a small enough portion of a sine wave, it will appear to be linear. But the linearity is an illusion when you widen the frame of reference.

All this implies that any degree of "precognition" would be a highly desirable trait from an evolutionary or chronobiological perspective. It would essentially involve a more sophisticated perceptual ability to decode smaller "sine waves" to predict different aspects of the future...

Hence, more DHA, more electrons means more neural computing power to essentially perform a mental "Fourier analysis" on different aspects of the "arrow of time" and essentially "see into the future."

That wouldn't have been the case if I were some structural engineer or medical professional, but you don't need a lab to do math (which is what Computer Science essentially is).

In my experience, the biggest problem isn't how fast you can learn something, but whether or not you're learning the correct things. While jackkruse.com doesn't tell you what exactly to study, it provides a straight-edge for telling you what NOT to study. The process of elimination will eventually lead one to the truth.

For me, I'm just going to dive into Quantum Mechanics and hope for the best

# Thread: Vortex by Vitamix?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2014-02-01T20:57:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-107759>

OK this may be the silliest question I've ever asked so I apologize in advance.

when I puree veggies for soup in my Vitamix, after a while I feel like I'm seeing a vortex. Dr K mentioned we could put water in those wine chillers that move water about to help structure it....and now I wonder....can't I just put some in a vitamix, turn it on for a while and get the same effect? Or is it null bc of the EMF coming from the needed energy?

### fitness@home — 2014-02-01T21:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-107761>

Interesting question...there's a Vitamix sitting on my kitchen counter.

### SeaHorse — 2014-02-01T21:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-107767>

I use a little latte frother to make a vortex in my morning concoction....same with bone broths...JK has mentioned lots of stirring is beneficial.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-02T23:54:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-108005>

I see several articles on putting wine into a blender (shock!!) with good results. I was thinking about doing the same for some of my water, before putting it on magnets.

### Hope — 2014-02-03T00:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-108020>

Thanks, ladies.......I will have to give it a try then!

### Danco3636 — 2014-02-03T08:05:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-108062>

I got a Nikken PiMag Optimizer II off eBay for 1/2 price. It magnetizes, runs a vortex and oxygenates my RO or well water. Got it about a month ago and now run most of our water we drink through this. I also have a vita-mix and thought of this.

### nonchalant — 2014-07-30T03:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vortex-by-vitamix.9539/post-136008>

I bought a new funnel a couple days ago. It has a long tube at the bottom, and the first time I used it, it got stopped up. I found that if I poured the liquid in off-center, however, a nice vortex formed and it never stopped up. Made me think of Jack's comments about breaking symmetry.

# Thread: ct and skin changes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-skin-changes.9540/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-02-01T21:01:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-skin-changes.9540/post-107762>

Hi,

this is my second round of attempting CT. i tried last summer but abborted on the way since that time I was still not sure about my condition and the way out of it. i strated again few weeks back and this time I go for it. The thing I wanted to ask is what I started to see about my skin. I never suffered by acne and after many months on keto epipaleo my skin looks just amazing, but I started to have acne on my upper back and now to skipped to neck and the skin on my face gets reddish and rashy time to time (must be connected with ct). That is absolutely new. Otherwise I see myself really getting better. Any experience with this and any recommendation what to do? I assume it is high estrogen sign, but not sure this would be something expecting. thanks!

### Hope — 2014-02-01T21:04:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-skin-changes.9540/post-107763>

Congrats on doing this!!! Wow!

I wonder if it could be a detox reaction? The skin is our biggest elimination organ and perhaps as you are dumping garbage from your cells, your liver gets overwhelmed and the skin is drafted to help in the elimination? If so I am certain it will resolve. Perhaps it's even estrogen being dumped?

have you changed anything in your diet?

### kovita — 2014-02-01T21:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-skin-changes.9540/post-107764>

thanks for your reply, Hope. I eat very strict diet which is epipaleo with autoimmune reductions. Is does not sound very fun but I do well and even enjoy it. I started to experiment with nuts and chocolate and raw egg yolks (i have no igG response aginst them). This is not the first attempt to eat those and have to say that CT had to make something because by the means of my measure of intolerance (increased joint pains and muscular pains, headache and veryverysenior feelings) I seem to improved. my body temerature went to normal from chronic elevation. I really look great and everyone is asking what the hell I do, i even mangaed to put on some weight where a woman would expect and I started to build again muscles. So the sudden skin erruptions just do not match. I notices JK was somewhere talking about this possibility mentioning some estrogen lowering foods and supps. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-produce-water-hydration-by-exercise.9543/post-107779>

Water is considered a by-product of energy production. Do Mitochondria actually CREATE water? And if so, than can we actually stay hydrated without drinking water (with low intensity movement/energy expenditure where we don't overheat and sweat)?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T00:48:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-produce-water-hydration-by-exercise.9543/post-107800>

Tony you my friend are jumping way ahead.......but I like it. When we oxidize food, the great majority of stored energy goes to the production of HEAT. What is heat? It is part of the infrared spectrum. After EE12 that should open your eyes a bit. What does it mean? What are the down stream effects for ........say your hormone panel? What governs this entire process? Water, light, magnetism, do.........and the translation is your hormone panel. Active tissues, like the heart and brain, contain more mitochondria. Infra red electromagnetic energy is what made us eutherian mammals and birds birds. What common event ties these two animals together. Where is the biologic tie to those two? Mitochondrial function is the short answer. We have hot bodies, for the great majority of life until we get sick or die.........connecting any dots yet?

Science has not been able to reconstruct the timeline for the recovery of the planet climate after KT, but let us focus on what we absolutely do know. We know the animals that made it thru and we know photosynthesis was shut down from 6months to estimates from NASA as far as ten yrs. Let me ask you......how does food grow without sun? It does not. What would life have to do? Live on water and radiant heat? Sounds a bit like my Quantum electron blog post from 3 yrs ago huh?
When the sun is missing in action photosynthesis was gone but it had a massive cooling effect on the surface. The climate cooling persisted for at least 10 to 1000 years depending upon whose article you believe. But we do know it was COLD. So if you're an animals who had to navigate this all.......how would your mitochondria have to be epigenetically directed?

Turns out that is what humans and birds still have today. What else might you expect? The principal metabolic reactions for energy generation take place in the mammal and bird mitochondria. Here is an organelle where food energy is released in electrons and oxygen is consumed. The electrons from food go to reduce oxygen to molecular oxygen. What are the end points? Water and carbon dioxide are the out put of this action............

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T00:52:49+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T00:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-produce-water-hydration-by-exercise.9543/post-107808>

Remember that Photosynthesis and the Calvin cycle are pathways used by plants where carbohydrates ( specifically glucose) are formed from air (CO2), and the electromagnetic power of the sun. It turns our in mammalian and bird mitochondria, the exact opposite biochemical reaction occurs!!! Energy from the exothermic reaction of water forming from air (O2) and the enzymatic burning of fuel (oxidizable food, glucogen, glucose, fat) result in HEAT (infra red electromagnetic energy) and ATP. Did anyone just notice the law of conservation happen before your eyes? Did not EE12 say this in the video? The sun's electromagnetic spectrum was given off to the cell in one small part called the infra red spectrum or heat. This heat was used to create a massive redox potential on the inner mitochondrial membrane.........What does this mean to the non geeks? It means your mitochondria become very very efficient even with small numbers? That means you can survive a real long time with water and heat alone...........WITHOUT A TON of food...........anyone connecting dots to anything I have been saying about today.......and now Factor X?

When one mole of H2O is created from one H2 (hydrogen) and half O2 (oxygen), 286 kJ of power are released to the cell to do work. That is an astounding amount of energy. This is just from water. For reference, a small tuber has only 68 kcal with in it. Still think food is more important than water when you are a mammal and have a mitochondria crafted by a unique set of environmental circumstances?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T01:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-produce-water-hydration-by-exercise.9543/post-107815>

So let us examine "fuel efficiency of mammalian mitochondrial function (THINK MITOCHONDRIAL RX folks)

With cellular respiration, instead of an enormous, exothermic explosion (like a hydrogen bomb) of subatomic particles called electrons and protons are added step-wise on a gradient known as the electron transport chain (ECT) in plants and animals. What drives this gradient to oxygen. That my friend is the story buried in EE12. It is the charge separation of water from the resonante infra red energy that came initially from sunlight. In other words......the entire spectrum of sunlight was absorbed and we are designed to spit out IR parts to water surrounding the mitochondria in a reverse micelle. This IR stream is unending provided the sun is warm. Water is the electronic adapter that allows the sun to communicate with our mitochondrial battery. Water provides the positive flow of a large proton gradient. Mitochondria created with the negative part of the battery called the 'anode' on the inner mitochondrial membrane. What dictates the actions of all charged particles on this planet and the universe? The answer was in EE1. THE ELECTROMAGNETIC FORCE!
Infra red heat energy is liberated from sunlight by the mitochondria and water is the middle man. Massive energy is released when oxygen is the final proton acceptor in the chain. That is coupled to the enzyme called F1F0-ATPase and it forms ATP. ATP allows proteins to fully unfold to bind more what? WATER!!! This creates a massive battery that requires no EXTERNAL energy. Does not this sound like a Maxwell demon or a zero entropy state to anyone with a science background.

In one day, it is estimated that our mitochondria may produce our own weight in ATP!!! That is how damn important water is........without water you don't get ATP.

Now lets talk energy efficiency or GREEN energy transfers: The experimental transfer of energy from oxidizable fuel to ATP and heat is pretty darn good: 39% ATP and 61% heat. See the cite below for that data. The power of the sun is endless.......water is constantly present. Mitochondria were crafted by the environment early mammals found them selves in to be energy rockstars because FOOD WAS SCARCE............FOOD AINT THE BIG DEAL.

Plants chloroplasts get 3-6% efficiency from transfer of solar energy to the energy bonds of plant starches and fatty acids. Particular C4 plants can get 7-8% (sugarcane) and one super cyanobacteria strain Chlorobaculum tepidum achieves 10%. To put this in a frame of reference you can understand and compare these numbers too consider that modern fuel efficiencies for coal are 20-30% and solar photovoltaics are around 25%.

> I think that when the sun was really strong and I had been laying it it for a while (I felt very comfortable, and didn't want to seek shade, but it felt like the sun was going right through me) the infra red energy might have been creating water with my mitochondria. And though I was quite tan, my shins were lighter in color, so more photons could get in at that area. I think if my collagen was in better condition, and I were better hydrated, I could move all that energy away to other places, and the water wouldn't have built up at the site. If anyone could add to that explanation, I'd love to hear more. It's nice to think that all my mitochondria might not be trashed.

Hmm, finally got a chance to listen to Jack's intro to EE12. How about this:

Droving sheep requires lots of time in the sun these days and I can only cope if I am totally wet..saturate all my clothing and hat, and if need be I have a wet towel over my legs on the bike....Looks pretty strange but if windy or in a breeze I can actually have a shiver in 37-40degrees while I'm zipping around. Have watering troughs about 1km apart to top up saturation regularly.....I may not be able to explain the physics but I sure can feel the difference in my body between hot and cold and wet. It's kinda like trying to somehow manage a bit of CT on a bike! I can be quite cold, especially late afternoon and ...it is high summer!

# Thread: Lots Of Wrong Turns  On My Road Here
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lots-of-wrong-turns-on-my-road-here.9544/>

### dheat — 2014-02-02T00:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lots-of-wrong-turns-on-my-road-here.9544/post-107797>

My decline in health started at least 4 years ago when i was 46 years old and that's when i started telling my doc that i go to every year for check ups that i am really tired all the time. So he did the blood work thing and it comes back and he said everything is range (in range in up being the key word i heard for 31/2 years) and the same thing happen with the following year also but i was even more tire then. So in 2012 i was really fatigue and it was effecting work now so I saw 3 more doctors 2 endocrine docs and 1 heart doctors and i heard the same words everything thing is in range. Last August i went and saw a another hormone doc and he did the blood work and he come back in with the results and said you have some low hormones they were all with in the so called range but they were all on the very low side. DHEA was low 255 free t level was 31 total t was 253 vitamin d was 32 just to name a few and he said your numbers need to be in the upper part of the range. Where have i heard that before?? . So the doc puts me on testosterone cream in Sept last year for 3 months and other vitamins. And September was when i started following jacks leptin reset diet and still on it today and i do ct also. So December last year i am still feeling fatigue so go back to check t levels and the blood test for t was even lower than before free t was 25 and total t was 250. So the doc gives me a shot of testosterone cypionate and in about 3 days i felt brand new what a turn around. After 4 years of fatigue and 2 of them life changing fatigue i found part of my problem. I don't like the thought of being on t shots the rest of my life but if that's what i have to do i will do. And i have hope that with ketogenic diet and doing ct again and watching my EMF will help my hormones where i won' need t shots in the future. That's my journey in a nut shell to this point and i am ready to go forward with the next one.

# Thread: It all makes sense.... patiently getting there.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-all-makes-sense-patiently-getting-there.9545/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### heatherh — 2014-02-02T01:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-all-makes-sense-patiently-getting-there.9545/post-107814>

Hi guys! I'm Heather, 30 years old, married, mother to 4 kiddos recently living in SoCal. So here it goes.....

I was raised pretty decent. I was always a good weight, played sports, ate meat, veggies, and fruit. Not much bread compared to most of my fellow southern friends. (NC) I broke a few bones growing up, had kidney stones twice, and migraines. Other than that, rarely sick and "healthy". I did get pregnant with my first child at 16, maybe that has something to do with where I am now. Also, at 22, I donated eggs 3x. I think that plays a part as things went downhill after that cycle-wise.

Had my other children at 18, 20, and 24. Still not overweight at that time. 5'4" and about 135. Once I had my last daughter, the shit hit the fan. I had a c-section and bleed for 9+ months. Finally ruled as having PCOS. Put on Met and told only BC would help as there is no cure. (Didn't do it cause I had my tubes tied) That was almost 6 years ago. My weight steadily increased, GERD got worse, and no diet changes, or exercising helped. I was hopeless. All I heard was "eat more salad" "work out more" "calories in, calories out".

In the fall of 2011, I googled PCOS one day and landed at Marks Daily Apple. Found Dr. Kruse, Gretchen, and the Leptin Reset thread. Started immediately. Weight fell off the first 2 months and I saw great results all over. Then, when I thought I was reset, I backed off. Skipped breakfast here, had a couple drinks there, went off Met, etc. Obviously, I wasn't ready. I have been 90% primal/paleo ever since. I teamed that up with a heavy lifting/sprinting schedule and NOTHING. Weight didn't budge.

Now I sit about 30 lbs overweight and ready to get a hold of this once and for all. I'm doing the LR again (fat, seafood, protein) and have started CTing. I am in the 90 day program. I keep my laptop and phone away from me, turn off my wifi when not using, and have limited microwave use. All of what Jack writes makes so much sense. That is why every single person has something (bad) going on. That is why overweight people don't usually lose weight. DUH! I knew there was a reason I craved meat and fat.

\*I take Vit D, Metformin, Fish Oil, Kelp, Magnesium, B12, Cinnamon, Omeperazole, L-glutamine
(If you have any input on these please let me know)

Anyway.... hi all! I'll be seeing you around!

### sooperb — 2014-02-26T19:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-all-makes-sense-patiently-getting-there.9545/post-112314>

Heather, first and foremost, heal yourself first. He is not your responsibility. I know you probably love him but sometimes all you can do is lead by example. If he refuses or doesn't want to follow, you risk losing your way and maybe failing, that's not right for you at any stage of the game. I'm married to someone who isn't ever going to commit to this path 100% but I'm not going to let that stop me doing what I feel is right for me. If he won't eat optimally, ask him to get his own food or just give him what he wants, you don't have to eat it, if he won't deal with EMF in the bedroom (as mine won't), do what you can to optimise it for you. Think about earthing, maybe if you can run to it, an Earthpulse, Arcole has started a journal about it in the journal section. You can maybe be direct, nicely lol, by telling him that you want to be around as long as possible for the children, in as fit a condition as you can, if he chooses not to, well, that's his decision. It needn't be a battle as long as he can accept that if wants to choose his path, it can't be at the expense of yours.

My hubby is happy to eat the food but sometimes baulks at all the inspection I do before I'll buy meat, he's never going to dunk even his nose in cold water and CT, well, he just rolls his eyes, I do mine when he's not around or busy. The TV in the bedroom might meet with a little accident lol although to be fair it's only on in the morning and never when we go to bed. Go for it girl, you're important and this is a big deal.

# Thread: Too big for my tub
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T02:42:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107837>

So I tried to get in the tub this evening and I couldn't fit. I'd like to think the tub runs small like most of my pants, haha. Actually it is small in that it's not very high. It's not even a foot deep. Anyway, will doing spot CT have the same benefits even though I can't do total submersion? I can definitely do cold showers and bags of ice for spot CT until I shrink enough to fit in the tub.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T02:49:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107841>

Annie - take bags of ice in the tub and put on your torso and neck - that is what I do. Maybe consider an ice vest?

Are you taking part in the free 90 optimal reset? there is a FB group....

### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T03:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107853>

So no water, just ice? Will the CT benefit my fat legs if the ice is only on my torso? I'm ok with cool showers and face dunks but I do need a thermometer to make sure I'm getting it low enough. How about just standing outside for a little while? I'd thought about leveraging our current weather right now in lieu of buying ice. Maybe that could help shrink things up a bit too.

I sure am taking part! That's why I attempted to shove my big ass in the tub. Should've brought a can of cooking spray into the bathroom. Lol!

### caroline — 2014-02-02T04:44:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107857>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=107853)
>
> So no water, just ice? Will the CT benefit my fat legs if the ice is only on my torso? I'm ok with cool showers and face dunks but I do need a thermometer to make sure I'm getting it low enough. How about just standing outside for a little while? I'd thought about leveraging our current weather right now in lieu of buying ice. Maybe that could help shrink things up a bit too.
>
> I sure am taking part! That's why I attempted to shove my big ass in the tub. Should've brought a can of cooking spray into the bathroom. Lol!

You are too cute!! LOL

No, no, no ...you need water! I freeze big bags of ice and then cover myself with them!

Where are you? do you have snow? what is the temp of your tap water? You don't need ice if your water is cold enough.

do it all! get outside in a bikini, Don't worry about a thermometer until you have a chance to get one. I got mine on ebay for under $5.00
You just have to go for it girl!

### Lahelada — 2014-02-02T10:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107871>

What Caroline says is correct. However Dr Kruse does soemthing else and that is expose parts of his body to heat and the other half to cold using Fournier's law.
That may also be an option for you if you can use your tub safely. If not ice packs and cold showers are just fine. Be as cold as you dare as much time as you can . If you are in winter use the free cold as Caroline said.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T14:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107888>

I'm in VA and we had some snow but since we've had some warmer days it's melting. It's going to get cold again soon though. Tap water is cold but I'm not sure how cold.

So I can do spot CT with ice and hang around outside and still receive the benefit without the tub for right now? I could try to get in there again but it is extremely uncomfortable being 6' tall and weighing 350. I might be able to get an inch of water in. A bikini? Holy balls Caroline you've lost it!

### nicld — 2014-02-02T18:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107928>

Some members here have bought a stock tank and set that outside for CT. Is that an option where you live? Come spring that is what I am going to do when out tank actually unthaws from the ground.

Nicole

### caroline — 2014-02-02T22:34:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107976>

Annie - do what you can do .... just start and keep working it! Maybe consider an icevest?

I have been known to lose it the odd time ...

### AnnieW — 2014-02-03T03:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-108038>

# Thread: G'day from down under.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-02-02T12:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107882>

Hi all...I just want to introduce myself and give a quick rundown on where I'm at health wise and what I've been doing about it. I am a 30 year old male living in Sydney, Australia. I have been paleo for nearly two years. I have been fairly consistent with my diet during that two year period, however, I have had bouts of massive sugar cravings that last anywhere between several days to several weeks. I haven't had any cravings now for quite some time. I haven't been eating any seafood (until last week) as I had been scared off by much that has been said about radiation in seafood. I read a comment by Jack on facebook that said that much of the talk to do with radiation in seafood is overblown and that's part of the reason why I have decided to start eating oysters (2 a day) and salmon (twice a week).
Up until about a month ago, most of my health concerns were quite minor....slight brain fog from time to time, trouble sleeping on occasion, and the odd day of feeling irritable for no apparent reason, but all in all not too bad.....this changed at the start of this year when I noticed my energy dipping considerably about halfway into my work shifts. This was also combined with a feeling of irritability/agitation. This has progressed to the point where I have huge energy crashes in which I basically just need to sit or lay down and do nothing. I cant even be bothered talking when it hits me. Some days are worst than others. I have never experienced anything like this before, and as far as I can tell, nothing has really changed in my diet as I eat the same things most weeks including beef, beef liver and kidneys, plenty of varieties of vegeteables, eggs, butter, coconut oil....and everything is fresh, organic, free range etc etc. I take very few supplements...I take a synbiotic and a wholefoods multi and I also take one to two diatomaceous earth baths each week for detox.
I have no idea what to do, which is what led me to this site for help. If anyone has any tips as far as where to start reading, then feel free to share. I am looking forward to chatting with some like-minded folks.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T13:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107885>

Hi Ben and welcome! first of all - please start a journal. Dr. K. loves journals ... he needs context if he is able to offer suggestions. It helps you with commitment and we can get to know you!

Go to the top of the page and click on forum and then go down 6 categories and you will see the journal section.

Have you joined in with the free 90 day Optimal reset? Have you read Dr. K's book? Epi-Paleo RX. It will give you a great overview of all of this - available on Amazon in kindle or paper.

The very most important thing is to be aware of the non native EMF in your life ...home, work, car. Did something change in your life at the start of the year?

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? check the home page. He talks a lot about EMF in most of them. Food is only 10% and it seems like you are doing well there.

Have you had labs done? There is a great section in his book about labs.

Please keep reading and asking question....

Are you doing the leptin reset? The BAB will knock out sugar cravings.

I don't know what those baths are? when did you start them? I wonder if you are experiencing detox from them?
If you aren't methylating properly you will be reabsorbing toxins back into your tissues and that could make you very ill.

There is also a free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson on healing your hormones - in the blog index on the home page.

Sorry - this is a lot to throw at you! I will stop for now and let you catch your breath! There are a lot of great people here working on our own optimal journey and everyone lends a hand .... ask away ...

### caroline — 2014-02-02T13:19:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107886>

BTW - I am in Wollongong ... we are practically neighbours!

There is a huge thread in the cave ....the aussie donut hole...

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-02T16:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107896>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=107882)
>
> This has progressed to the point where I have huge energy crashes in which I basically just need to sit or lay down and do nothing. I cant even be bothered talking when it hits me. Some days are worst than others.

I should think it certainly could be hypothyroidism -- but doubtless a lot of other things, too. There are a few other signs that tend to go along with hypothyroidism, such as a tendency to lose the outer third of the eyebrows.

I should go to your doctor and tell him or her that you're experiencing extreme fatigue. They'll then take blood samples and run a battery of tests to see what they can find.

### Ben1983 — 2014-02-03T16:52:31+0200

Hi Caroline, I have signed up to the 90 Day Optimal Reset. I have Jack's book, but so far I have only skimmed through certain parts of it. I am taking 10 days off work (annual leave) starting this Friday, so I'll have a good chance then to read it properly, and to read a lot in the forum, too. I'm primarily taking the time off work just to see if it helps my body to recover. I'm just going to take walks on the beach, have massages and read each day.
I'm not currently doing the leptin reset (as far as I am aware of). I am basically just eating paleo with the addition of more seafood and bone broths into my diet as of last week. What does "BAB" stand for?
Actually, now that you mention it, I started those baths only a short time before I started to feel strange. How do I ensure that I am methylating properly so that the toxins are released from my body and not just reabsorbed?
I love the gong! I lived in Figtree for 5 years when I was studying at TAFE and UOW. I still get down there about once a month. What part of the gong are you in?

Also, I may do this as a separate forum question, but I wanted to find out if any Aussies have sourced out which various places we can get all of the recommended lab tests done? I would really like to see where I'm at in regards to what the labs are showing instead of just being able to say "I have huge crashes in energy".

### caroline — 2014-02-04T01:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-108204>

Hi Ben .... I live in the North Gong - close to the beach! This is probably the nicest place I have ever lived - I love it here!

I would take a semi educated guess and say to stop those baths ASAP.

Please read Dr. Jacksons blog and listen to his webinar. Read Dr. K's hormones 101 and 102 blogs.

BAB = Big ass Breakfast

this should be your routine...

get up and go outside and look at the sun
have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising \[50gr of protein\]
leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed

no artificial lites after sunset

R/O water

do you have blue blocker glasses?

I can help you with labs when you are ready - read Jack's book first!

what else do you want to know?

don't forget to get outside as much as possible with as little clothes as possible \[nude is best!\] barefoot - go to the ocean!
hug a tree or two.

Google movnat ...and Erwan Lecorre.....

# Thread: How to spot a horribly done study with bias.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-spot-a-horribly-done-study-with-bias.9550/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T16:42:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-spot-a-horribly-done-study-with-bias.9550/post-107898>

[http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/93/4/748.full#](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/93/4/748.full#) Here is the study. Just read the abstract first and spot the bias.

Realize they are telling the readers ketosis is bad before they ever get to their study. Then realize there was no control for water content in the cells or for temperature even thought they mention UCP3 and proton uncoupling. Remember I told you all in nature ketosis is coupled with large water intake and colder temps.

The riot to me......is while they tell us ketosis is bad and "impairs cardiac high-energy phosphate metabolism" they never make the connection to water and ATP.
And then they report that despite this finding cardiac muscle function was UNCHANGED? But look at the title of the paper. How is something physiologically impaired yet there is no change? The reason they say it is because of the timing of their measurements. They reported 9% lower cardiac PCr/ATP (P< 0.01)" and then said, there was "no change in cardiac function"

The other big issue: we know from Volek Phinney and Veech's work on ketosis that it takes 7-10 days to see mitochondrial adaptation to the higher FFA and ketone fuel. Take a look at when these researchers did their work in this study. They timed it precisely so they would get the result before this adaptation happens. This is how a researchers games the experiment. They know money from the NIH only comes supporting the USDA pyramid so they need to support the HC LF diet. They can not just let the science play out............and even gaming the experiment as they did here.........we still see cardiac function was unchanged. This has huge implications for those with bad heart and brains where mitochondrial density is greatest. This is why CO help those with bad brains. The same is true if you have a bad heart.........They do not even realize this paper is great news........for patients with heart disease because it completely shows you why the diet heart hypothesis is complete crap........it also puts another dagger in the fat being the enemy of heart health...........So here we have a paper that is trying to game the system.....get published to gain the researchers an academic credit so they can go back to apply for more money to fortify modern nutrition dogma. This is how people get hurt........keep the truth from docs who do not discern the materials and methods..........puts patients at risk.

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-02T17:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-spot-a-horribly-done-study-with-bias.9550/post-107901>

I see Peter has come to the same conclusion as you -- deliberate bias:

> For a lesson in how to stack a real study against low carb this Oxford group beats any television entertainment hands down. ...

[http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.co.uk/2014/01/tv-pantomine-or-oxford-study.html](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.co.uk/2014/01/tv-pantomine-or-oxford-study.html)

But what are Oxford University researchers doing deliberately skewing results (they must know what they're doing)? They don't have to worry about the NIH and the USDA. In the affiliations it says:

> Supported by The British Heart Foundation and the Medical Research Council.

[http://www.bhf.org.uk/#&panel1-1](http://www.bhf.org.uk/#&panel1-1)

[http://www.mrc.ac.uk/index.htm](http://www.mrc.ac.uk/index.htm)

I suppose those organisations are probably deeply sold on the "conventional wisdom" but there's no direct link to agricultural interests there.

Whatever is going on, even if it's no more than a prideful refusal to rethink old positions, it's still deeply sinister. As Peter says:

> ... the study … \[has\] the potential to injure people who need ketogenic eating ...

You might as well send someone unarmed into a Tiger-infested jungle, telling them it's quite safe, as put out skewed propaganda in the health field. I know more than one diabetic, for example -- huge guts, lasered eyes every few months, amputations, gradually failing kidneys. These people do stupid stuff like eating cake and swigging alcohol, but then their doctors don't give them any clear message to _just cut carbs dead_, under 50 or 20 or whatever grams per day. In fact, they're actually _told_ to eat plenty of bread, rice, and pasta.

It angers me. This silliness is maiming and killing people in the real world. It's not a game, as these researchers perhaps think.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T17:35:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-spot-a-horribly-done-study-with-bias.9550/post-107909>

Correct..........it just sickens me.

A great study might be to track the health conditions of these so called experts. Would that ever happen? Not likely.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-spot-a-horribly-done-study-with-bias.9550/post-107955>

# Thread: Erik J. Ware's Journal, Beginning 2-2-14
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### erikjware — 2014-02-02T18:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107925>

I really began a ramp up in health & fitness last June when I began training for the Hartford Half Marathon and discovered the Bulletproof Podcast. I finished my 13.1 and then had some blood work done late in the year for surgery. On December 6, I had old hardware, (2 plates and 13 screws; they left one screw in because the bone grew completely around it) removed from my left forearm from a bad break 4 and a half years ago. I had been having pain and soreness and it was discovered one of the screws was backing out of the bone over time. The diet guidelines and experience from rehabbing the arm the first time has me almost all the way back in only 2 months. Of course the bone will take longer to fully regenerate but range on motion in the hand and muscle healing is probably 90-95% back.

The major points of interest on the blood work was a 1:1 ratio of Tri/HDL. Fasting glucose: 89. B12: 442. Total testosterone: 610. Total vitamin D: 34. Nothing to be too concerned over, correct?

I also had a 23andme profile done and I came out as elevated risks for: Atrial Fibrillation (6.7% above avg.), Prostate Cancer (3.7% above avg.) \[Father has had some prostate issues, but not cancer\], Alzheimer's Disease, (5.4% above avg.) \[Aunt on my Mother's side now has our family's first case of Alzheimer's\], Colorectal Cancel (1.2% above avg.), and Chronic Kidney Disease (.8% above avg.).

Honestly though, none of these issues scare me as my Father believes the current cholesterol myth and follows the recommended low fat, high carb diet, and has for a decade, and my Aunt with Alzheimer's eats a SAD diet and has her entire life.

I'm 6'5" and weigh 197. I'm about to start some 2014 fitness goals with my wife and family which will include 1, 3, and 6 month goals. I am going to start religiously tracking what I eat, how I'm sleeping, and all CT I am practicing. I really want to get into and stay into ketosis long-term so I can have direct experience of what is possible on that diet.

And just for fun, my favorite breakfast is 1 avocado (dusted with dulse flakes and chipotle powder), 4 eggs over easy, and 5 pieces of bacon, with a BP coffee! Thanks in advance and I'll be posting more soon.

### sooperb — 2014-02-02T18:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107933>

Welcome Erik, I love the sound of your breakfast.

I'm in 2 minds about the 23andme, how accurate are the results and what are they based on? I can see that if you were going to get really stuck in to self analysis it might be useful to have advance warning of what could be coming your way, on the other hand, who wouldn't start fretting if something really unpleasant was forecast? The more I read, the more I wonder how anyone can achieve optimal when there are so many interconnecting bits and finding the missing or mismatched bits can be such minefield of potential booms (to borrow from Jack lol)?

### erikjware — 2014-02-02T19:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107950>

I wanted the 23andme just in case there was something really unusual or alarming showing up. I don't think that happened. Now, I'm more interested in the relatives/genealogy aspect.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T23:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-107988>

Welcome Erik! your breakfast does sound yummy! What is your non native EMF exposure? Are you and your family drinking R/O water?

### erikjware — 2014-02-03T02:04:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108031>

I have begun to address EMF. I cut the breaker box at night. I've cut down on how much I wear socks, especially at home on the hardwood floors. I've started cold adapting in small ways; not turning on the heat in the car (it's winter here in CT!), wearing less clothes, sleeping with less cover, turning down the thermostat, not wearing a beanie and gloves, etc. Tonight I will doing my first face dunking session (after watching the SuperBowl). At this time I have just upped my water intake but my wife and I are looking at a Berkey filter system and will be making some other changes, such as glass bottles, etc. Thanks for the welcome. Do you have any water hints?

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T14:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108087>

Our local water is heavily chlorinated, turn on the tap sometimes and it smells like a swimming pool! We have used a bog standard jug filter for 20+ years. The water isn't fluoridated though, apparently the northwest is one of the few areas in England where it isn't added, maybe because a lot comes from the lakes and may be considered to be purer, till they start messing with it of course. I wonder if you could empty the carbon bits from a jug filter into the shower head, would that work, would it still screw back on and the water still come through?

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Shower-Head...91431385&sr=8-1&keywords=sprite+shower+filter](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Shower-Head-Sprite-Royale--filtration/dp/B004CEV69E/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1391431385&sr=8-1&keywords=sprite+shower+filter) - there seem to be plenty here in the uk!

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-03T15:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108090>

It seems you're right: in some areas of the UK they do add it. Jeez, I didn't think they'd sunk so low in Britain yet. Poisoning the \*\*\*\*\*\* water supply! And, since it's done to you at source (corporate-society enforcement), no opt out. Yes, here's what I found by googling:

> Most water supplies contain some fluoride and around half a million people in the UK receive naturally fluoridated water.
>
> Another **5.5 million people receive water that has had the fluoride concentration raised** to around one part per million (1ppm). ...
>
> The West Midlands has the biggest water fluoridation scheme in the UK, which serves over three-quarters of the local population ...

[http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Fluoride/Pages/Introduction.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Fluoride/Pages/Introduction.aspx)

IOW, most of the UK water companies are leaving the water alone. 5.5 million is less than 10% of the population (now heading for 80 million). Still, no-one should be doing this. I guess someone's got a fat wallet from this "public health" con.

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T15:23:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108092>

Remind me never to move to the West Midlands lol.

### erikjware — 2014-02-05T17:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108481>

A few days in, limiting carbs and increasing veggies is the toughest part, I'm finding. Eating plenty of fat and protein in proportion is okay but I'm only eating one large meal a day; dinner. However, I have 1 or 2 avocado's with breakfast and I'm working on a can of green food powder, so I'm not worried right now. Thinking ahead though, I really like the BP coffee and/or full breakfast in the AM, BP yerba mate around 2/3 PM, then a veggie based meal for dinner, that I've been doing.

So, all-in-all, I feel good about this.

### caroline — 2014-02-06T02:48:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-108628>

re consider your veggie based dinner ... how about some seafood and a small salad - maybe a seafood stew?
a can of sardines for breakfast? Why are you so worried about veggies?

have you read Jack's easy start guides?

### erikjware — 2014-02-22T17:26:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111692>

Worried because I don't get enough. Still working through how to plan and organize in advance to ensure I have the right high quality foods ready when I get hungry.

### erikjware — 2014-02-22T17:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111693>

I also joined the YMCA this month and have been using the steam room almost daily. After only two days of the jacuzzi I had to stop because the chlorine have me a headache that lasted all day. I was only able to get it to go away after I drank over 2 gallons of water, spaced out over the second half of the day. Terrible feeling. Still have not done a full CT session. Getting mentally comfortable with colder temperatures by showering cooler, rolling down the car window when I drive, not turning in the car heat, wearing less clothes outdoors (Winter here in CT), and cold water splashes on the face often.

### sooperb — 2014-02-22T19:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/erik-j-wares-journal-beginning-2-2-14.9554/post-111710>

Don't be in a rush, you can't fix everything at once. You can try but it becomes tricky keeping each bit online so to speak. I dipped my face for ages before I plucked up the courage to step in the bath, I used the shower too but it's not the same.

# Thread: The Woodpecker frequency:  July 4, 1976
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-woodpecker-frequency-july-4-1976.9557/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T20:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-woodpecker-frequency-july-4-1976.9557/post-107956>

The Woodpecker Signal

Then for reasons that have never been disclosed, the Soviets then decided to get serious and the microwave bombardment of the US Embassy became the prototype for the 1977 Soviet Woodpecker EMR signal. Robert Becker, in the book "The Body Electric"\[1980\] was probably the first American to publish the presence of the Soviet, so called psychoactive, "Woodpecker signal" in the USA. According to him the signal appeared on the bicentennial celebration of July 4, 1976.

The term "Woodpecker signal" first noticed and coined by ham radio operators around the world because the signals were pulse-modulated at a rate of several times a second, so it sounded like a buzz or woodpecker. The source was located at a giant transmitter near Kiev in the USSR. Robert Becker was not sure at the time of writing his book what the effects were, but he wrote: "The available evidence, then suggests that the Russian woodpecker is a multipurpose radiation that combines a submarine link with an experimental attack on the American people." Robert Becker mentions that a source had given him details of an American attempt at retaliation called "Operation Pique". This included "bouncing radio signals off the ionosphere to affect the mental functions of people in selected areas, including Eastern European nuclear installations." Robert Becker sums up the situation up by saying, "The whole business sounds too much like an undeclared electromagnetic war." Maybe you can begin to understand why the military destroyed Becker and Marino's funding for their lab now.
[https://climateviewer.com/2018/05/0...tdown-ionospheric-heating-over-usa-1983-1986/](https://climateviewer.com/2018/05/04/russian-woodpecker-chernobyl-meltdown-ionospheric-heating-over-usa-1983-1986/)

The expose called 'Angels Don't Play This HAARP', tells us that there has been a long history of experimentation of electromagnetic signals scaled up and tested as 'non - lethal weapons' by the U.S and the former U.S.S.R. Today this continues with the USA now using underwater EMF technologies and things such as geo-engineering. This also links why we are trying to change our climate and grow seeds artificially via Monsanto. All of this is tied to these experiments. We don't how much of this could be considered an unofficial war. It was clear the Woodpecker frequency was a pulsed EMF designed to effect have an effect on Americans without them knowing. This is very much like smart meter technology today. I think many of the things going with our climate, HAARP, and Monsanto are all tied back to these effects begun in 1976. We still don't know for sure what the Woodpecker signal was aiming to do, but it is agreed by all researchers who understood the science there was a 'psychoactive' component.

# Thread: Calcium RDA figure must be wrong
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-02-02T20:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107960>

Where did they get it from? This figure _has_ to be a totally broken suggestion.

I know that Professor Cordain has said so for some time.

One wonders if they originally thought something like "bones … ah, ha, _calcium_ and almost plucked a figure out of the air. And suggested one _independent of_ recommendations for D or K2 (admittedly, that hadn't be isolated yet, and it seems doctors in the West have still to catch up with the Japanese in realising its therapeutic value. Another ten to fifteen years?)

This seems to be one area where Weston Price was wrong -- a rare case where he was misled by the assumptions of the day, perhaps. Price seems to be basing his statements on what a guy called Sherman had said:

> The normal adult needs to receive from the foods eaten one-half to one gram of calcium or lime per day

[http://journeytoforever.org/farm\_library/price/price15.html](http://journeytoforever.org/farm_library/price/price15.html)

This is referenced to **SHERMAN, H. C. Chemistry of Foods and Nutrition. New York, Macmillan, 1933**.

I have a strong suspicion that once this figure had become imbedded in the textbooks, the USDA would have been happy to run with it, since you then "need" a fourth "food group" -- namely, dairy products -- it being difficult to get up to this levels without such and you thereby cater to the dairy industry. Hasn't it been said that the "four food groups" actually represent the "four food lobbies"?

I'm not dead against dairy -- I'd certainly be happy to eat a piece of goat's or ewe's milk cheese two or three times a week, although I've gone strict Paleo right now, since I have some minor skin issues and wonder whether they're connected to dairy produce. But the "recommended" three or four glasses of milk a day, or whatever it currently is? **I just don't buy it**. In fact I wonder if that much calcium, in the absence of sufficient K2, is actually dangerous.

Price seems to have made measurements of various minerals in the diets of the isolated populations that he visited. On the same page I already linked (Chapter 15: Characteristics of primitive and modernised dietaries) he asserts:

> In other words the foods of the native Eskimos contained 5.4 times as much calcium as the displacing foods of the white man, five times as much phosphorus, 1.5 times as much iron, 7.9 times as much magnesium, 1.8 times as much copper, 49.0 times as much iodine, and at least ten times that number of fat-soluble vitamins. …
>
> The ratio in the Swiss native diets to that in the displacing diet was for calcium, 3.7 fold; for phosphorus, 2.2 fold; for magnesium, 2.5 fold; for iron, 3.1 fold; and for the fat-soluble activators, at least ten fold. …
>
> The native diet of the New Zealand Maori provided an increase in the native foods over the displacing foods of the modernized whites of 6.2 fold for calcium, 6.9 fold for phosphorus, 23.4 fold for magnesium, 58.3 fold for iron; and the fatsoluble activators were increased at least ten fold. ...

The trouble here is that he is not giving absolute figures. These figures are based on so many times what's in the "displacing foods"

It's curious that the Swiss, who seem to have eaten plenty of cheese are stated to be taking in much less than the Eskimos or the Maori.

Further up the page the "typical American dietary" of Price's day is stated to:

> have a calcium intake ranging from 0.3 to 0.5 gram

If the "displacing foods" are supposed to contain 0.3 g of Ca, then the Maori, for example, were supposedly getting 0.3 x 6.2 = 1.86 g per day; the Swiss, 0.3 x 3.7 = 1.11 g.

I'm deep suspicious of these figures. I can't help wondering if Price, or his lab assistants, botched their assays.

I just don't believe that one can get a whopping great calcium intake on a Paleo diet. I crunched the numbers on what I had today just to see.

Breakfast: Manx cure kipper, 2 cups steamed Swiss chard served with a tbs olive oil, 1 avocado, coffee

Lunch: 4 oz tuna steak fried in olive oil, 2 oz crabmeat, steamed leeks and chicory with olive oil, 1 oz walnuts, 20 g 90% chocolate, one 4 fl oz glass Argentinian Malbec, 1 cup coffee

Dinner: 1 tsp cod liver oil, 1 tsp coconut oil, 2 oz cooked bacon, 1 duck egg fried in duck fat, 2 cups savoy cabbage served with 1 tsp ghee, and 1 oz desiccated coconut (as a pudding)

Now FitDay tells me that that lot is 2195 kcal (68% fat, 19% protein, 9% carbs, 4% alcohol).

Here's the thing: I take the RDAs with a grain of salt and don't worry about them, but FWIW this hits or exceeds the RDA for everything (OK, it says 93% for vitamin E and 85% for Potassium, but that's near enough), for everything, i say, except calcium. According to FitDay I only had 30% of the RDA for calcium.

Well, I'm sorry, the figure must be wrong.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-02T23:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107990>

All that RDA stuff is from such old research. I imagine the true numbers for everything now is quite different, and also different if you are cold-adapted and eating animal meat/fat. And I imagine the number of "essential nutrients" is larger now, with all the EMF keeping our systems from optimally synthesizing/recycling amino acids, proteins, vitamins and such.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T23:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107992>

I love the sounds of what you had to eat!!

# Thread: The Moscow Signal Experiment of the US Embassy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moscow-signal-experiment-of-the-us-embassy.9559/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-02T20:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moscow-signal-experiment-of-the-us-embassy.9559/post-107961>

The Moscow Signal

This is an old story but one that many readers will be unfamiliar with, it illustrates that experiments in the electromagnetic 'Dark Arts' has been going on for at least half a century. It is widely reported and accepted that the Soviets started bombarding the American Embassy in Moscow in 1953 with 'microwaves', which continued for thirty years. In 1962 the CIA decided to officially investigate the Moscow "signal", so in 1965 the U.S. government funded Project Pandora, "a top secret multi-million dollar program". Top scientific experts were consulted by the American Government "about the meaning of microwaving" the Moscow Embassy. Dr. Robert Becker, was asked if central nervous system, CNS disturbances occur by microwave radiation. The Pandora Project found that the microwave radiation interferes with decision making capacity, causes chronic stress and low efficiency. The white blood count of Embassy workers was 40% higher than normal. Monkeys exposed to microwave radiation showed a marked decreased performance of simple tasks.

Nothing was publicly revealed to the U.S. public until 1976 when stories started circulating in the Boston Globe. Dr. Becker states in a 1984 TV documentary that the U.S. couldn't say anything about it because safety standards for the U.S. were higher than the microwave signals used by the Soviets on the Moscow Embassy. If the U.S. admitted bioeffects or athermal effects, (non heating), from the radiation, such as a high white blood count, it would throw into doubt the U.S. standard set "rather arbitrarily " in the 1950s. It would also be an embarrassment to the Americans who chose to keep the public in the dark about the microwaving of the Moscow Embassy from 1953 to 1975. So, in 1976 the embassy employees found out that they were being used as guinea pigs when the US decided to install defensive screens to protect personnel, a situation roundly condemned in an official report. Consequently without admitting liability, the state department eventually tried to appease the embassy staff by re-grading Moscow posts as carrying an extra health risk and gave all staff a 20% salary rise â€¦ a number of staff tried to sue the US Governmentâ€¦'

Neil Cherry in his **mammoth 2000** report (review of 188 research papers on EMR for the New Zealand government), included the Moscow Embassy affair in his analysis of the harmful affects of EMR. He wrote that the employees and dependants were studied for possible health effects from the radar exposure (Cherry disputes the "microwave" description for the EMR detected), by a team from the John Hopkins University under the direction of highly respected epidemiologist, Professor Abraham Lilienfeld. Dr Lilienfeld noted that the group was quite small and the follow-up time too short to generally identify significant health effects such as cancer. He thus recommended that continued health status surveillance should be carried out. This was not done. The incidence of sickness and death were compare with the average US rates for similar age groups for both the Moscow Embassy and other Eastern European Embassies. Cherry writes there was great pressure for the group not to identify adverse health effects. Dr Herbert Pollack, the U.S. State Department Contract Officer is recorded to have changed the conclusions of the report, Goldsmith (1995a, 1997). Despite the small numbers, the Lilienfeld data shows a significant increases in:

Cardiac symptoms
Neurological and psychological symptoms
Altered blood cell counts
Increased chromosome aberrations, and
Elevated cancer in children and adults
Sickness increasing in a dose-response manner with years of residence.
These symptoms are associated with chronic exposure to very low intensity pulsed microwaves in the range < 0.04 to 0.2(W/cm2).
Cherry concludes that, 'In a sense too, the fact that the State Department case officer, Dr Herbert Pollack, altered the conclusions, attests to the significance of this study, the results of which would be embarrassing to the U.S. Government, both in terms of compensation and in terms of the validity of the U.S. exposure standard.'

The 1984 BBC documentary 'Opening Pandora's Box' explained how the safety standards for electromagnetic radiation, EMR, were set higher in the 1950s to allow the military to have unlimited use of EMR technology. At the time, American science reports suggesting EMR health effects of brain tumours, heart conditions, leukaemia, cataracts and more, but these were ignored. The military was a major source of funding and so reports were not followed up. Microwave News, a journal on nonionizing radiation, reported that radar men, air traffic controllers and police officers filed complaints. These court cases revolved around the validity of the safety standard. Dr. **Milton Zaret**, (famous Scarsdale eye doctor who linked RADAR TO cataract formation) another Pandora scientist explained that most government committees, who set the safety standards around the world, were set up in the same way as in the U.S. Members of the committee did not want to impede or put restraints on progress by tightening the safety standards for EMR. This is illustrated by government action, as follows. As part of Project Pandora, the U.S. government conducted Operation Big Boy on Navy ships to find any health effects from radar equipment on the ships. When effects were found, the government terminated the project.

Dr. Becker stated that there was tremendous growth of the communications and power industries and a complete lack of information or even consideration of EMR health risks. Industry didn't consider bioeffects of EMR until the mid seventies. There was a "complete lack of consideration" of any biological effects from electromagnetic radiation and the repeated dogma was there are "no possible biological effects." Then there were concerns over complaints and the subsequent study results on computer VDTs and miscarriages and birth defects and powerline exposure and leukaemia in adults and children living near them. Studies reported a high incidences of suicide in people living near power lines. Other studies reported that electrical workers were at risk for leukemia. Meanwhile, the U.S. standard had been virtually unchanged since the 1950s. In comparison the Soviet standard was originally 1000 times lower than the U.S position. The targetting of the Moscow Embassy highlighted the fact that very small dosages of EMR can have long term harmful effects, which are still officially denied even today.

Adam D, Computerising the body: Microsoft wins patent to exploit network potential of skin Fact or fiction - carrying a keyboard on your arm, The Guardian, Tuesday July 6, 2004

[http://www.rense.com/general3/patent.htm](http://www.rense.com/general3/patent.htm)

The skinny on IT: The human body as a computer bus, From The Economist print edition Jul 1st 2004 [http://www.economist.com/science/displayStory.cfm?story\_id=2876950](http://www.economist.com/science/displayStory.cfm?story_id=2876950)

Japan Unveils "Robot Suit" That Enhances Human Power, SpaceDaily.com, Jun 07, 2005 [www.spacedaily.com/news/robot-05zq.html](http://www.spacedaily.com/news/robot-05zq.html)

Sony Invention Beams Sights, Sounds Into Brain, Yahoo News 6th April 2005-05-04 [http://story.news.yahoo.com/news?tmpl=story&cid=581&e=1&u=/nm/20050406/tc\_nm/tech\_sony\_brain\_dc](http://story.news.yahoo.com/news?tmpl=story&cid=581&e=1&u=/nm/20050406/tc_nm/tech_sony_brain_dc)

No. I know someone who used to cook a pork joint rather like that, though. She followed a recipe that said to score the skin like that, to rub it with oilve oil, and to start it at high temperature, then turn it down. It did work inasmuch as it did result in crisp crackling. Unfortunately, the recipe also said to poke garlic cloves in. Bad idea. Perhaps you were supposed to make deeper holes for those, but at any rate I guess they'd be stil be a bit too close to the surface. The garlic used to burn, which makes it taste bitter and smell nasty.

Did you ever read Anthony Bourdain's _Kitchen Confidential,_ Audrey?

[http://www.amazon.com/Kitchen-Confidential-Anthony-Bourdain/dp/1408845040/](http://www.amazon.com/Kitchen-Confidential-Anthony-Bourdain/dp/1408845040/)

(One you'd very much enjoy, I'd think.)

Anyway, Bourdain said as a French-trained chef he could always smell a Chinese restaurant streets away -- because they burn the garlic (since they cook it at high temperature in stir-fries) which is an absolute no-no in French cooking.

Bourdain's pretty Epi-Paleo himself in a way. A big seafood man. He said the best meal he ever had was in Japan -- course after course of really high-quality seafood, much of it raw.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-05T16:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108473>

I think 200F is what Jack means. I'm gently frying some bacon right now. It barely makes any noise, and yes, its a process that takes hours. I occasionally get up and turn it, and when some pieces start shrinking, I remove them and add more fresh bacon. I nibble all morning. Plz don't tell Jack! I got my laser CT thermometer and measured the temps. They range from 165F to 210F in various areas of the cast iron frying pan.

I suppose the correct way would be to save up all the cooked bacon and eat it for lunch, but I can never resist it.
